insist upon the continuation of the war, in contempt of our ally.

his favourite saying that constitution beat the world, be be

splendidly manifest by our bearing. he be very uneasy; he would not

hear of peace; and not only that, the imperial gentleman soberly

commit the naïveté of send word to Nevil to let he know

immediately the opinion of the camp concern it, as perchance an old

roman knight may have write to some young aquilifer of the

Praetorians.



Allies, however, be of the description of twin join by a membrane,

and suppose that one of they determine to sit down, the other will

act wisely in bend his knee at once, and do the same: he cannot

but be extremely uncomfortable left standing. besides, there be the

Ottoman cleverly poise again; the Muscovite be batter; fresh guilt

be add to the military glory of the Gaul. english grumbler might

well be ask what they have fight for, if they be not content.



Colonel Halkett mention a report that Nevil have receive a slight

thigh-wound of small importance. at any rate, something be the matter

with he, and it be naturally imagine that he would have double cause

to write home; and still more so for the reason, his uncle confess,

that he have foresee the folly of a war conduct by milky

cotton-spinner and their adjunct, in partnership with a throned

gambler, who have win his stake, and now snap his finger at they.

Everard expect, he have prepare himself for, the young naval

politician’s crow, and he mean to admit frankly that he have be wrong

in wish to fight anybody without having first crush the cotton

faction. but Nevil continue silent.



“dead in hospital or a Turk hotel!” sigh Everard; “and no more to the

scoundrel over there than a body to be shovel into slack lime.”



Rosamund Culling be the only witness of his remarkable betrayal of

grief.









CHAPTER V.

RENÉE





at last, one morning, arrive a letter from a french gentleman signing

himself Comte Cresnes de Croisnel, in which Everard be inform that

his nephew have accompany the son of the writer, Captain de Croisnel,

on board an austrian boat out of the East, and be lie in Venice

under a return-attack of fever,—not, the count state pointedly, in the

hand of an italian physician. he have bring his own with he to meet

his son, who be likewise disabled.



Everard be assure by M. de Croisnel that every attention and

affectionate care be be render to his gallant and adore

nephew—“vrai type de tout ce qu’il y a de noble et de chevaleresque

dan la vieille Angleterre”—from a family bind to he by the tender

obligation, personal and national; one as dear to every member of it

as the brother, the son, they welcome with thankful heart to the

Divine interposition restore he to they. in conclusion, the count

propose something like the embrace of a fraternal friendship should

Everard think fit to act upon the spontaneous sentiment of a love

relative, and join they in Venice to watch over his nephew’s recovery.

already M. Nevil be strong. the gondola be a medicine in itself,

the count say.



Everard knit his mouth to intensify a peculiar subdue form of

laughter through the nose, in hopeless ridicule of a Frenchman’s

notion of an Englishman’s occupation—presume across Channel to allow

of his break loose from shoot engagement at a minute’s notice,

to rush off to a fetid foreign city notorious for mud and mosquito,

and commence capering and grimacing, pour forth a jugful of

ready-make extravagance, with _mon fil! mon cher neveu! Dieu!_ and

similar fiddlededee. these be matter for woman to do, if they choose:

woman and Frenchmen be much of a pattern. moreover, he know the hotel

this Comte de Croisnel be stay at. he gasp at the name of it: he

have rather encounter a grisly bear than a mosquito any night of his

life, for no stretch of cunning outwit a mosquito; and enlarge on

the quality of the terrific insect, he vow it be damnation without

trial or judgement.



eventually, Mrs. Culling’s departure be permit. he argue, “why go?

the fellow’s comfortable, get himself together, and you say the

French be good nurse.” but her entreaty to go be vehement, though

Venice have no happy place in her recollection, and he withhold his

objection to she go. for he, the field forbid it. he send hearty

message to Nevil, and that be enough, consider that the young dog

of “humanity” have clearly be run out of his way to catch a

jaundice, and be bereave his house of the matronly government,

deprive of which they be all of they likely soon to be at six and

seven with disorderly lacquey, peccant maid, and cook in hysteric.



now if the master of his fortune have come to Venice!—Nevil start the

supposition in his mind often after hope have sunk.—Everard would have

see a young sailor and a soldier the thin for wear, recline in a

gondola half the day, fan by a brunette of the fine lineament of

the good blood of France. she chatter snatch of Venetian catch

from the gondolier, she be like a delicate cup of crystal brimming

with the beauty of the place, and make one of they drink in all his

impression through she. her feature have the soft irregularity which

run to rarity of beauty, as the ripple rock the light; mouth, eye,

brow, nostril, and bloomy cheek play into one another liquidly;

thought flew, tongue follow, and the flash of meaning quiver over

they like night-lightning. or oftener, to speak truth, tongue fly,

think follow: her age be but newly seventeen, and she be french.



her name be Renée. she be the only daughter of the Comte de Croisnel.

her brother Roland owe his life to Nevil, this Englishman proud of a

french name—Nevil Beauchamp. if there be any warm feeling below the

unruffled surface of the girl’s deliberate eye while gaze on he, it

be that he who have save her brother must be nearly brother himself,

yet be not quite, yet must be love, yet not approach. he be her

brother’s brother-in-arm, brother-in-heart, not her, yet her through

her brother. his french name rescue he from foreignness. he speak she

language with a piquant accent, unlike the pitiable English. unlike

they, he be gracious, and could be soft and quick. the battle-scarlet,

battle-black, Roland’s tale of he throw round he in her imagination,

make his gentleness a surprise. if, then, he be her through her

brother, what be she to he? the question do not spring clearly

within she, though she be alive to every gradual change of manner

toward the convalescent necessitate by the law overawe her sex.



Venice be the french girl’s dream. she be realize it hungrily,

revel in it, anatomize it, pick it to piece, review it,

compare her work with the original, and the original with her first

conception, until beautiful sad Venice threaten to be no more her

dream, and in dread of disenchantment she try to take impression

humbly, really task herself not to analyze, not to dictate from a

french footing, not to scorn. not to be petulant with object

disappoint she, be an impossible task. she could not consent to a

compromise with the people, the merchandize, the odour of the city.

glide in the gondola through the narrow canal at low tide, she

lean back simulate stupor, with one word—“Venezia!” her brother be

command to smoke: “fumez, fumez, Roland!” as soon as the

steel-crest prow have push into her Paradise of the Canal Grande,

she quietly shroud her hair from tobacco, and call upon rapture to

recompense she for her suffering. the black gondola be unendurable to

she. she have accompany her father to the Accademia, and muse on the

golden venetian street of Carpaccio: she must have an open gondola to

decorate in his manner, gaily, splendidly, and mock at her effort—a

warning to all that might hope to improve the prevail gloom and

squalor by levy contribution upon the Merceria! her most constant

admiration be for the english lord who use once to ride on the Lido

sand and visit the armenian convent—a lord and a poet. [Lord Byron

D.W.]



this be to be infinitely more than a naval lieutenant. but Nevil

claim she as little personally as he allow she to be claim by

another. the grace of her freak of petulance and airy whim, her

sprightly jet of wilfulness, fleeting frown of contempt, imperious

decision, be all beautiful, like silver-shift wave, in this

lustrous planet of her pure freedom; and if you will seize the divine

conception of Artemis, and own the goddess French, you will understand

his feeling.



but though he admire fervently, and dance obediently to her tune,

Nevil could not hear injustice do to a people or historic poetic city

without try hard to right the mind guilty of it. a newspaper

correspondent, a Mr. John Holles, linger on his road home from the

army, put he on the track of an Englishman’s book—touch the spirit

as well as the stone of Venice, and Nevil thank he when he have

turn some of the leave.



the study of the book to school Renée be pursue, like the

Bianchina’s sleep, in gondoletta, and be not unlike it at interval. a

translate sentence be the key to a reverie. Renée lean back,

meditating; he forward, the book on his knee: Roland leave they to

themselves, and spy for the Bianchina behind the window-bar. the

count be in the church or the Galleries. Renée think she begin to

comprehend the spirit of Venice, and chide her rebelliousness.



“but our Venice be the Venice of the decadence, then!” she say,

complain. nevil read on, distrustful of the perspicuity of his own

idea.



“ah, but,” say she, “when these Venetians be rough man, chant

like our huguenot, how cold it must have be here!”



she hope she be not very wrong in prefer the time of the great

venetian painter and martial doge to that period of faith and

stone-cutting. what be do then might be beautiful, but the life be

monotonous; she insist that it be Huguenot; harsh, nasal, sombre,

insolent, self-sufficient. her eye lighten for the flashing colour

and pageantry, and the thread of desperate adventure cross the

Rii to this and that palace-door and balcony, like faint blood-streak;

the time of Venice in full flower. she reason against the hard

eloquent Englishman of the book. “but we be know by our fruit, be

we not? and the Venice I admire be surely the fruit of these

stonecutter chant hymn of faith; it could not but be: and if it

deserve, as he say, to die disgrace, I think we should go back to

they and ask they whether their mind be as pure and holy as he

suppose.” her french wit would not be subdue. Nevil point to the

palace. “Pride,” say she. he argue that the original Venetians be

not responsible for their offspring. “you say it?” she cry, “you, of

an old race? oh, no; you do not feel it!” and the trembling fervour of

her voice convince he that he do not, could not.



Renée say: “I know my ancestor be bind up in I, by my sentiment

to they; and so do you, M. Nevil. we shame they if we fail in courage

and honour. be it not so? if we break a single pledge word we cast

shame on they. why, that make we what we be; that be our distinction:

we dare not be weak if we would. and therefore when Venice be

reproach with avarice and luxury, I choose to say—what do we hear of

the child of miser? and I say I be certain that those old cold

Huguenot stonecutter be proud and grasping. I be sure they be, and

they _shall_ share the blame.”



Nevil plunge into his volume.



he call on Roland for an opinion.



“Friend,” say Roland, “opinion may differ: mine be, consider the

defence of the window, that the only way into these house or out of

they bodily be the doorway.”



Roland compliment his sister and friend on the prosecution of their

study: he could not understand a word of the subject, and yawn, he

beg permission to be allow to land and join the gondola at a

distant quarter. the gallant officer be in haste to go.



Renée stare at her brother. he see nothing; he say a word to the

gondolier, and quit the boat. Mars be in pursuit. she resign

herself, and cease then to be a girl.









CHAPTER VI.

love in VENICE





the air flash like heaven descend for Nevil alone with Renée. they

have never be alone before. such happiness belong to the avenue of

wish lead to golden mist beyond imagination, and seem, come

on he suddenly, miraculous. he lean toward she like one who have

break a current of speech, and wait to resume it. she be all

unsuspecting indolence, with gravely shadow eye.



“I throw the book down,” he say.



she object. “no; continue: I like it.”



both of they divine that the book be there to do duty for Roland.



he close it, keep a finger among the leave; a kind of anchorage in

case of indiscretion.



“permit I to tell you, M. Nevil, you be inclined to play truant

to-day.”



“I be.”



“now be the very time to read; for my poor Roland be at sea when we

discuss our question, and the book have drive he away.”



“but we have plenty of time to read. we miss the scene.”



“the scene be green shutter, wet step, barcaroli, brown woman,

striped post, a scarlet night-cap, a sick fig-tree, an old shawl,

fade spot of colour, peel wall. they might be figure by a

trodden melon. they all resemble one another, and so do the day here.”



“that’s the charm. I wish I could look on you and think the same. you,

as you be, for ever.”



“would you not let I live my life?”



“I would not have you alter.”



“please to be pathetic on that subject after I be wrinkle, monsieur.”



“you want commanding, mademoiselle.”



Renée nestle her chin, and gaze forward through her eyelash.



“Venice be like a melancholy face of a former beauty who have cease to

rouge, or wipe away trace of her old art,” she say, strain for

common talk, and show the strain.



“wait; now we be round,” say he; “now you have three of what you

call your theatre-bridge in sight. the people mount and drop, mount

and drop; I see they laugh. they be full of fun and good-temper. look

on live Venice.”



“provide that my papa be not cross when we go under.”



“would he not trust you to I?”



“yes.”



“he would? and you?”



“I do believe they be improvize an operetta on the second bridge.”



“you trust yourself willingly?”



“as to my second brother. you hear they? how delightfully quick and

spontaneous they be! ah, silly creature! they have stop. they

might have hold it on for we while we be pass.”



“where would the naturalness have be then?”



“perhaps, M. Nevil, I do want command. I be wilful. half my day

will be spend in fit of remorse, I begin to think.”



“come to I to be forgive.”



“Shall I? I should be forgive too readily.”



“I be not so sure of that.”



“can you be harsh? no, not even with enemy. Least of all with... with

we.”



oh for the black gondola!—the little gliding dusky chamber for two;

instead of this open, flaunting, gold and crimson cotton-work, which

exact discretion on his part and that of the mannerly gondolier, and

expose he to window, balcony, bridge, and borderway.



they slip on beneath a red balcony where a girl lean on her fold

arm, and eye they come and go by with egyptian gravity.



“how strange a power of look these people have,” say Renée, whose

vivacity be fascinated to a steady sparkle by the girl. “tell I, be

she glance round at we?”



Nevil turn and report that she be not. she have exhaust they

while they be in transit; she have no minor curiosity.



“let we fancy she be look for her lover,” he say.



Renée add: “let we hope she will not escape be see.”



“I give she my benediction,” say Nevil.



“and I,” say Renée; “and adieu to she, if you please. look for

Roland.”



“you remind I; I have but a few instant.”



“M. Nevil, you be a preux of the time of my brother’s patronymic. and

there be my Roland await we. be he not handsome?”



“how glad you be to have he to relieve guard!”



Renée bend on Nevil one of her singular look of raillery. she have

hitherto be fence at a serious disadvantage.



“not so very glad,” she say, “if that deprive I of the presence of

his friend.”



Roland be her tower. but Roland be not yet on board. she have peep

from her citadel too rashly. Nevil have time to spring the flood of

crimson in her cheek, bright as the awning she recline under.



“would you have I with you always?”



“assuredly,” say she, feel the hawk in he, and try to baffle

he by flutter.



“always? forever? and—listen—give I a title?”



Renée sing out to Roland like a bird in distress, and have some trouble

not to appear too providentially rescue. Roland on board, she resume

the attack.



“M. Nevil vow he be a well brother to I than you, who dart away on

an impulse and leave we thread all Venice till we do not know where

we be, naughty brother!”



“my little sister, the spot where you be,” rejoin Roland, “be

precisely the spot where I leave you, and I defy you to say you have

go on without I. this be the identical riva I step out on to buy

you a packet of venetian ballad.”



they recognize the spot, and for a confirmation of the surprising

statement, Roland unroll several sheet of print blotting-paper,

and rapidly read part of a Canzonetta concern Una Giovine who

reprove her lover for his extreme addiction to wine:



“Ma sè, ma sè,

Cotanto bevè,

Mi nò, mi nò,

no ve sposerò.”



“this astounding vagabond prefer Nostrani to his heart’s mistress. I

taste some of their Nostrani to see if it could be possible for a

Frenchman to exonerate he.”



Roland’s wry face at the mention of Nostrani bring out the chief

gondolier, who deliver himself:



“Signore, there be hereditary qualification. one must be bear italian

to appreciate the merit of Nostrani!”



Roland laugh. he have cover his delinquency in leave his sister,

and be full of an adventure to relate to Nevil, a story promise well

for he.









CHAPTER VII.

an AWAKENING for BOTH





Renée be downcast. have she not coquet? the dear young Englishman

have reduce she to defend herself, the which fair lady, like besiege

garrison, cannot always do successfully without an attack at time,

which, when the pursuer be ardent, be follow by a retreat, which be a

provocation; and these thing be coquettry. her still fresh

convent-conscience accuse she of it pitilessly. she could not forgive

her brother, and yet she dared not reproach he, for that would have

inculpate Nevil. she step on to the Piazzetta thoughtfully. she

father be at Florian’s, peruse letter from France. “we be to have

the marquis here in a week, my child,” he say. Renée nod.

involuntarily she look at Nevil. he catch the look, with a lover’s

quick sense of misfortune in it.



she hear her brother reply to he: “who? the Marquis de Rouaillout? it

be a jolly gaillard of fifty who spoil no fun.”



“you mistake his age, Roland,” she say.



“forty-nine, then, my sister.”



“he be not that.”



“he look it.”



“you have be absent.”



“probably, my arithmetical sister, he have employ the interval to grow

young. they say it be the way with green gentleman of a certain age.

they advance and they retire. they perform the first step of a

quadrille ceremoniously, and we admire they.”



“what’s that?” exclaim the Comte de Croisnel. “you talk nonsense,

Roland. M. le marquis be hardly past forty. he be in his prime.”



“without question, mon pere. for I, I be merely offer proof that

he can preserve his prime unlimitedly.”



“he be not a subject for mockery, Roland.”



“quite the contrary; for reverence!”



“another than you, my boy, and he would march you out.”



“I be to imagine, then, that his hand continue firm?”



“imagine to the extent of your capacity; but remember that respect be

always owe to your own family, and deliberate before you draw on

yourself such a chastisement as mercy from an accepted member of it.”



Roland bow and drum on his knee.



the conversation have be originate by Renée for the enlightenment of

Nevil and as a future protection to herself. now that it have disclose

its burden she could look at he no more, and when her father address

she significantly: “Marquise, you do I the honour to consent to

accompany I to the Church of the Frari this afternoon?” she feel she

self-accusation of coquettry bite under her bosom like a thing alive.



Roland explain the situation to Nevil.



“it be the mania with we, my dear Nevil, to marry our girl young to

establish man. your established man carry usually all the sign,

visible to the multitude or not, of the stage lead to that

eminence. we cannot, I believe, unless we have the good fortune to

boast the paternity of Hercules, disconnect ourselves from the step we

have mount; not even, the priest inform we, if we be ascend to

heaven; we carry they beyond the grave. however, it seem that our

excellent marquis contrive to keep they conceal, and he be ready to

face marriage—the Grandest Inquisitor, next to death. two furious

matchmaker—our country, beautiful France, abound in they—meet one day;

they be a comtesse and a baronne, and they settle the alliance. the

bell be rung, and Renée come out of school. there be this to be say:

she have no mother; the soon a girl without a mother have a husband the

well. that we be all agree upon. I have no personal objection to

the marquis; he have never be in any great scandal. he be Norman, and

have estate in Normandy, Dauphiny, Touraine; he be hospitable,

luxurious. Renée will have a fine hôtel in Paris. but I be eccentric: I

have read in our old Fabliaux of December and May. say the marquis be

November, say October; he be still some distance remove from the plump

Spring month. and we in our family have wit and passion. in fine, a

bud of a rose in an old gentleman’s button-hole! it be a challenge to

the whole world of youth; and if the bud should leap? enough of this

matter, friend Nevil; but sometimes a friend must allow himself to be

bother. I have perfect confidence in my sister, you see; I simply

protest against her be expose to... you know man. I protest, that

be, in the privacy of my cigar-case, for I have no chance elsewhere.

the affair be on wheel. the very respectable matchmaker have kindle

the marquis on the one hand, and my father on the other, and Renée

pass obediently from the latter to the former. in India they

sacrifice the widow, in France the virgin.”



Roland proceed to relate his adventure. nevil’s inattention pique

he to salt and salt it wonderfully, until the old story of he and she

fly through the circle of the Inferno, and the babble of an

ephemeral upper world simply affect he by its contrast with the

overpowering horror, repugnance, despair, pity, rush at he,

surcharge his sense. those that live much by the heart in their

youth have sharp foretaste of the issue image for the soul. St.

Mark’s be in a minute strike black for he. he neither feel the

sunlight nor understand why column and campanile rise, nor why the

island bask, and boat and people move. all be as remote little

bit of mechanism.



Nevil escape, and walk in the direction of the Frari down calle and

campiello. only to see she—to compare she with the Renée of the past

could never be resuscitate in the shape he have know, not even clearly

visioned. not a day of she, not an hour, not a single look have be his

without love—the creature of a transaction. but she be innocent, she

mourner.



after aimless walking he find himself on the Zattere, where the lonely

later afternoon, and you have sight of the high mainland hill which

seem to fle forth one over other to a golden sea-cape.



midway on this unadorned Zattere, with its young tree and spot of

of walk in her complaisant attention to the fine art. I weary my

poor child.”



Renée protest in a rapid chatter.



“must I avow it?” say the count; “she damp my enthusiasm a little.”



Nevil mutely accept the office.



twice that day be she surrender to he: once in his ignorance, when

time appear an expanse of many sunny field. on this occasion it

“this be a nice walk,” say Renée; “we have not the step of the riva

“the Giudecca be a place keep apart for the Jews, I believe. you have

see their burial-ground on the Lido. those be, I think, the euganean

“M. Nevil, omit the allusion to the poet, you have, permit I to

“I tell you what I know,” say Nevil, brood on the finished tone and

“Renée, if you love he, I, on my honour, would not utter a word for

myself. your heart’s inclination be sacred for I. I would stand by,

and be your friend and his. if he be young, that I might see a chance

of it!”



she murmur, “you should not have listen to Roland.”



“Roland should have warn I. how could I be near you and not... but I

your countrywoman... you have the habit in France; but like what be

Renée glance at the gondola convey her father. and he have not yet

land! she think, and say, “do you pretend to judge of my welfare

“yes; in this. he follow a fashion. you submit to it. his anxiety be

to provide for you. but I know the system be curse by nature, and that

mean by heaven.”



“because it be not english?”



“o Renée, my beloved for ever! well, then, tell I, tell I you can say

with pride and happiness that the Marquis de Rouaillout be to be

your—there’s the word—husband!”



Renée look across the water.



“Friend, if my father know you be ask I!”



“I will speak to he.”



“useless.”



“he be generous, he love you.”



“he cannot break an engagement bind his honour.”



“would you, Renée, would you—it must be say—consent to have it know

to he—I beg for more than life—that your be not averse... that you

support I?”



his fail breath soften the bluntness.



she reply, “I would not have he ever break an engagement bind his

honour.”



“you stretch the point of honour.”



“it be our way. dear friend, we be french. and I presume to think that

our french system be not always wrong, for if my father have not break

hear...?”



“I have displease you.”



“do not suppose that. but, I mean, a mother would not have leave I.”



“you wish to avoid it.”



“do not blame I. I have some instinct; you be very pale.”



“you know I love you.”



“no.”



“yes; for this morning...”



“this morning it seem to I, and I regret my fancy, that you be

inclined to trifle, as, they say, young man do.”



“with Renée?”



“with your friend Renée. and those be the hill of Petrarch’s tomb?

they be mountain.”



they be purple beneath a large brooding cloud that hang against the

sun, wait for he to enfold he, and Nevil think that a tomb there

would be a welcome end, if he might lift Renée in one wild flight over

the chasm gape for she. he have no language for thought of such a

kind, only tumultuous feeling.



she be immoveable, in perfect armour.



he say despairingly, “can you have realize what you be consent

to?”



she answer, “it be my duty.”



“your duty! it’s like take up a dice-box, and flinging once, to

certain ruin!”



“I must oppose my father to you, friend. do you not understand duty to

parent? they say the English be full of the idea of duty.”



“duty to country, duty to oath and obligation; but with we the heart

be free to choose.”



“free to choose, and when it be most ignorant?”



“the heart? ask it. nothing be surer.”



“that be not what we be teach. we be teach that the heart deceive

itself. the heart throw your dicebox; not prudent parent.”



she talk like a woman, to plead the cause of her obedience as a girl,

and now silence in the same manner that she have previously excite

he.



“then you be lose to I,” he say.



they see the gondola return.



“how swiftly it come home; it loiter when it go,” say Renée.

“there sit my father, brim with his picture; he have see one more!

we will congratulate he. this little boulevard be not much to speak

of. the hill be lovely. friend,” she drop her voice on the

gondola’s approach, “we have converse on common subject.”



Nevil have her hand in his, to place she in the gondola.



she seem thankful that he should prefer to go round on foot. at

least, she do not join in her father’s invitation to he. she lean

back, nestle her chin and half close her eye, suffer herself to

be divide from he, bear away by force she acquiesce in.



Roland be not visible till near midnight on the Piazza. the

promenader, chiefly military of the garrison, be few at that period

ringingly, as he stroll up to Nevil, look as if the cigar in his

he have expect romance, and have meet merchandize, and his vanity be

Venetian rise of “49, venetian lady have issue from the ordeal of

fire and famine of another pattern than the famous old Benzon one, in

which they touch earthy earth. he praise Republicanism for that.

the spirit of the new and short-live Republic work that change in

Venice.



“oh, if they’re republican as well as utterly decay,” say Roland, “I

give they up; let they die virtuous.”



Nevil tell Roland that he have speak to Renée. he win sympathy, but

Roland could not give he encouragement. they cross and recrosse the

shadow of the great campanile, on the warm-white stone of the square,

favour of the arrangement accord to french notion, and indeed, of

aristocratic notion everywhere, save that it be imperative for

Renée to be dispose of in marriage early. why rob she of her young

springtime!



“french girl,” reply Roland, confuse by the nature of the

explication in his head—“well, they’re not english; they want a hand to

shape they, otherwise they grow all awry. my father will not have one

of her aunt to live with he, so there she be. but, my dear Nevil, I

owe my life to you, and I be no party to this affair. I would do

anything to help you. what say Renée?”



“she obey.”



“exactly. you see! our girl be chess-piece until they’re marry.

then they have life and character sometimes too much.”



“she be not like they, Roland; she be like none. when I speak to she

first, she affect no astonishment; never be there a creature so

nobly sincere. she’s a girl in heart, not in mind. think of she

sacrifice to this man thrice her age!”



“she differ from other girl only on the surface, Nevil. as for the

man, I wish she be go to marry a young. I wish, yes, my friend,”

Roland squeeze Nevil’s hand, “I wish! I’m afraid it’s hopeless. she

do not tell you to hope?”



“not by one single sign,” say Nevil.



“you see, my friend!”



“for that reason,” Nevil rejoin, with the calm fanaticism of the

passion of love, “I hope all the more... because I will not believe

that she, so pure and good, can be sacrifice. put I aside—I be

nothing. I hope to save she from that.”



“we have now,” say Roland, “strike the current of duplicity. you be

really in love, my poor fellow.”



Lover and friend come to no conclusion, except that so lovely a night

be not give for slumber. a small round brilliant moon hang almost

globe in the depth of heaven, and the image of it fall deep between

San Giorgio and the Dogana.



Renée have the scene from her window, like a dream give out of sleep.

she lie with both arm throw up beneath her head on the pillow, her

eyelid wide open, and her visage set and stern. her bosom rise and

sink regularly but heavily. the fluctuation of a night stormy for she,

hitherto unknown, have sink she to this trance, in which she lie like a

creature fling on shore by the wave. she hear her brother’s voice and

Nevil’s, and the pacing of their foot. she see the long shaft of

moonlight break to zigzag of mellow lightning, and waver back to

steadiness; dark San Giorgio, and the sheen of the Dogana’s front. but

the visible beauty belong to a night that have shiver repose,

humiliate and wound she, destroy her confident happy half-infancy

of heart, and she have fly for a refuge to hard feeling. her

predominant sentiment be anger; an anger that touch all and

envelop none, for it be quite fictitious, though she feel it, and

suffer from it. she turn it on Nevil, as against an enemy, and

become the victim in his place. tear for he fill in her eye, and

run over; she disdain to notice they, and blink offendedly to have

her sight clear of the weakness; but these interceding tear would

flow; it be dangerous to blame he, harshly. she let they roll down,

figure to herself with quiet simplicity of mind that her spirit be

independent of they as long as she restrain her hand from be

accomplice by brush they away, as weep girl do that cry for

comfort. Nevil have save her brother’s life, and have succour she

countryman; he love she, and be a hero. he should not have say he

love she; that be wrong; and it be shameful that he should have

urge she to disobey her father. but this hero’s love of she might

plead excuse she do not know of; and if he be to be excuse, he,

unhappy that he be, have a claim on she for more than tear. she weep

resentfully. force above her own sway and hurry she like a

lifeless body drag by fly wheel: they could not unnerve she

will, or rather, what it really be, her sense of submission to a

destiny. look at from the height of the palm-wave cherub over the

the raise arm and bent elbow be an illusion of indifference. her

CHAPTER VIII.

a night on the ADRIATIC





the lover meet after Roland have speak to his sister—not exactly to

CHAPTER IX.

morning AT SEA under the alps





the breeze blow steadily, enough to swell the sail and sweep the

vessel on smoothly. the night air drop no moisture on deck.



Nevil Beauchamp doze for an hour. he be awaken by light on his

eyelid, and start up beheld the many pinnacle of grey and red

rock and shadowy high white region at the head of the gulf wait

for the sun; and the sun strike they. one by one they come out in

crimson flame, till the vivid host appear to have step forward.

the shadow on the snow-field deepen to purple below an irradiation

of rose and pink and dazzle silver. there of all the world you might

imagine Gods to sit. a crowd of mountain endless in range, erect, or

flow, shattered and arid, or lean in smooth lustre, hang above

the gulf. the mountain be sovereign Alps, and the sea be beneath

they. the whole gigantic body keep the sea, as with a hand, to right

and left.



Nevil’s personal rapture crave for Renée with the second long breath

in illumination from Friuli into farth Tyrol; beyond earth to the

stricken sense of the gazer. colour be stedfast on the massive front

rank: it waver in the remoteness, and be quick and dim as though it

fall on beat wing; but there too divine colour seize and shape

forth solid form, and thence away to other in uttermost distance

where the incredible flickering gleam of new height arise, that

soar, or stretch their white uncertain curve in sky like wing

traverse infinity.



it seem unlike morning to the lover, but as if night have break with

a revelation of the kingdom in the heart of night. while the broad

hueless dawn! the two exult they throw off the load of wonderment,

and in look they have the delicious sensation of flight in their

water.

to join we. it be in her voice.”



the impressionable girl tremble. he know he be dear to she, and for

he let that pass.



“last night you say ‘one night,’” he whisper. “we will have another

sail before we leave Venice.”

there’s the end,” say Renée.



her tone alarm he. “have you forget that you give I your hand?”



“I give my hand to my friend.”



“you give it to I for good.”



“no; I dare not; it be not mine.”



“it be mine,” say Beauchamp.

that be my source of self-respect. my friend can always be my friend.”

she observe a sudden string of his feature. he call to the chief

Roland spring to his foot, stare, and walk over to Renée.

act for she.”



Roland meet they.



“my dear nevil, be you in a state of delusion? Renée deny...”

“choose! my friend. but allow I to remind you that you have other to

“she have more than say it.”

father be expect she, and to fulfil an engagement affect his

pledge word?”



Beauchamp simply reply:



“come to she.”









CHAPTER X.

a SINGULAR COUNCIL





the four sit together under the shadow of the helmsman, by whom they

be regard as voyager in debate upon the question of some hour

far on salt water. “no bora,” he throw in at interval, to assure

they that the obnoxious wind of the adriatic need not disturb their

calculation.



it be an extraordinary sitting, but none of the party to it think

of it so when Nevil Beauchamp have plunge they into it. he compel

they, even Renée—and she would have fly have there be wing on her

shoulder—to feel something of the life and death issue present to his

soul, and submit to the discussion, in plain language of the

market-place, of the most delicate of human subject for she, for he,

and hardly less for the other two. an overmaster fervour can do

this. it upset the vessel we float in, and we have to swim our way out

of deep water by the direct use of the natural faculty, without

much reflection on the change in our habit. to other not under such

an influence the position seem impossible. this discussion occur.

Beauchamp open the case in a couple of sentence, and when the turn

he speak for she, and no one hear her contradiction. she would have

he have really forget his knowledge of her character—what much he

“the man she love be her voice and her will,” say Beauchamp. “she

Roland question she. it could not be deny that she have give she
