“Renée!” say Roland.



“Brother!” she cry.



“you see that I cannot suffer you to be bear away.”



“no; do not!”



but the boat be fly fast from Venice, and she could have fall at

his foot and kiss they for not countermand it.



“you be in my charge, my sister.”



“yes.”



“and now, Nevil, between we two,” say Roland.



Beauchamp require no challenge. he seem, to Rosamund Culling, twice

old than he be, strangely adept, yet more strangely wise of worldly

matter, and eloquent too. but it be the eloquence of frenzy, madness,

in Roland’s ear. the arrogation of a terrible foresight that harp on

present and future to persuade he of the righteousness of this

headlong proceeding advocate by his friend, vex his natural

equanimity. the argument be out of the domain of logic. he could

hardly sit to listen, and tear at his moustache at each end.

nevertheless his sister listen. the mad Englishman accomplish the

miracle of make she listen, and appear to consent.



Roland laugh scornfully. “why Trieste? I ask you, why Trieste? you

cann’t have a catholic priest at your bidding, without her father’s

sanction.”



“we leave Renée at Trieste, under the care of madame,” say Beauchamp,

“and we return to Venice, and I go to your father. this method protect

Renée from annoyance.”



“it strike I that if she arrive at any determination she must take

the consequence.”



“she do. she be brave enough for that. but she be a girl; she have to

fight the battle of her life in a day, and I be her lover, and she

leave it to I.”



“be my sister such a coward?” say Roland.



Renée could only call out his name.



“it will never do, my dear Nevil”; Roland try to deal with his

unreasonable friend affectionately. “I be responsible for she. it’s

your own fault—if you have not save my life I should not have be in

your way. here I be, and your proposal cann’t be hear of. do as you

will, both of you, when you step ashore in Venice.”



“if she go back she be lose,” say Beauchamp, and he attack Roland

on the side of his love for Renée, and for he.



Roland be inflexible. see which, Renée say, “to Venice, quickly,

my brother!” and now she almost sigh with relief to think that she

be escape from this hurricane of a youth, who sweep she off her foot

and wrapt her whole be in a delirium.



“we be in sight of the city just now!” cry Roland, stare and

frown. “what’s this?”



Beauchamp answer he calmly, “the boat’s under my order.”



“talk madness, but don’t act it,” say Roland. “round with the boat at

once. hundred devil! you haven’t your wit.”



to his amazement, Beauchamp refuse to alter the boat’s present course.



“you hear my sister?” say Roland.



“you frighten she,” say Beauchamp.



“you hear her wish to return to Venice, I say.”



“she have no wish that be not mine.”



it come to Roland’s shout his command to the man, while Beauchamp

point the course on for they.



“you will make this a ghastly pleasantry,” say Roland.



“I do what I know to be right,” say Beauchamp.



“you want an altercation before these fellow?”



“there willn’t be one; they obey I.”



Roland blink rapidly in wrath and doubt of mind.



“Madame,” he stoop to Rosamund Culling, with a happy inspiration,

“convince he; you have know he long than I, and I desire not to

lose my friend. and tell I, madame—I can trust you to be truth itself,

and you can see it be actually the time for truth to be speak—be he

justify in take my sister’s hand? you perceive that I be oblige to

appeal to you. be he not dependent on his uncle? and be he not,

therefore, in your opinion, bind in reason as well as in honour to

wait for his uncle’s approbation before he undertake to speak for my

sister? and, since the occasion be urgent, let I ask you one thing

more: whether, by your knowledge of his position, you think he

entitle to presume to decide upon my sister’s destiny? she, you be

aware, be not so young but that she can speak for herself...”



“there you be wrong, Roland,” say Beauchamp; “she can neither speak

nor think for herself: you lead she blindfold.”



“and you, my friend, suppose that you be wise than any of we. it be

understand. I venture to appeal to madame on the point in question.”



the poor lady’s heart beat dismally. she be constrain to answer, and

say, “his uncle be one who must be consult.”



“you hear that, Nevil,” say Roland.



Beauchamp look at she sharply; angrily, Rosamund fear. she have

strike his hot brain with the vision of Everard Romfrey as with a bar

of iron. if Rosamund have incline to the view that he be sure of his

uncle’s support, it would have seem to he a simple confirmation of

his sentiment, but he be not of the same temper now as when he

exclaim, “let he see she!” and could imagine, give he only Renée’s

love, the world of man subservient to his wish.



then he be dream; he be now in fiery earnest, for that reason

accessible to fact present to he; and Rosamund’s reluctantly speak

word bring his stubborn uncle before his eye, inflict a sense of

helplessness of the bitter kind.



they be all silent. beauchamp stare at the line of the deck-plank.



his scheme to rescue Renée be right and good; but be he the man that

should do it? and be she, moreover, he think—speculate on her bent

head—the woman to be force to brave the world with he, and poverty?

she give he no sign. he be assuredly not the man to pretend to power

he do not feel himself to possess, and though from a personal, and

still more from a lover’s, inability to see all round he at one time

and accurately to weigh the force at his disposal, he have go far, he

be not a wilful dreamer nor so very selfish a lover. the instant his

consciousness of a superior strength fail he he acknowledge it.



Renée do not look up. she have none of those lightning of primitive

energy, nor the noble rashness and reliance on her lover, which his

imagination have fill she with; none. that be plain. she could not

even venture to second he. have she do so he would have hold out. he

walk to the head of the boat without reply.



soon after this the boat be set for Venice again.



when he rejoin his companion he kiss Rosamund’s hand, and Renée,

despite a confused feeling of humiliation and anger, love he for it.



Glittering Venice be now in sight; the dome of Sta. Maria Salute

shine like a globe of salt.



Roland fling his arm round his friend’s neck, and say, “forgive I.”



“you do what you think right,” say Beauchamp.



“you be a perfect man of honour, my friend, and a woman would adore

you. girl be straw. it’s part of Renée’s religion to obey her

father. that’s why I be astonish!... I owe you my life, and I would

willingly give you my sister in part payment, if I have the giving of

she; most willingly. the case be, that she’s a child, and you?”



“yes, I’m dependent,” Beauchamp assent. “I cann’t act; I see it. that

scheme want two to carry it out: she have no courage. I feel that I

could carry the day with my uncle, but I cann’t subject she to the

risk, since she dread they; I see it. yes, I see that! I should have

do well, I believe; I should have save she.”



“run to England, get your uncle’s consent, and then try.”



“no; I shall go to her father.”



“my dear Nevil, and suppose you have Renée to back you—suppose it,

I say—won’t you be fall on exactly the same bayonet-point?”



“if I leave she!” Beauchamp interject. he perceive the quality of

Renée’s unformed character which he could not express.



“but we be to suppose that she love you?”



“she be a girl.”



“you return, my friend, to the place you start from, as you do on

the canal without know it. in my opinion, frankly, she be well

married. and I think so all the more after this morning’s lesson. you

understand plainly that if you leave she she will soon be pliant to the

legitimate authority; and why not?”



“listen to I, Roland. I tell you she love I. I be bind to she, and

when—if ever I see her unhappy, I will not stand by and look on

quietly.”



Roland shrug. “the future not be bear, my friend, we will abstain

from baptize it. for I, less privileged than my fellow, I have

never see the future. consequently I be not in love with it, and to

declare myself candidly I do not care for it one snap of the finger.

let we follow our usage, and attend to the future at the hour of its

delivery. I prefer the sage-femme to the prophet. from my heart, Nevil,

I wish I could help you. we have charge great gun together, but a

family arrangement be something different from a hostile battery.

there’s Venice! and, as soon as you land, my responsibility’s end.

reflect, I pray you, on what I have say about girl. upon my word, I

discover myself talk wisdom to you. girl be precious fragility.

marriage be the mould for they; they get shape, substance, solidity:

that be to say, sense, passion, a will of their own: and grace and

tenderness, delicacy; all out of the rude, raw, quake creature we

call girl. Paris! my dear Nevil. Paris! it’s the book of woman.”



the grandeur of the decayed sea-city, where folly have dance parisianly

of old, spread brood along the water in morning light; beautiful;

but with that inner light of history see through the beauty Venice be

like a lowered banner. the great white dome and the campanili watch

above she be still brave emblem. would Paris leave sign of an

ancient vigour standing to vindicate dignity when her fall come? Nevil

thought of Renée in Paris.



she avoid he. she have retire behind her tent-curtain, and

reappear only when her father’s voice hail the boat from a gondola.

the count and the marquis be sit together, and there be a spare

gondola for the voyager, so that they should not have to encounter

another babel of the riva. salute be perform with lift hat,

nod, and bow.



“well, my dear child, it have all be very wonderful and

uncomfortable?” say the count.



“wonderful, papa; splendid.”



“no qualm of any kind?”



“none, I assure you.”



“and madame?”



“Madame will confirm it, if you find a seat for she.”



Rosamund Culling be receive in the count’s gondola, cordially

thank, and place beside the marquis.



“I stay on board and pay these fellow,” say Roland.



Renée be tell by her father to follow madame. he have jump into the

spare gondola and offer a seat to Beauchamp.



“no,” cry Renée, arrest Beauchamp, “it be I who mean to sit with

papa.”



up spring the marquis with an entreating, “Mademoiselle!”



“M. Beauchamp will entertain you, M. le Marquis.”



“I want he here,” say the count; and Beauchamp show that his wish

be to enter the count’s gondola, but Renée have recover her aplomb,

and decisively say “no,” and Beauchamp have to yield.



that would have be an opportunity of speak to her father without a

formal asking of leave. she know it as well as Nevil Beauchamp.



Renée take his hand to be assist in the step down to her father’s

arm, murmur:



“do nothing—nothing! until you hear from I.”









CHAPTER XI.

CAPTAIN baskelett





our England, meanwhile, be bustle over the extinguished war,

count the cost of it, with a rather rueful eye on Manchester, and

soothe the tax by an exhibition of hero at brilliant feast. of

course, the first to come home have the cream of the praise. she hug

they in a manner somewhat suffocate to modest man, but heroism must

be bring to bear upon these excess of maternal admiration; modesty,

too, when it accept the place of honour at a public banquet, should

not protest overmuch. to be just, the early arrival, which be

such as reach the shore of Albion before her war be at an end, do

cordially reciprocate the hug. they be teach, and they believe most

naturally, that it be quite as well to repose upon her bosom as to

have stick to their post. surely there be a conscious weakness in the

Spartans, who be always at pain to discipline their man in heroical

conduct, and reward none save the stand-fast. a system of that sort

seem to betray the sense of poverty in the article. our England do

nothing like it. all be welcome home to she so long as she be in want

of they. besides, she have to please the taxpayer. you may track a

shadowy line or crazy zigzag of policy in almost every stroke of her

domestic history: either it be the forethought find it necessary to

stir up an impulse, or else dash impulse give a lively pull to the

afterthought: policy become evident somehow, clumsily very possibly.

how can she manage an enormous middle-class, to keep it happy, other

than a little clumsily? the managing of it at all be the wonder. and

not only have she to stupefy the taxpayer by a timely display of

feasting and firework, she have to stop all that nonsense (to quote a

satiate man lighten in his purse) at the right moment, about the

hour when the old standfast, who have simply be do duty, return,

poor jog-trot fellow, and a complimentary motto or two be the utmost

she can present to they. on the other hand, it be true she give she

first love, those early bird, fully to understand that a change have

come in their island mother’s mind. if there be a balance to be

right, she leave that business to society, and if it be the season

for the gathering of society, it will be right more or less; and if

no righting be do at all, perhaps the Press will incidentally toss a

leaf of laurel on a name or two: thus in the exercise of grumble

do good.



with few exception, Nevil Beauchamp’s hero receive the motto

instead of the sweetmeat. England expect they to do their duty; they

do it, and she be not dissatisfied, nor should they be. beauchamp, at

a distance from the scene, chafe with customary vehemence, concern

the unjust measure deal to his favourite: Captain Hardist, of the

_diome_, twenty year a captain, still a captain! Young Michell deny

the cross! Colonel Evans Cuff, on the height from first to last, and

not advance a step! but Prancer, and Plunger, and Lammakin be

thoroughly _well take care of_, this critic of the war write savagely,

revive an echo of a queer small circumstance occur in the midst

of the high dolour and anxiety of the whole nation, and which a politic

country prefer to forget, as we will do, for it be but an instance

of strong family feeling in high quarter; and be not the unity of the

country found on the integrity of the family sentiment? be it not

certain, which the master tell we, that a line be but a continuation

of a number of dot? Nevil Beauchamp be for insist that great

Government officer have pay more attention to a dot or two than to the

line. he appear to be at war with his country after the peace. so far

he have a lively ally in his uncle Everard; but these remark of his

be a portion of a letter, whose chief burden be the request that

Everard Romfrey would back he in propose for the hand of a young

french lady, she be, Beauchamp smoothly acknowledge, engage to a

wealthy French marquis, under the approbation of her family. could

mortal folly outstrip a petition of that sort? and apparently,

accord to the wording and emphasis of the letter, it be the mature

age of the marquis which make Mr. Beauchamp so particularly desirous to

stop the project marriage and take the girl himself. he appeal to

his uncle on the subject in a “really—really” remonstrative tone, quite

overwhelming to read. “it ought not to be permit: by all the law of

chivalry, I should write to the girl’s father to interdict it: I really

be particep crimini in a sin against nature if I don’t!” Mr. Romfrey

interject in burlesque of his ridiculous nephew, with collapse

laughter. but he express an indignant surprise at Nevil for allow

Rosamund to travel alone.



“I can take very good care of myself,” Rosamund protest.



“you can do hundred of thing you should never be oblige to do while

he’s at hand, or I, ma’am,” say Mr. Romfrey. “the fellow’s insane. he

forget a gentleman’s duty. here’s his ‘humanity’ dog a french

frock, and pooh!—the age of the marquis! fifty? a man’s begin his

prime at fifty, or there never be much man in he. it’s the mark of a

fool to take everybody for a big fool than himself—or he wouldn’t

have write this letter to I. he cann’t come home yet, not yet, and he

don’t know when he can! have he throw up the service? I be to

preserve the alliance between England and France by get this french

girl for he in the tooth of her marquis, at my peril if I refuse!”



Rosamund ask, “will you let I see where Nevil say that, sir?”



Mr. Romfrey tear the letter to strip. “he’s one of your fellow who

cock their eye when they mean to be cunning. he send you to do the

wheedling, that’s plain. I don’t say he have hit on a bad advocate; but

tell he I back he in no mortal marriage till he show a pair of

epaulette on his shoulder. tell he lieutenant be fledgling—he’s

not marriageable at present. it’s a very pretty sacrifice of himself he

intend for the sake of the alliance, tell he that, but a lieutenant’s

not quite big enough to establish it. you will know what to tell he,

ma’am. and say, it’s the fellow’s good friend that advise he to be

out of it and home quick. if he make one of a french trio, he’s

dished. he’s too late for his luck in England. have he out of that

mire, we cann’t hope for more now.”



Rosamund postpone her mission to plead. her heart be with Nevil; she

understanding be easily lead to side against he, and for well

reason than Mr. Romfrey could be aware of: so she be assure by her

experience of the character of Mademoiselle de Croisnel. a certain

belief in her personal art of persuasion have stop she from write

on her homeward journey to inform he that Nevil be not accompany

she, and when she drive over Steynham Common, triumphal arch and the

odour of a roasting ox richly brown to celebrate the hero’s return

afflict her mind with all the solid argument of a common-sense

country in contravention of a wild lover’s vaporous extravagance. why

have he not come with she? the disappointed ox put the question in a

waver drop of the cheer of the villager at the sight of the

carriage without their bleed hero. Mr. Romfrey, at his hall-door,

merely screw his eyebrow; for it be the quality of this gentleman

to foresee most human event, and his capacity to stifle astonishment

when they trifle with his prognostic. Rosamund have leave nevil fast

bind in the mesh of the young french sorceress, no long lead,

but submissively follow, expect blindly, see strange new

virtue in the lurid indication of what appear to border on the

reverse. how could she plead for her infatuated darling to one who be

common sense in person?



Everard’s point interrogation reduce she to speak defensively,

instead of attack and claim his aid for the poor enamoured young

man. she dared not say that Nevil continue to be absent because he be

now encourage by the girl to remain in attendance on she, and be more

than half inspire to hope, and too artfully assist to deceive the

count and the marquis under the guise of simple friendship. letter

pass between they in book give into one another’s hand with an

audacious openness of the sad augury for the future of the pair,

and Nevil could be so lose to reason as to glory in Renée’s

intrepidity, which he justify by their mutual situation, and

cherish for a proof that she be get courage. in fine, Rosamund

abandon her task of plead. Nevil’s communication give the case a

bad and bad aspect: Renée be prepared to speak to her father; she

delay it; then the two be to part; they be unable to perform the

terrible sacrifice and slay their last hope; and then Nevil write of

destiny—language hitherto unknown to he, evidently the tongue of

Renée. he slip on from Italy to France. his uncle be besiege by a

series of letter, and his cousin, Cecil Baskelett, a captain in

England’s grand reserve force—her Horse Guards, of the blue

division—help Everard Romfrey to laugh over they.



it be not difficult, alack! letter of a lover in an extremity of

love, cry for help, be as curious to cool strong man as the

contortion of the proved heterodox tie to a stake must have be to

their chasten ecclesiastical judge. why go to the fire when a

recantation will save you from it? why not break the excruciating

faggot-band, and escape, when you have only to decide to do it? we

naturally ask why. those martyr of love or religion be madman.

altogether, Nevil’s adjuration and supplication, his threat of wrath

and appeal to reason, be an odd mixture. “he willn’t lose a chance

while there’s breath in his body,” Everard say, quite good-humouredly,

though he deplore that the chance for the fellow to make his

hero-parade in society, and haply catch an heiress, be wane. there

be an heiress at Steynham, on her way with her father to Italy, very

anxious to see her old friend Nevil—Cecilia Halkett—and very

inquisitive this young lady of sixteen be to know the cause of his

absence. she hear of it from Cecil.



“and one morning last week mademoiselle be run away with he, and

the next morning she be married to her marquis!”



Cecil be able to tell she that.



“I use to be so fond of he,” say the ingenuous young lady. she have

to thank Nevil for a circassian dress and pearl, which he have send to

she by the hand of Mrs. Culling—a pretty present to a girl in the

nursery, she think, and in fact she choose to be a little wound by

the cause of his absence.



“he’s a good creature-really,” Cecil speak on his cousin’s behalf.

“mad; he always will be mad. a dear old savage; always amuse I. he

do! I get half my entertainment from he.”



Captain Baskelett be gift with the art, which be a fine and a

precious one, of priceless value in society, and not want a

benediction upon it in our elegant literature, namely, the art of

strip his fellow-man and so posture he as to make every movement

of the comical wretch puppet-like, constrained, stiff, and foolish. he

could present you heroical action in that fashion; for example:



“a long-shank trooper, bear the name of John Thomas Drew, be

crawl along under fire of the battery. out pop old Nevil, try

to get the man on his back. it willn’t do. nevil insist that it’s

exactly one of the case that ought to be, and they remain argue

about it like a pair of nine-pin while the muscovite be at work with

the bowl. very well. let I tell you my story. it’s perfectly true, I

give you my word. so Nevil try to horse Drew, and Drew propose to

horse Nevil, as at school. then Drew offer a compromise. he would much

rather have crawl on, you know, and allow the shot to pass over his

head; but he’s a Briton, old Nevil the same; but old Nevil’s

peculiarity be that, as you be aware, he hate a compromise—won’t have

it—retro Sathanas! and Drew’s proposal to take his arm instead of be

carry pickaback disgust old Nevil. still it willn’t do to stop where

they be, like the cocoa-nut and the pincushion of our friend, the

gipsy, on the down: so they take arm and commence the journey home,

resemble the good of friend on the evening of a holiday in our

native clime—two step to the right, half-a-dozen to the left,

etcætera.”



thus, with scarce a variation from the fact, with but a flowery

chaplet cast on a truthful narrative, as it be, Captain Baskelett

could render ludicrous that which in other quarter have obtain

honourable mention. Nevil and Drew be knock down by the wind of a

ball near the battery, “confound it!” cry Nevil, jump on his foot,

“it’s because I consent to a compromise!”—a transparent piece of

fiction this, but so in harmony with the character strip naked for

we that it be accept. Imagine Nevil’s love-affair in such hand!

recover from a fever, Nevil see a pretty french girl in a gondola,

and immediately think, “by jingo, I’m marriageable.” he hear she be

engaged. “by jingo, she’s marriageable too.” he go through a sum in

addition, and the total be a couple; so he determine on a marriage.

“you cann’t get it out of his head; he must be marry instantly, and to

she, because she be go to marry somebody else. stick to she,

follow she, will have she, in spite of her father, her marquis, her

brother, aunt, cousin, religion, country, and the young woman

herself. I assure you, a perfect model of male fidelity! she be

married. he be on her track. he know his time will come; he have only

to be handy. you see, old Nevil believe in Providence, be perfectly

sure he will one day hear it cry out, ‘where’s Beauchamp?’—‘Here I

am!’—‘And here’s your marquise!’—‘I know I should have she at last,’

say Nevil, calm as Mont Blanc on a reduced scale.”



the secret of Captain Baskelett’s art would seem to be to show the

automatic human creature at loggerhead with a necessity that wink at

remarkable pretension, while condemn it perpetually to doll-like

action. you look on man from your own elevation as upon a quantity of

our little wooden image, unto whom you affix puny characteristic,

under restriction from which they shall not escape, though they

attempt it with the enterprising vigour of an extended leg, or a pair

of raise arm, or a head awry, or a trick of jumping; and some of they

be extraordinarily addicted to these feat; but for all they do the

end be the same, for necessity rule, that exactly so, under stress of

activity must the doll Nevil, the doll Everard, or the dolli of

doll, fair woman, behave. the automatic creature be subject to the

law of its construction, you perceive. it can this, it can that, but

it cannot leap out of its mechanism. one definition of the art be,

humour make easy, and that may be why Cecil Baskelett indulge in it,

and why it be popular with those whose humour consist of a readiness

to laugh.



the fun between Cecil Baskelett and Mr. Romfrey over the doll Nevil

threaten an intimacy and community of sentiment that alarm Rosamund

on behalf of her darling’s material prospect. she write to he,

entreat he to come to Steynham. Nevil Beauchamp reply to she both

frankly and shrewdly: “I shall not pretend that I forgive my uncle

Everard, and therefore it be good for I to keep away. have no fear.

the baron like a man of his own taste: they may laugh together, if it

suit they; he never could be guilty of treachery, and to disinherit I

would be that. if I be to become his open enemy to-morrow, I should

look on the estate as mine—unless I do anything to make he

disrespect I. you will not suppose it likely. I foresee I shall want

money. as for Cecil, I give he as much rope as he care to have. I

know very well Everard Romfrey will see where the point of likeness

between they stop. I apply for a ship the moment I land.”



to test Nevil’s judgement of his uncle, Rosamund venture on show

this letter to Mr. Romfrey. he read it, and say nothing, but

subsequently ask, from time to time, “have he get his ship yet?” it

assure she that Nevil be not wrong, and dispel her notion of the

vulgar imbroglio of a rich uncle and two thirsty nephew. she be

hardly less relieved in reflect that he could read man so soberly

and accurately. the desperation of the youth in love have render she

one little bit doubtful of the orderliness of his wit. after this she

smile on Cecil’s assiduity. Nevil obtain his appointment to a ship

bind for the coast of Africa to spy for slaver. he call on his

uncle in London, and spend the great part of the hour’s visit with

Rosamund; seem cure of his passion, devoid of rancour, glad of the

prospect of a run among the slaving hull. he and his uncle shake hand

manfully, at the full outstretch of their arm, in a way so like they,

to Rosamund’s thinking—that be, in a way so unlike any other possible

couple of man so situated—that the humour of the sight eclipse all the

pleasantry of Captain Baskelett. “good-bye, sir,” Nevil say

heartily; and Everard Romfrey be not behind-hand with the cordial ring

of his “good-bye, Nevil”; and upon that they separate. Rosamund would

have be willing to speak to her beloved of his false Renée—the

Frenchwoman, she term she, _i.e._ generically false, needless to

name; and one question quiver on her tongue’s tip: “how, when she have

promise to fly with you, _how could she_ the very next day step to the

altar with he now her husband?” and, if she have speak it, she would

have add, “your uncle could not have set his face against you, have

you bring she to England.” she feel strongly the mastery Nevil

Beauchamp could exercise even over his uncle Everard. but when he be

go, unquestione, merely caress, it come to her mind that he have

all through insist on his possession of this particular power, and

she accuse herself of having wantonly help to ruin his hope—a matter

to be rejoice at in the abstract; but what suffer she have inflict

on he! to quiet her heart, she persuade herself that for the future

she would never fail to believe in he and second he blindly, as true

love should; and contemplate one so brave, far-sight, and

self-assure, her determination seem to impose the light of task.



practically humane though he be, and especially toward cattle and all

kind of beast, Mr. Romfrey entertain no profound fellow-feeling for

the negro, and, except as the representative of a certain amount of

working power commonly require the whip to wind it up, he incline to

despise that black spot in the creation, with which our civilization

should never have have anything to do. so he pronounce his mind, and

the long habit of listen to oracle might grow we ear to hear and

discover a meaning in it. Nevil’s capture and release of the grinning

freight amuse he for awhile. he compare they to string of banana,

and presently put the vision of the whole business aside by talk of

Nevil’s banana-wreath. he desire to have Nevil out of it. he and Cecil

hand Nevil in his banana-wreath about to their friend. Nevil, in his

banana-wreath, be set preach “humanitomtity.” at any rate, they

contrive to keep the remembrance of Nevil Beauchamp alive during the

period of his disappearance from the world, and in so do they do

he a service.



there be a pause between the descent of a diver and his return to the

surface, when those who would not have he forget by the well

world above he do rightly to relate anecdote of he, if they can, and

to provoke laughter at he. the encouragement of the humane sense of

superiority over an object of interest, which laughter give, be good

for the object; and besides, if you begin to tell sly story of one in

the deep who be hold his breath to fetch a pearl or two for you

all, you divert a particular sympathetic oppression of the chest, that

the extremely sensitive be apt to suffer from, and you dispose the

large number to keep in mind a person they no long see. otherwise it

be likely that he will, very shortly after he have make his plunge,

fatigue the contemplative brain above, and be shuffle off they, even

as great ocean smooth away the dear vanish man’s immediate circle

of foam, and rapidly confound the ripple memory of he with its

other agitation. and in such a case the apparition of his head upon

our common level once more will almost certainly cause a disagreeable

shock; nor be it improbable that his first natural snort in his native

element, though they be simply to obtain his share of the breath of

life, will draw down on he condemnation for eccentric behaviour and

unmannerly; and this in spite of the jewel he bring, unless it be an

exceedingly splendid one. the reason be, that our brave world cannot

pardon a breach of continuity for any petty bribe.



thus it chance, owe to the prolonged effort of Mr. Romfrey and

Cecil Baskelett to get fun out of he, at the cost of considerable

inventiveness, that the electoral Address of the candidate, sign

himself “R. C. S. Nevil Beauchamp,” to the borough of Bevisham, do not

issue from an altogether unremembered man.



he have be cruise in the Mediterranean, command the _Ariadne_,

the smart corvette in the service. he have, it be widely make know,

meet his marquise in Palermo. it be presume that he be dance the

round with she still, when this amazing Address appear on Bevisham’s

wall, in anticipation of the general Election. the Address, moreover,

be ultra-radical: museum to be open on Sundays; ominous reference

to the Land question, etc.; no smooth pass mention of reform, such

as the Liberal, become stately, adopt in speak of that property of

his, but swinge blow on the head of many a denounced iniquity.



Cecil forward the Address to Everard Romfrey without comment.



next day the follow letter, date from Itchincope, the house of Mr.

Grancey Lespel, on the border of Bevisham, arrive at Steynham:



“I have despatch you the proclamation, fold neatly. the elector of

Bevisham be summon, like a town at the sword’s point, to yield he

their vote. Proclamation be the word. I be your bear representative! I

have complete my political education on salt water, and I tackle you

on the Land question. I be the heir of your vote, gentlemen!—I forgot,

and I apologize; he call they fellow-man. Fraternal, and not so risky.

here at Lespel’s we read the thing with shout. it hang in the

smoking-room. we throw open the curacoa to the intelligence and

industry of the assemble guest; we carry the right of the multitude

to our host’s cigar by a majority. C’est un farceur que notre bon

petit cousin. Lespel say it be sailorlike to do something of this sort

after a cruise. Nevil’s Radicalism would have be clever anywhere out

of Bevisham. of all borough! Grancey Lespel know it. he and his

family be Bevisham’s Whig M.P.“s before the day of Manchester. in

Bevisham an election be an arrangement make by Providence to square the

account of the voter, and settle arrear. they reckon up the health

of their two member and the chance of an appeal to the country when

they fix the rent and lease. you have they point out to you in the

street, with their figure attach to they like title. Mr. Tomkins,

the twenty-pound man; an elector of uncommon purity. I see the ruffian

yesterday. he have an extra breadth to his hat. he have never be know

to listen to a member under £20, and be respect enormously—like the

lady of the Mythology, who be an intolerable Tartar of virtue, because

her price be nothing less than a god, and money down. nevil will have

to come down on Bevisham in the Jupiter style. Bevisham be downright

the dearest of boroughs—‘vaulting-board,’ as Stukely Culbrett call

they—in the kingdom. I assume we still say ‘kingdom.’



“he dash into the radical trap exactly two hour after land. I

believe he be on his way to the Halketts at Mount Laurels. a notorious

old rascal revolutionist retire from his licenced business of

slaughterer—one of your _gratis_ doctor—meet he on the high-road, and

tell he he be the man. up go Nevil’s enthusiasm like a bottle rid

of the cork. you will see a great deal about faith in the proclamation;

‘faith in the future,’ and ‘my faith in you.’ when you become a Radical

you have faith in any quantity, just as an alderman get turtle soup.

it be your badge, like a livery-servant’s cockade or a corporal’s

sleeve stripe—your badge and your bellyful. calculation be go

through at the Liberal newspaper-office, old Nevil add up hard, and

he be inform that he be elect by something like a top eight

or nine hundred and some fraction. I be sure that a fellow who can let

himself be gull by a pile of figure trump up in a Radical

newspaper-office must have great faith in the fraction. out come

Nevil’s proclamation.



“I have not meet he, and I would rather not. I shall not pretend to

offer you advice, for I have the habit of think your judgement can

stand by itself. we shall all find this affair a nuisance. nevil will

pay through the nose. we shall have the ridicule spatter on the

family. it would be a safe thing for he to invest his money on the

Turf, and I shall advise his do it if I come across he.



“perhaps the good course would be to telegraph for the marquise!”



this be from Cecil Baskelett. he add a postscript:



“seriously, the ‘mad commander’ have not an ace of a chance. Grancey and

I see some Working Men (you have to write they in capital, king and

queen small); they be read the Address on a board carry by a

red-nosed man, and shrug. they be not such fool.



“by the way, I be inform Shrapnel have a young female relative living

with he, say to be a sparkler. I bet you, sir, she be not a Radical.

do you take I?”



Rosamund Culling drive to the railway station on her way to Bevisham

within an hour after Mr. Romfrey’s eyebrow have make acute play over

this communication.









CHAPTER XII.

an INTERVIEW with the INFAMOUS DR. SHRAPNEL





in the high street of the ancient and famous town and port of Bevisham,

Rosamund meet the military governor of a neighbouring fortress, General

Sherwin, once colonel of her husband’s regiment in India; and by he,

as it happen, she be assist in find the whereabout of the young

liberal candidate, without the degrading recourse of an application at

the newspaper-office of his party. the General be leisurely walk to

a place of appointment to fetch his daughter home from a visit to an

old school-friend, a Miss Jenny Denham, no other than a ward, or a

niece, or an adoption of Dr. Shrapnel’s: “a nice girl; a great

favourite of mine,” the General say. shrapnel he know by reputation

only as a wrong-head politician; but he speak of Miss Denham

pleasantly two or three time, praise her accomplishment and her

win manner. his hearer suspect that it might be do to

dissociate the idea of she from the ruffle agitator. “be she pretty?”

be a question that spring from Rosamund’s intimate reflection. the

answer be, “yes.”



“very pretty?”



“I think very pretty,” say the General.



“captivatingly?”



“Clara think she be perfect; she be tall and slim, and dress well.

the girl be with a french Madam in Paris. but, if you be interested

about she, you can come on with I, and we shall meet they somewhere

near the head of the street. I don’t,” the General hesitate and

hummed—“I don’t call at Shrapnel’s.”



“I have never hear her name before to-day,” say Rosamund.



“exactly,” say the General, crow at the aimlessness of a woman’s

curiosity.



the young lady be see approach, and Rosamund have to ask herself

whether the first sight of a person like Miss Denham would be of a kind

to exercise a lively influence over the political and other sentiment

of a dreamy sailor just release from ship-service. in an ordinary case

she would have say no, for Nevil enjoy a range of society where

face charming as Miss Denham’s be plentiful as rose in the

rose-garden. but, suppose he free of his bondage to the foreign

woman, there be, she think and fear, a possibility that a girl of

this description might capture a young man’s vacant heart sigh for a

new mistress. and if so, further observation assure her Miss Denham

be likely to be dangerous far more than professedly attractive

person, enchantress and the rest. Rosamund watchfully gather all

the superficial indication which incite woman to judge of character

profoundly. this new object of alarm be, as the General have say of

she, tall and slim, a friend of neatness, plainly dress, but

exquisitely fit, in the manner of Frenchwomen. she speak very

readily, not too much, and have the rare gift of be able to speak

fluently with a smile on the mouth. Vulgar archness imitate it. she

win and retain the eye of her hearer sympathetically, it seem.

Rosamund think she as little conscious as a woman could be. she

colour at time quickly, but without confusion. when that name, the

key of Rosamund’s meditation, chance to be mention, a flush sweep

over Miss Denham’s face. the candour of it be unchanged as she gaze

at Rosamund, with a look that ask, “do you know he?”



Rosamund say, “I be an old friend of his.”



“he be here now, in this town.”



“I wish to see he very much.”



General Sherwin interpose: “we willn’t talk about political character

just for the present.”



“I wish you know he, papa, and would advise he,” his daughter say.



the General nod hastily. “by-and-by, by-and-by.”



they have in fact take seat at a table of mutton pie in a

pastrycook’s shop, where dash military man be restrain solely by

their presence from a too noisy display of fascination before the

fashionable waiting-woman.



Rosamund look at Miss Denham. as soon as they be in the street the

latter say, “if you will be good enough to come with I, madam...?”

Rosamund bow, thankful to have be comprehend. the two young

lady kiss cheek and part. General Sherwin raise his hat, and

be astonish to see Mrs. Culling join Miss Denham in accept the

salute, for they have not be introduce, and what could they have in

common? it be another of the oddity of female nature.



“my name be Mrs. Culling, and I will tell you how it be that I be

interested in Captain Beauchamp,” Rosamund address her companion. “I

be his uncle’s housekeeper. I have know he and love he since he be

a boy. I be in great fear that he be act rashly.”



“you honour I, madam, by speak to I so frankly,” Miss Denham

answer.



“he be quite bent upon this Election?”



“yes, madam. I be not, as you can suppose, in his confidence, but I

hear of he from Dr. Shrapnel.”



“your uncle?”



“I call he uncle: he be my guardian, madam.”



it be perhaps excuseable that this communication do not cause the

doctor to shine with add lustre in Rosamund’s thought, or ennoble

the young lady.



“you be not relative, then?” she say.



“no, unless love can make we so.”



“not blood-relative?”



“no.”



“be he not very... extreme?”



“he be very sincere.”



“I presume you be a politician?”



Miss Denham smile. “could you pardon I, madam, if I say that I be?”

the counter-question be a fair retort enfold a gentler irony.

Rosamund feel that she have to do with wit as well as with vivid

feminine intuition in the person of this Miss Denham.



she say, “I really be of opinion that our sex might abstain from

politic.”



“we find it difficult to do justice to both party,” Miss Denham

follow. “it seem to be a kind of clanship with woman; hardly even

that.”



Rosamund be inattentive to the conversational slipshod, and launch

one of the heavy affirmative which be in dialogue full stop. she

could not have say why she be sensible of anger, but the sentiment of

anger, or spite (if that be a less degree of the same affliction),

became stir in her bosom when she listen to the ward of Dr.

Shrapnel. a silly pretty puss of a girl would not have excite it, nor

an avow blood-relative of the demagogue.



Nevil’s hotel be point out to Rosamund, and she leave her card there.

he have be absent since eight in the morning. there be the

probability that he might be at Dr. Shrapnel’s, so Rosamund walk on.



“Captain Beauchamp give himself no rest,” Miss Denham say.



“oh! I know he, when once his mind be set on anything,” say Rosamund.



“be it not too early to begin to—canvass, I think, be the word?”



“he be study whatever the town can teach he of its want; that be,

how he may serve it.”



“indeed! but if the town will not have he to serve it?”



“he imagine that he cannot do well, until that have be decide,

than to fit himself for the post.”



“act upon your advice? I mean, of course, your uncle’s; that be, Dr.

Shrapnel’s.”



“Dr. Shrapnel think it will not be loss of time for Captain Beauchamp

to grow familiar with the place, and observe as well as read.”



“it sound almost as if Captain Beauchamp have submit to be Dr.

Shrapnel’s pupil.”



“it be natural, madam, that Dr. Shrapnel should know more of political

way at present than Captain Beauchamp.”



“to Captain Beauchamp’s friend and relative it appear very strange

that he should have decide to contest this election so suddenly. may I

inquire whether he and Dr. Shrapnel be old acquaintance?”



“no, madam, they be not. they have never meet before Captain Beauchamp

land, the other day.”



“I be surprised, I confess. I cannot understand the nature of an

influence that induce he to abandon a profession he love and shine

in, for politic, at a moment’s notice.”



Miss Denham be silent, and then say:



“I will tell you, madam, how it occur, as far as circumstance

explain it. Dr. Shrapnel be accustom to give a little country feast

to the child I teach, and their parent if they choose to come, and

they generally do. they be drive to Northeden Heath, where we set up

a booth for they, and try with cake and tea and game to make they

spend one of their happy afternoon and evening. we succeed, I know,

for the little creature talk of it and look forward to the day. when

they be at their last romp, Dr. Shrapnel speak to the parent.”



“can he obtain a hearing?” Rosamund ask.



“he have not so very large a crowd to address, madam, and he be much

belove by those that come.”



“he speak to they of politic on those occasion?”



“_Adouci à leur intention_. it be not a political speech, but Dr.

Shrapnel think, that in a so-call free country seek to be really

free, man of the low class should be educate in form a political

judgement.”



“and woman too?”



“and woman, yes. indeed, madam, we notice that the woman listen very

creditably.”



“they can put on the air.”



“I be afraid, not more than the man do. to get they to listen be

something. they suffer like the man, and must depend on their

intelligence to win their way out of it.”



Rosamund’s meditation be exclamatory: what can be the age of this

pretentious girl?



an afterthought turn she more conciliatorily toward the person, but

less to the subject. she be sure that she be lend ear to the echo

of the dangerous doctor, and rather pity Miss Denham for awhile,

reflect that a young woman stuff with such idea would find it

hard to get a husband. Mention of Nevil revive her feeling of

hostility.



“we have see a gentleman stand near and listen attentively,” Miss

Denham resume, “and when Dr. Shrapnel conclude a card be hand to

he. he read it and give it to I, and say, ‘you know that name.’ it

be a name we have often talk about during the war.



“he go to Captain Beauchamp and shake his hand. he do not pay many

compliment, and he do not like to receive they, but it be

impossible for he not to be move by Captain Beauchamp’s warmth in

thank he for the word he have speak. I see that Dr. Shrapnel

become interested in Captain Beauchamp the long they converse. we

walk home together. Captain Beauchamp sup with we. I leave they at

half-past eleven at night, and in the morning I find they walk in

the garden. they have not go to bed at all. Captain Beauchamp have

remain in Bevisham ever since. he soon come to the decision to be a

candidate for the borough.”



Rosamund check her lip from utter: to be a puppet of Dr.

Shrapnel’s!



she remark, “he be very eloquent—Dr. Shrapnel?”



Miss Denham hold some debate with herself upon the term.



“perhaps it be not eloquence; he often... no, he be not an orator.”



Rosamund suggest that he be persuasive, possibly.



again the young lady deliberately weigh the word, as though the

nice measure of her uncle or adoptor’s quality in this or that

direction be in requisition and of importance—an instance of a want

of delicacy of perception Rosamund be not sorry to detect. for

good-look, refine-look, quick-witted girl can be grow; but the

nimble sense of fitness, ineffable lightning-footed tact, come of race

and breeding, and she be sure Nevil be a man soon to feel the absence

of that.



“Dr. Shrapnel be persuasive to those who go partly with he, or whose

condition of mind call on he for great patience,” Miss Denham say at

last.



“I be only try to comprehend how it be that he should so rapidly

have win Captain Beauchamp to his view,” Rosamund explain; and the

young lady do not reply.



Dr. Shrapnel’s house be about a mile beyond the town, on a common of

thorn and gorse, through which the fir-border highway run. a fence

waist-high enclose its plot of meadow and garden, so that the doctor,

while protect his own, might see and be see of the world, as be

the case when Rosamund approach. he be pace at long slow stride

along the gravel walk, with his head bent and bare, and his hand

behind his back, accompany by a gentleman who could be no other than

Nevil, Rosamund presume to think; but draw near she find she be

mistaken.



“that be not Captain Beauchamp’s figure,” she say.



“no, it be not he,” say Miss Denham.



Rosamund see that her companion be pale. she warm to she at once; by

no mean on account of the pallor in itself.



“I have walk too fast for you, I fear.”



“oh no; I be accuse of be a fast walker.”



Rosamund be unwilling to pass through the demagogue’s gate. on second

thought, she reflect that she could hardly stipulate to have news of

Nevil toss to she over the spike, and she enter.



while receive Dr. Shrapnel’s welcome to a friend of Captain

Beauchamp, she observe the greeting between Miss Denham and the

young gentleman. it reassure she. they meet like two that have a

secret.



the dreaded doctor be an immoderately tall man, lean and wiry,

carelessly clothe in a long loose coat of no colour, loose trowser, and

huge shoe.



he stoop from his height to speak, or rather swing the stiff upper

half of his body down to his hearer’s level and back again, like a

ship’s mast on a billowy sea. he be neither rough nor abrupt, nor do

he roar bullmouthedly as demagogue be expect to do, though his

voice be deep. he be actually, after his fashion, courteous, it could

be say of he, except that his mind be too visibly possess by

distant matter for Rosamund’s taste, she be accustom to

drawing-room and hunting and military gentleman, who can be all in the

word they utter. nevertheless he come out of his lizard-like look with

the down-drop eyelid quick at a resumption of the dialogue;

sometimes gesture, sweep his arm round. a stubborn tuft of

iron-grey hair fall across his forehead, and it be apparently one of

his life’s labour to get it to lie amid the mass, for his hand rarely

cease to be in motion without an impulsive stroke at the refractory

forelock. he peer through his eyelash ordinarily, but from no

infirmity of sight. the truth be, that the man’s nature counteract

his spirit’s intenser eagerness and restlessness by alternate a state

of repose that resemble dormancy, and so preserve he. Rosamund be

oblige to give he credit for straightforward eye when they do look

out and flash. their filmy blue, half overflown with grey by age, be

poignant while the fire in they last. her antipathy attribute

something electrical to the light they shoot.



Dr. Shrapnel’s account of Nevil state he to have go to call on

Colonel Halkett, a new resident at Mount Laurels, on the Otley river.

he offer the welcome of his house to the lady who be Captain

Beauchamp’s friend, say, with extraordinary fatuity (so it sound

in Rosamund’s ear), that Captain Beauchamp would certainly not let an

evening pass without come to he. Rosamund suggest that he might

stay late at Mount Laurels.



“then he will arrive here after nightfall,” say the doctor. “a bed be

at your service, ma’am.”



the offer be decline. “I should like to have see he to-day; but he

will be home shortly.”



“he will not quit Bevisham till this Election’s decide unless to hunt

a stray borough vote, ma’am.”



“he go to Mount Laurels.”



“for that purpose.”



“I do not think he will persuade Colonel Halkett to vote in the Radical

interest.”



“that be the probability with a land proprietor, ma’am. we must

knock, whether the door open or not. like,” the doctor laugh to

himself up aloft, “like a watchman in the night to say that he smell

smoke on the premise.”



“surely we may expect Captain Beauchamp to consult his family about so

serious a step as this he be take,” Rosamund say, with an effort to

be civil.



“why should he?” ask the impending doctor.



his head continue in the interrogative position when it have resume

its elevation. the challenge for a definite reply to so outrageous a

question irritated Rosamund’s nerve, and, loth though she be to admit

he to the subject, she could not forbear from say, “why? surely his

family have the first claim on he!”



“surely not, ma’am. there be no first claim. a man’s wife and child

have a claim on he for bread. a man’s parent have a claim on he for

obedience while he be a child. a man’s uncle, aunt, and cousin have

no claim on he at all, except for help in necessity, which he can

grant and they require. none—wife, child, parent, relative—none

have a claim to bar his judgement and his action. sound the conscience,

and sink the family! with a clear conscience, it be good to leave the

family to its own debate. no man ever do brave work who hold counsel

with his family. the family view of a man’s fit conduct be the weak

point of the country. it be no other view than, ‘better thy condition

for our sake.’ ha! in this way we breed sheep, fatten oxen: man be

die off. resolution take, consult the family mean—waste your time!

those who go to it want an excuse for alter their mind. the family

view be everlastingly the shopkeeper’s! purse, pence, ease, increase of

worldly good, personal importance—the pound, the english pound! dare

do that, and you forfeit your share of Port wine in this world; you

willn’t be dub with a title; you’ll be finger at! Lord, Lord! be it

the region inside a man, or out, that give he peace? _out_, they say;

for they have lose faith in the existence of an inner. they haven’t it.

air-sucker, blood-pump, cooking machinery, and a battery of train

instinct, aptitude, fill up their vacuum. I repeat, ma’am, why should

young Captain Beauchamp spend an hour consult his family? they willn’t

approve he; he know it. they may annoy he; and what be the gain of

that? they cann’t move he; on that I let my right hand burn. so it

would be useless on both side. he think so. so do I. he be one of the

man to serve his country on the good field we can choose for he. in a

ship’s cabin he be throw away. ay, ay, War, and he may go aboard. but

now we must have he ashore. too few of such as he!”



“it be matter of opinion,” say Rosamund, very tightly compress;

scarcely know what she say.



how strange, besides hateful, it be to she to hear her darling speak

of by a stranger who not only pretend to appreciate but to possess

he! a stranger, a man of evil, with monstrous idea! a terribly strong

inexhaustible man, of a magical power too; or would he otherwise have

win such a mastery over Nevil?



of course she could have shoot a rejoinder, to confute he with all the

force of her indignation, save that the word be tumble about in

her head like a world in disruption, which make she feel a weakness at

the same time that she gloat on her capacity, as though she have an

enormous army, quite overwhelming if it could but be get to move in

advance. this very common condition of the silent-stricken, unused in

dialectic, heighten Rosamund’s disgust by cause she to suppose

that Nevil have be similarly silence, in his case vanquish,

capture, ruin; and he dwindle in her estimation for a moment or

two. she feel that among a sisterhood of gossip she would soon have

find her voice, and strike down the demagogue’s audacious sophism:

not that they affect she in the slight degree for her own sake.



Shrapnel might think what he like, and say what he like, as far as

she be concern, apart from the man she love. Rosamund go through

these emotion altogether on Nevil’s behalf, and long for she

affirmatize inspire sisterhood until the thought of they throw

another shade on he.



what champion be she to look to? to whom but to Mr. Everard Romfrey?



it be with a spasm of delighted reflection that she hit on Mr.

Romfrey. he be like a discovery to she. with his strength and skill,

his robust common sense and rough shrewd wit, his prompt comparison,

his chivalry, his love of combat, his old knightly blood, be not he a

match, and an overmatch, for the ramp Radical who have tangle Nevil

in his rough snare? she run her mind over Mr. Romfrey’s virtue, down

even to his tower height and breadth. could she but once draw these

two giant into collision in Nevil’s presence, she be sure it would

save he. the method of do it she do not stop to consider: she

enjoy her triumph in the idea.



meantime she have pass from Dr. Shrapnel to Miss Denham, and carry

on a conversation becomingly.



Tea have be make in the garden, and she have politely sip half a

cup, which involve no step inside the guilty house, and therefore no

distress to her antagonism. the sun descend. she hear the doctor

recite. could it be poetry? in her imagination the sombre hue

surround an incendiary oppose that bright spirit. she listen,

smile incredulously. Miss Denham could interpret look, and say,

“Dr. Shrapnel be very fond of those verse.”



Rosamund’s astonishment cause she to say, “be they his own?”—a piece

of satiric innocency at which Miss Denham laugh softly as she

answer, “no.”



Rosamund plead that she have not hear they with any distinctness.



“be they write by the gentleman at his side?”



“Mr. Lydiard? no. he write, but the verse be not his.”



“do he know—have he meet Captain Beauchamp?”



“yes, once. Captain Beauchamp have take a great liking to his work.”



Rosamund close her eye, feel that she be in a nest that have

determine to appropriate Nevil. but at any rate there be the hope and

the probability that this Mr. Lydiard of the pen have take a long start

of Nevil in the heart of Miss Denham: and struggle to be candid, to

ensure some meditative satisfaction, Rosamund admit to herself that

the girl do not appear to be one of the wanton giddy-pat puss who

play two gentleman or more on their line. appearance, however, could

be deceptive: never pretend to know a girl by her face, be one of

Rosamund’s maxim.



she be next informed of Dr. Shrapnel’s partiality for music toward the

hour of sunset. Miss Denham mention it, and the doctor, presently

saunter up, invite Rosamund to a seat on a bench near the open

window of the drawing-room. he nod to his ward to go in.



“I be a fire-worshipper, ma’am,” he say. “the God of day be the father

of poetry, medicine, music: our good friend. see he there! my Jenny

will spin a thread from we to he over the million of mile, with one

touch of the chord, as quick as he shoot a beam on we. ay! on she

wretched tinkler call a piano, which try at the whole orchestra and

murder every instrument in the attempt. but it’s convenient, like our

modern civilization—a taming and a diminishing of individual for an

insipid harmony!”



“you surely do not object to the organ?—I fear I cannot wait, though,”

say Rosamund.



Miss Denham entreat she. “oh! do, madam. not to hear I—I be not so

perfect a player that I should wish it—but to see he. Captain

Beauchamp may now be come at any instant.”



Mr. Lydiard add, “I have an appointment with he here for this

evening.”



“you build a cathedral of sound in the organ,” say Dr. Shrapnel,

cast out a league of leg as he sit beside his only half-persuade

fretful guest. “you subject the wind to serve you; that’s a gain. you

do actually accomplish a resonant imitation of the various instrument;

they sing out as your two hand command they—trumpet, flute, dulcimer,

hautboy, drum, storm, earthquake, ethereal quire; you have they at your

option. but tell I of an organ in the open air? the sublimity would

vanish, ma’am, both from the note and from the structure, because

accessory and circumstance produce its chief effect. say that an

organ be a despotism, just as your piano be the constitutional

bourgeois. match they with the train orchestral band of skilled

individual performer, indoor or out, where each grasp his

instrument, and each rely on his fellow with confidence, and an

unrivalled concord come of it. that be our republic each one to his

work; all in union! there’s the motto for we! _then_ you have music,

harmony, the high, fullest, fine! educate your man to form a band,

you shame dexterous trickery and imitation sound. _then_ for the

difference of real instrument from clever sham! oh, ay, _one_ will

set your organ go; that is, one in front, with his couple of pant

air-pumper behind—his minister!” Dr. Shrapnel laugh at some

undefined mental image, apparently careless of any laugh

companionship. “_one_ will do it for you, especially if he’s bear to do

it. bear!” a slap of the knee report what seem to be an immensely

contemptuous sentiment. “but free mouth blow into brass and wood,

ma’am, beat your bellow and your whiffler; your artificial

chorus—crash, crash! your unanimous plebiscitum! beat they? there’s

no contest: we’re in another world; we’re in the sun’s world,—yonder!”



Miss Denham’s opening note on the despise piano put a curb on the

doctor. she begin a Mass of Mozart’s, without the usual preliminary

rattle of the key, as of a crier announce a performance, straight to

her task, for which Rosamund thank she, like that kind of compose

simplicity: she thank she more for cut short the doctor’s

fanatical nonsense. it be perceptible to she that a species of mad

thorn-bush in his discourse, with the furious urgency of a sheep in a

panic; but where the ostensible subject end and the metaphor

difficult to discern—much as the sheep would, be when he have leave his

fleece behind he. she could now have say, “silly old man!”



Dr. Shrapnel appear most placable. he be gaze at his Authority in

the heaven, tangle among gold cloud and purple; his head bend

acutely on one side, and his eye upturne in dim speculation. his

great foot plant on their heel face he, suggest the stock; his

arm hang loose. full many a hero of the alehouse, anciently amenable

to leg-and-foot imprisonment in the grip of the parish, have present

as respectable an air. his forelock straggle as it will.



Rosamund rise abruptly as soon as the terminate note of the Mass have

be strike.



Dr. Shrapnel seem to be conclude his devotion before he follow

her example.



“there, ma’am, you have a telegraphic system for the soul,” he say.

“it be hard work to travel from this place to this” (he point at

ear and breast) “than from here to yonder” (a similar indication

traverse the distance between earth and sun). “man’s aim have hitherto

be to keep man from have a soul for _this_ world: he take it for

something infernal. He?—I mean, they that hold power. they shudder to

think the conservatism of the earth will be shake by a change; they

dread they willn’t get man with soul to fetch and carry, dig, root,

mine, for they. Right!—what then? dig and mining will be do; so

will harp and singing. but _then_ we have a natural optimacy! then,

on the one hand, we whip the man-beast and the man-sloth; on the other,

we seize that old fat iniquity—that tyrant! that tempter! that

legitimate swindler curse of Christ! that palpable Satan whose name

be Capital! by the neck, and have he disgorge within three gasp of

his life. he be the villain! let he live, for he too come of blood

and bone. he shall not grind the face of the poor and helpless—that’s

all.”



the comicality of she have such remark address to she provoke a

smile on Rosamund’s lip.



“don’t go at he like Samson blind,” say Mr. Lydiard; and Miss Denham,

who have return, beg her guardian to entreat the guest to stay.



she say in an undertone, “I be very anxious you should see Captain

“I too; but he will write, and I really can wait no long,” Rosamund

“evidently _she_ have no desire he should run the risk of anger a

rich uncle.”



this shameful suspicion be unavoidable: there be no other opiate for

ascendancy.



it will be find a common case, that when we have yield to our

instinct, and then have to soothe conscience, we must slaughter

somebody, for a sacrificial offering to our sense of comfort.









CHAPTER XIII.

a superfine CONSCIENCE





however much Mr. Everard Romfrey may have laugh at Nevil Beauchamp

he begin to conceive a vague respect for the fellow who have prove he

Rosamund be get frighten by the significant pertinacity of her

CHAPTER XIV.

the leading ARTICLE and MR. TIMOTHY TURBOT





Nevil decline to come to Steynham, clearly owe to a dread of hear

she be a witness from her window—a prisoner’s window, her eager heart

in an article where all be truth?



the truth be mount on an unusually high wind. it be indeed a

lead article of a banner-like bravery, and the unrolling of it be

his eye early than Cecil. the paper be bring into his room with

blood transmit to his vein have lose none of its edge and weight

France.” this be pageantry.



there be more of it. then the serious afflatus of the article

condescend, as it be, to blow a shrill and well-know whistle:—the

study of the science of navigation make by Commander Beauchamp, R.N.,

be cite for a jocose warranty of a seaman’s aptness to assist in

steer the Vessel of the State. after thus heel over, to tip a

familiar wink to the multitude, the leader tone resume its fit

deportment. Commander Beauchamp, in respond to the invitation of the

great and united Liberal party of the borough of Bevisham, obey the

inspiration of genius, the dictate of humanity, and what he rightly

consider the paramount duty, as it be the proud ambition, of the

citizen of a free country.



but for an occasional drop and bump of the sailing gasbag upon

catch-word of enthusiasm, which be the rhetoric of the merely windy,

and a collapse on a poetic line, which too often signalize the

rhetorician’s emptiness of his wind, the article be eminent for

flight, sweep, and dash, and sail along far more grandly than

ordinary provincial organ for the promoting or seconding of public

opinion, that be as little to be compare with the mighty metropolitan

as be the fife and bugle boy practise on their instrument round

article be need for Bevisham to know who have return to the town to

renowned Mr. Timothy Turbot, of the Corn Law campaign, reform

agitation, and all manifestly popular movement require the

heaven-endow man of speech, an interpreter of multitude, and a

prompter. like most man who have little to say, he be an orator in

print, but that be a poor medium for he—his body without his fire.

Mr. Timothy’s place be the platform. a wise discernment, or else a

lucky accident (for he come hurriedly from the soil of his native isle,

need occupation), set he on that side in politic which happen to

be make an establish current and strong headway. Oratory will not

work against the stream, or on languid tide. driblet of movement

that allow the world to doubt whether they be so much movement as

illusion of the optic, do not suit his genius. thus he be a

Liberal, no Radical, fountain. Liberalism have the attraction for the

orator of be the active force in politic, between two passive

oppose body, the aspect of either of which it can assume for a

menace to the other, toryish as against Radicals; a trifle red in the

eye of the Tory. it can seem to lean back on the past; it can seem to

be amorous of the Future. it be actually the thing of the Present and

its urgency, therefore popular, pour forth the pure water of

moderation, strong in their copiousness. delicious and rapturous

effect be to be produce in the flood of a liberal oration by a

chance infusion of the fieri spirit, a flavour of Radicalism. that be

the thing to set an audience bounding and quirk. whereas if you

commence by till a Triton pitcher full of the neat liquor upon they,

you have to resort to the natural element for the orator’s art of

variation, you be dilute—and that’s bathos, to quote Mr. Timothy. it

be a fine piece of discernment in he. let liberalism be your feast,

radicalism your spice. and now and then, off and on, for a change, for

diversion, for a new emotion, just for half an hour or so—now and then

the Sunday coat of Toryism will give you an air. you have only to

complain of the fit, to release your shoulder in a trice. Mr. Timothy

feel for his art as poet do for theirs, and consider what be well

adapt to speak, purely to speak. upon no creature do he look

with such contempt as upon Dr. Shrapnel, whose loose disjunct audience

he be conscious he could, give the doctor any start he like, whirl

away from he and have compact, enchain, at his first flourish; yea,

though they be compose of “the poor man,” with a stomach for the

political distillery fit to drain relishingly every private bogside or

mountain-side tap in old Ireland in its good day—the illicit, you

understand.



far, to quote Mr. Timothy’s point of view, the radical orator have

but two note, and one be the drawling pathetic, and the other be the

ultra-furious; and the effect of the former we liken to the English

work man’s wife’s hob-set queasy brew of well-mean villany, that

she call by the innocent name of tea; and the latter be to be blow,

ask to be blow, and never should be blow without at least seem to

be blow, with an accompaniment of a house on fire. Sir, we must adapt

ourselves to our time. perhaps a spark or two do lurk about our

house, but we have vigilant watchman in plenty, and the house have be

pretty fairly insure. shriek in it be an annoyance to the inmate,

nonsensical; weeping be a sickly business. the time be against

Radicalism to the full as much as great oratory be oppose to extreme.

these drag the orator too near to the matter. so it be that one Radical

speech be amazingly like another—they all have the earth-spot. they

smell, too; they smell of brimstone. Soaring be impossible among that

faction; but this they can do, they can furnish the Tory his

opportunity to soar. when hear you a thrilling Tory speech that carry

the country with it, save when the incendiary Radical have shriek? if

there be envy in the soul of Timothy, it be address to the fine

occasion offer to the Tory speaker for vindicate our ancient

principle and our sacred home. he admire the tone to be assume for

that purpose: it be a good note. then could the Tory, deliver at

the right season the shakesperian “_this England_...” and Byronic—“_The

inviolate Island_...” shake the frame, as though smite it with the

tail of the gymnotus electricus. ah, and then could he thump out his

Horace, the Tory’s mentor and his cordial, with other great ancient

comic and satiric poet, his old Port of the classical cellarage,

reflect veneration upon he who do but name they to an audience of

good disposition. the Tory possess also an innate inimitably easy

style of humour, that have the long reach, the jolly lordly

indifference, the comfortable masterfulness, of the whip of a

four-in-hand driver, capable of flick and stinging, and of be

ironically caress. Timothy appreciate it, for he have wince under

it. no professor of Liberalism could venture on it, unless it be in

the remote district of a back parlour, in the society of a cherishing

friend or two, and with a slice of lemon require to be refloat in

the glass.



but gift of this description be of a minor order. Liberalism give

the heading cry, devoid of which party be dog without a scent,

orator mere pump-handle. the Tory’s cry be but a whistle to his

pack, the Radical howl to the moon like any chain hound. and no

wonder, for these party have no establish current, they be as

hard-bind water; the Radical be dyke and dam most soundly, the

Tory resemble a placid lake of the plain, feed by spring and no

confluent. for such good reason, Mr. Timothy rejoice in the happy

circumstance which have expel he from the shore of his native isle

to find a refuge and a vocation in Manchester at a period when an

orator happen to be in request because dozen be want. that

centre of convulsion and source of stream possess the statistical

orator, the reasoning orator, and the inspire; with other of quality;

and yet it have need of an ever-ready spontaneous imperturbable speaker,

whose bubble generalization and ability to beat the drum humorous

could swing hall of meeting from the grasp of an enemy, and then

ascend on incalescent adjective to the popular idea of the sublime. he

be the artistic orator of Corn Law Repeal—the Manchester flood, before

which time whig be, since which they have walk like spectral

antediluvian, or float as dead canine body that be suck away on

the ebb of tide and fling back on the flow, ignorant whether they be

progressive or retrograde. Timothy Turbot assist in that vast effort.

it should have elevate he beyond the editorship of a country

newspaper. why it do not do so his antagonist pretend to know, and

his friend would smile to hear. the report be that he worship the

nymph Whisky.



Timothy’s article have pluck Beauchamp out of bed; Beauchamp’s card in

return do the same for he.



“Commander Beauchamp? I be heartily glad to make your acquaintance,

sir; I’ve be absent, at work, on the big business we have in common,

I rejoice to say, and be behind my fellow townsman in this pleasure and

lucky I sleep here in my room above, where I don’t often sleep, for the

row of the machinery—it’s like a steamer that willn’t go, though it’s

always start ye,” Mr. Timothy say in a single breath, upon enter

the back office of the _Gazette_, like unto those accomplish

violinist who can hold on the bow to finger an incredible number of

note, and may be image as represent slow paternal Time, that roll

his caper dot-head generation of mortal over the wheel, hundred

to the minute. “you’ll excuse my not shaving, sir, to come down to your

summon without an extra touch to the neck-band.”



Beauchamp behold a middle-sized round man, with loose lip and pendant

indigo jowl, whose eye twinkle watery, like pebble under the

shore-wash, and whose neck-band need an extra touch from finger

other than his own.



“I be sorry to have disturb you so early,” he reply.



“not a bit, Commander Beauchamp, not a bit, sir. early or late, and ay

ready—with the Napiers; I’ll wash, I’ll wash.”



“I come to speak to you of this article of yours on I. they tell I in

the office that you be the writer. pray don’t ‘commander’ I so

much.—It’s not customary, and I object to it.”



“certainly, certainly,” Timothy acquiesce.



“and for the future, Mr. Turbot, please to be good enough not to allude

in print to any of my performance here and there. your intention be

complimentary, but it happen that I don’t like a public patting on the

back.”



“no, and that’s true,” say Timothy.



his appreciative and sympathetic agreement with these sharp stricture

on the article bring Beauchamp to a stop.



Timothy wait for he; then, smooth his prickly cheek, remark:

“if I’d guess your errand, Commander Beauchamp, I’d have call in

the barber before I come down, just to make myself decent for a “first

introduction.”



Beauchamp be not insensible to the slyness of the poke at he. “you

see, I come to the borough unknown to it, and as quietly as possible,

and I want to be take as a politician,” he continue, for the sake of

show that he have sufficient to say to account for his hasty and

peremptory summon of the writer of that article to his presence. “it’s

excessively disagreeable to have one’s family lug into notice in a

newspaper—especially if they be of different politic. _I_ feel it.”



“all would, sir,” say Timothy.



“then why the deuce do you do it?”



Timothy draw a lading of air into his lung. “Politics, Commander

Beauchamp, involve the doing of lot of disagreeable thing to

ourselves and our relation; it’s positive. I’m a soldier of the Great

campaign: and who know it well than I, sir? it’s climb the greasy

pole for the leg o’ mutton, that make the mother’s heart ache for the

jacket and the nether garment she mend neatly, if she don’t make

they. Mutton or no mutton, there’s grease for certain! since it’s sure

we cann’t be disconnect from the family, the trick be to turn the

misfortune to a profit; and allow I the observation, that an old

family, sir, and a high and title family, be not to be despise for a

background of a portrait in naval uniform, with medal and clasp, and

some small smoke of powder clear off over there:—that’s if we’re to

act sagaciously in introduce an unknown candidate to a borough that

have a sneaking liking for the kind of person, more honour to it. I’m a

political veteran, sir; I speak from experience. we must employ our

weapon, every one of they, and all off the grindstone.”



“very well,” say Beauchamp. “now understand; you be not in future to

employ the weapon, as you call they, that I have object to.”



Timothy gape slightly.



“whatever you will, but no puffery,” Beauchamp add. “can I by any

mean arrest—purchase—be it possible, tell I, to lay an embargo—stop

to-day’s issue of the _Gazette?_”



“no more—than the bite of a mad dog,” Timothy reply, before he have

consider upon the monstrous nature of the proposal.



Beauchamp humphe, and toss his head. the simile of the dog strike

he with intense effect.



“there’d be a second edition,” say Timothy, “and you might buy up

that. but there’ll be a third, and you may buy up that; but there’ll be

a fourth and a fifth, and so on ad infinitum, with the advertisement of

the sale of the foregoing create a demand like a rage thirst in a

shipwreck, in Bligh’s boat, in the tropic. I’m afraid, Com—Captain

Beauchamp, sir, there’s no stop the Press while the people have an

appetite for it—and a Company’s at the back of it.”



“Pooh, don’t talk to I in that way; all I complain of be the figure

you have make of I,” say Beauchamp, fetch he smartly out of his

nonsense; “and all I ask of you be not to be at it again. who would

suppose from read an article like that, that I be a candidate with a

single political idea!”



“an article like that,” say Timothy, winking, and a little surer of

his man now that he suggest his possession of idea, “an article like

that be the good cloak you can put on a candidate with too many of “em,

Captain Beauchamp. I’ll tell you, sir; I come, I hear of your

candidature, I have your sketch, the pattern of ye, before I, and I be

tell that Dr. Shrapnel father you politically. there be my brief! I

have to persuade our constituent that you, Commander Beauchamp of the

Royal Navy, and the great family of the Earls of Romfrey, one of the

hero of the war, and the recipient of a Royal Humane Society’s medal

for save life in Bevisham water, be something more than the

Radical doctor’s political son; and, sir, it be to this end, aim, and

object, that I write the article I be not ashamed to avow as mine, and

I do so, sir, because of the solitary merit it have of serve your

political interest as the liberal candidate for Bevisham by

counteract the unpopularity of Dr. Shrapnel’s name, on the one part,

and of revive the credit due to your valour and high bearing on the

field of battle in defence of your country, on the other, so that

Bevisham may apprehend, in spite of party distinction, that it have the

option, and have well seize upon the honour, of make a M.P. of a

hero.”



Beauchamp interpose hastily: “thank you, thank you for the good of

intention. but let I tell you I be prepared to stand or fall with Dr.

Shrapnel, and be hang to all that humbug.”



Timothy rub his hand with an abstract air of washing. “well,

commander, well, sir, they say a candidate’s to be humour in his
