borderland between lustreful significance and the mist? she catch at

the nerve like certain aoristic combination in music, like tone of a

string instrument sweep by the wind, entice, unseizable. yet she

sit there at her father’s foot gaze out into the world indifferent to

spectator, indifferent even to the common sentiment of gracefulness.

her left hand clasp his right, and she support herself on the floor

with the other hand lean away from he, to the destruction of

conventional symmetry in the picture. none but a woman of consummate

breeding dare have do as she do. it be not southern suppleness

that save she from the charge of harsh audacity, but something of the

kind of genius in her mood which have hurry the great poet of sound

and speech to impose their naturalness upon accepted law, or show the

law to have be our meagre limitation.



the writer in this country will, however, be make safest, and the

excellent body of self-appoint thongman, who walk up and down our

rank flap their leathern strap to terrorize we from experiment

in imagery, will well be satisfy, by the statement that she be

indescribable: a term that exact no labour of mind from he or from

they, for it flow off the pen as readily as it fill a vacuum.



that posture of Renée displease Cecilia and fascinate she. in an

exhibition of painting she would have pass by it in pure

displeasure: but here be Nevil’s first love, the woman who love he;

and she be french. after a continue study of her Cecilia’s grow

jealousy betray itself in a conscious rivalry of race, come to the

admission that Englishwomen cannot fle themselves about on the floor

without agonize the grace: possibly, too, they cannot look

singularly without risk in the direction of slyness and brazen

archness; or talk animatedly without dip in slang. conventional

situation preserve they and interchange dignity with they; still life

befit they; pre-eminently that judicial seat from which in brief

speech they deliver their judgement upon their foreign sister.

Jealousy it be that pluck Cecilia from her majestic place and cause

she to envy in Renée thing she would otherwise have disapprove.



at last she have see the french lady’s likeness! the effect of it be a

horrid trouble in Cecilia’s cool blood, abasement, a sense of eclipse,

hardly any sense of deserve worthiness: “what be I but an heiress!”

Nevil have once call she beautiful; his praise have give her beauty.

but what be beauty when it be outshone! ask the owner of gem. you

think they rich; they be pine.



then, too, this Renée, who look electrical in repose, might really

love Nevil with a love that send her heart out to he in his

enterprise, justify and adore he, pierce to the hero in his

very thought. would she not see that his championship of the

unfortunate man Dr. Shrapnel be heroic?



Cecilia surrender the card to Rosamund, and it be out of sight when

Beauchamp step in the drawing-room. his cheek be flush; he have

be one against three for the well part of an hour.



“be you go to show I the down to-morrow morning?” Cecilia say to

he; and he reply, “you will have to be up early.”



“what’s that?” ask the colonel, at Beauchamp’s heel.



he be volunteer to join the party of two for the early morning’s

ride to the down. Mr. Romfrey press his shoulder, say, “there’s

no third horse can do it in my stable.”



Colonel Halkett turn to he.



“I have your promise to come over the kennel with I and see how I

treat a cry of mad dog, which be ninety-nine time out of a hundred mad

fool man,” Mr. Romfrey add.



by that the colonel know he mean to stand by Nevil still and offer he

his chance of win Cecilia.



having pledge his word not to interfere, Colonel Halkett submit,

and mutter, “ah! the kennel.” consider however what he have be

witnessing of Nevil’s behaviour to his uncle, the colonel be amazed at

Mr. Romfrey’s magnanimity in not cut he off and disown he.



“why the down?” he say.



“why the deuce, colonel?” a question quite as reasonable, and Mr.

Romfrey laugh under his breath. to relieve an uncertainty in

Cecilia’s face, that might soon have become confusion, he describe the

down front the paleness of early dawn, and then their arch and

curve and dip against the pearly grey of the half-glow; and then, among

their hollow, lo, the illumination of the East all around, and up and

away, and a gallop for mile along the turfy thymy rolling billow,

land to leave, sea to right, below you. “it’s the near hit to wing

we can make, Cecilia.” he surprise she with her christian name, which

kindle in she the secret of something he expect from that ride on

the down. compare you the Alps with they? if you could jump on the

back of an eagle, you might. the alp have height. but the down have

swiftness. those long stretch line of the down be greyhound in

full career. to look at they be to set the blood racing! speed be on

the down, glorious motion, odorous air of sea and herb, exquisite as

in the isle of Greece. and the Continental travel ninny leave

England for health!—run off and forth from the down to the steamboat,

the railway, the steam hotel, the tourist’s shiver mountain-top,

in search of sensation! there on the down the fine and lively

be at their bidding ready to fly through they like host of angel.



he speak somewhat in that strain, either to relieve Cecilia or prepare

the road for Nevil, not in his ordinary style; on the contrary, with a

swing of enthusiasm that seem to spring of ancient heartfelt

fervour. and indeed soon afterward he be tell she that there on

those down, in full view of Steynham, he and his wife have first join

hand.



Beauchamp sit silent. Mr. Romfrey despatch order to the stable, and

Rosamund to the kitchen. Cecilia be rather dismay by the formal

preparation for the ride. she decline the early cup of coffee. Mr.

Romfrey beg she to take it. “who know the hour when you’ll be

back?” he say. Beauchamp say nothing.



the room grow insufferable to Cecilia. she would have like to be

waft to her chamber in a veil, so shamefully unveiled do she seem to

be. but the french lady would have be happy in her place! her father

kiss she as father do when they hand the bride into the

travel-carriage. his “good-night, my darling!” be in the voice of

a soldier on duty. for a concluding sign that her dim apprehension

point correctly, Mr. Romfrey kiss she on the forehead. she could

not understand how it have come to pass that she find herself suddenly

on this incline, precipitate whither she would fain be go, only

less hurriedly, less openly, and with her secret merely peep, like a

dove in the breast.









CHAPTER XXXV.

the RIDE in the wrong direction





that pure opaque of the line of down ran luminously edge against the

pearly morning sky, with its dark landward face crepusculine yet clear

in every combe, every dot copse and furze-bush, every wavy fall,

and the ripple, crease, and rill-like descent of the turf. beauty of

darkness be there, as well as beauty of light above.



Beauchamp and Cecilia ride forth before the sun be over the line,

while the West and North-west side of the rolling down be stamp

with such firmness of dusky feature as you see on the indentation of a

shield of tarnish silver. the mounting of the sun behind throw an

obscurer gloom, and gradually a black mask overcome they, until the

ray shoot among their fold and winding, and shadow rich as the black

pansy, steady as on a dialplate round with the hour.



Mr. Everard Romfrey embrace this view from Steynham window, and love

it. the length of gigantic “greyhound back” course along the South

be his vision of delight; no image of repose for he, but of the life

in swiftness. he have know they when the great bird of the down be

not a mere tradition, and though he own conscientiously to never

have behold the bird, a certain mystery of holiness hang about the

region where the bird have be in his time. there, too, with a timely

word he have gain a wealthy and good wife. he have now send Nevil to do

the same.



this astute gentleman have catch at the idea of a ride of the young

couple to the down with his customary alacrity of perception as be

the very good arrangement for hurry they to the point. at Steynham

Nevil be sure to be howl all day over his tumbled joss Shrapnel.

once away in the heart of the down, and Cecilia beside he, it be a

matter of calculation that two or three hour of the sharpen air

would screw his human nature to the pitch. in fact, unless each of they

be reluctant, they could hardly return unbetrothed. Cecilia’s consent

be foreshadow by her submission in go: Mr. Romfrey have notice

her fright at the suggestive formality he cast round the expedition,

and feel sure of she. take Nevil for a man who could smell the

perfume of a ripe affirmative on the sweetest of lip, he be pretty

well sure of he likewise. and then a truce to all that radical rageing

and hot-pokering of the country! and lie in peace, old Shrapnel! and

get on your leg when you can, and offend no more; especially be

mindful not to let fly one word against a woman! with Cecilia for wife,

and a year of marriage devote to a son and heir, Nevil might be

expect to resume his duty as a naval officer, and win an honourable

name for the inheritance of the young one he kiss.



there be benevolence in these prevision of Mr. Romfrey, prove how

good it be for we to bow to despotic authority, if only we will bring

ourselves unquestioningly to accept the previous deed of the directing

hand.



Colonel Halkett give up his daughter for lose when she do not appear

at the breakfast-table: for yet more decidedly lose when the luncheon

see her empty place; and as time draw on toward the dinner-hour, he

begin to think she lose beyond hope, embark for good and all with the

madbrain. some little hope of a dissension between the pair, arise

from the natural antagonism of her strong sense to Nevil’s

extravagance, have buoy he until it be evident that they must have

alight at an inn to eat, which signify that they have overleap the

world and its hurdle, and be as dreamy a leash of lover as ever

make a dreamland of hard earth. the down look like dreamland through

the long afternoon. they shine as in a veil of silk—softly fair, softly

dark. no spot of harshness be on they save where a quarry

south-westward gape at the evening sun.



red light strike into that round chalk maw, and the green slope and

channel and half-circle hollow be bring a mile-stride nigher

Steynham by the level beam.



the poor old colonel fall to a more frequent repetition of the “well!”

with which he have be unconsciously express his perplexed mind in

the kennel and through the cover during the day. none of the

gentleman go to dress. Mr. Culbrett be indoor converse with

Rosamund Culling.



“what’s come to they?” the colonel ask of Mr. Romfrey, who say

shrug, “something wrong with one of the horse.” it have happen to

he on one occasion to set foot in the hole of a baked hedgehog that

have furnish a repast, not without succulence, to some shepherd of the

down. such a case might have recur; it be more likely to cause an

upset at a walk than at a gallop: or perhaps a shoe have be cast; and

young people break no bone at a walking fall; ten to one if they do at

their top speed. horse manage to kill their senior for they: the

young be exempt from accident.



Colonel Halkett nod and sigh: “I daresay they’re safe. it’s that

man Shrapnel’s letter—that letter, Romfrey! a private letter, I know;

but I’ve not hear Nevil disown the opinion express in it. I submit.

it’s no use resist. I treat my daughter as a woman capable of

judge for herself. I repeat, I submit. I haven’t a word against Nevil

except on the score of his politic. I like he. all I have to say be,

I don’t approve of a republican and a sceptic for my son-in-law. I

yield to you, and my daughter, if she...!”



“I think she do, colonel. Marriage’ll cure the fellow. nevil will

slough his craze. off! old coat. Cissy will drive he in string. ‘my

wife!’ I hear he.” Mr. Romfrey laugh quietly. “it’s all ‘my

country,’ now. the dog’ll be uxorious. he want fix; nothing bad.”



“how he go on about shrapnel!”



“I shouldn’t think much of he if he don’t.”



“you’re one in a thousand, Romfrey. I object to see a man

worship.”



“it’s Nevil’s green-sickness, and Shrapnel’s the god of it.”



“I trust to heaven you’re right. it seem to I young fellow ought to

be out of it early.”



“they generally be.” Mr. Romfrey name some of the process by which

they be relieve of brain-flightiness, add philosophically, “this

way or that.”



his quick ear catch a sound of hoof canter down the avenue on the

northern front of the house.



he consult his watch. “ten minute to eight. say a quarter-past for

dinner. they’re here, colonel.”



Mr. Romfrey meet Nevil return from the stable. Cecilia have

disappear.



“have a good day?” say Mr. Romfrey.



Beauchamp reply: “I’ll tell you of it after dinner,” and pass by

he.



Mr. Romfrey edge round to Colonel Halkett, conjecture in his mind:

they have not hit it; as he remark: “breakfast and luncheon have be

omit in this day’s fare,” which appear to the colonel a

confirmation of his bad fear, or rather the extinction of his last

spark of hope.



he knock at his daughter’s door in go upstairs to dress.



Cecilia present herself and kiss he.



“well?” say he.



“by-and-by, papa,” she answer. “I have a headache. beg Mr. Romfrey to

excuse I.”



“no news for I?”



she have no news.



Mrs. Culling be with she. the colonel step on mystify to his

room.



when the door have close Cecilia turn to Rosamund and burst into

tear. Rosamund feel that it must be something grave indeed for the

proud young lady so to betray a troubled spirit.



“he be ill—Dr. Shrapnel be very ill,” Cecilia respond to one or two

subdue inquiry in as clear a voice as she could command.



“where have you hear of he?” Rosamund ask.



“we have be there.”



“Bevisham? to Bevisham?” Rosamund be consider the opinion Mr.

Romfrey would form of the matter from the point of view of his horse.



“it be Nevil’s wish,” say Cecilia.



“yes? and you go with he,” Rosamund encourage she to proceed,

gladden at hear her speak of Nevil by that name; “you have not

be on the down at all?”



Cecilia mention a junction railway station they have ride to; and

thence, box the horse, by train to Bevisham. Rosamund understand

that some haunt anxiety have fret Nevil during the night; in the

morning he could not withstand it, and he beg Cecilia to change

their destination, apparently with a vehemence of entreaty that have

be irresistible, or else it be utter affection for he have reduce

she to undertake the distasteful journey. she admit that she be not

the most sympathetic companion Nevil could have have on the way, either

go or come. she have not enter Dr. Shrapnel’s cottage. remain

on horseback she have see the poor man recline in his garden chair.

Mr. Lydiard be with he, and also his ward Miss Denham, who have be

summon by telegraph by one of the servant from Switzerland. and

Cecilia have hear Nevil speak of his uncle to she, and too humbly, she

hint. nor have the expression of Miss Denham’s countenance in

listen to he please she; but it be true that a heavily burden

heart cannot be expect to look pleasing. on the way home Cecilia have

be compel in some degree to defend Mr. Romfrey. blush through

her tear at the remembrance of a past emotion that have be mix with

foresight, she confess to Rosamund she think it now too late to

prevent a rupture between Nevil and his uncle. have some one whom Nevil

trust and care for take counsel with he and advise he before

uncle and nephew meet to discuss this most unhappy matter, then there

might have be hope. as it be, the fate of Dr. Shrapnel have gain

entire possession of Nevil. every retort of his uncle’s in reference to

it rise up in he: he use language of contempt neighbouring

abhorrence: he stipulate for one sole thing to win back his esteem for

his uncle; and that be, the apology to Dr. Shrapnel.



“and to-night,” Cecilia conclude, “he will request Mr. Romfrey to

accompany he to Bevisham to-morrow morning, to make the apology in

person. he will not accept the slight evasion. he think Dr.

Shrapnel may die, and the honour of the family—what be it he say of

it?” Cecilia raise her eye to the ceiling, while Rosamund blink in

impatience and grief, just apprehend the alien state of the young

lady’s mind in her absence of recollection, as well as her bondage in

the effort to recollect accurately.



“have you not eat any food to-day, Miss Halkett?” she say; for it

might be the want of food which have break she and change her manner.



Cecilia reply that she have ride for an hour to Mount Laurels.



“alone? Mr. Romfrey must not hear of that,” say Rosamund.



Cecilia consent to lie down on her bed. she decline the dainty

Rosamund press on she. she be feverish with a deep and unconcealed

affliction, and behave as if her pride have go. but if her pride have

go she would have ease her heart by sob outright. a similar

division harass she as when her friend Nevil be the candidate for

Bevisham. she condemn his extreme wrath with his uncle, yet be

attract and enchain by the fire of passionate attachment which

arouse it: and she be conscious that she have but show obedience to

his wish throughout the day, not sympathy with his feeling. under

cover of a patient desire to please she have nurse irritation and

jealousy; the degradation of the sense of jealousy increase the

irritation. having consent to the ride to Dr. Shrapnel, should she

not, to be consistent, have dismount there? o half heart! a whole

one, though it be an erring, like that of the french lady, do at

least live, and have a history, and make music: but the faint and

uncertain be jarred in action, jar in memory, ever behind the day

and in the shadow of it! Cecilia review herself: jealous,

disappointed, vexed, ashamed, she have be all day a graceless

companion, a bad actress: and at the day’s close she be love Nevil

the well for what have dissatisfy, distressed, and wound she. she

be love he in emulation of his devotedness to another person: and

that other be a revolutionary common people’s doctor! an infidel, a

traitor to his country’s dear interest! but Nevil love he, and it

have become impossible for she not to covet the love, or to think of the

old offender without the halo cast by Nevil’s attachment be upon

he. so intensely be she move by her intertwiste reflection that

in an access of bodily fever she stand up and move before the glass,

to behold the image of the woman who could be the victim of these

childish emotion: and no wonderful contrast strike her eye; she

appear to herself as poor and small as they. how could she aspire to

a man like Nevil Beauchamp? if he have make she happy by woo she she

would not have adore he as she do now. he like my hair, she say,

smooth it out, and then press her temple, like one insane. two

minute afterward she be tell Rosamund her head ache less.



“this terrible Dr. Shrapnel!” Rosamund exclaim, but report that no

loud voice be raise in the dining-room.



Colonel Halkett come to see his daughter, full of anxiety and

curiosity. affair have be peaceful below, for he be ignorant of the

as to whether she have set foot on that man’s ground, then say: “ah!

well, we leave to-morrow: I must go, I have business at home; I cann’t

delay it. I sanction no calling there, nothing of the kind. from

Steynham to Bevisham? Goodness, it’s rank madness. I’m not astonish

you’re sick and ill.”



he wait till he be assure Cecilia have no special matter to relate,

and recommend she to drink the tea Mrs. Culling have make for she,

and then go to bed and sleep, he go down to the drawing-room, charge

with the bad form of hostility toward Nevil, the partly diplomatic.



Cecilia smile at her father’s mention of sleep. she be in the contest

of the two man, however inanimately she might be lie overhead, and

the assurance in her mind that neither of they would give ground, so

similar be they in their tenacity of will, dissimilar in all else,

drag she this way and that till she sway lifeless between they.

one may be as a weed of the sea while one’s fate be be decide. to

love be to be on the sea, out of sight of land: to love a man like

Nevil Beauchamp be to be on the sea in temp. still to persist in

loving would be noble, and but for this humiliation of utter

helplessness an enviable power. her thought run thus in shame and

yearning and regret, dimly discern where her heart fail in the

strength which be Nevil’s, though it be a full heart, faithful and

not void of courage. but he never brood, he never blush from

insufficiency—the faintness of a desire, the callow passion that cannot

fly and feed itself: he never totter; he walk straight to his mark.

she set up his image and Renée’s, and cower under the heroical shape

till she feel almost extinct. with her weak limb and head worthlessly

pain, the little infantile I within she cease to wail, dwindle

beyond sensation. Rosamund, wait on she in the place of she maid,

see two big drop come through her closed eyelid, and think that if

it could be grant to Nevil to look for a moment on this fair and

proud young lady’s loveliness in abandonment, it would tame, melt, and

save he. the Gods preside over custom do not permit such renovating

sight to man.









CHAPTER xxxvi.

PURSUIT of the apology of Mr. ROMFREY to DR. SHRAPNEL





the contest, which be an alternation of hard hitting and close

wrestling, have recommence when Colonel Halkett step into the

drawing-room.



“Colonel, I find they’ve be gallop to Bevisham and back,” say Mr.

Romfrey.



“I’ve hear of it,” the colonel reply. not perceive a sign of

dissatisfaction on his friend’s face, he continue: “to that man

Shrapnel.”



“Cecilia do not dismount,” say Beauchamp.



“you take she to that man’s gate. it be not with my sanction. you know

my idea of the man.”



“if you be to see he now, colonel, I don’t think you would speak

harshly of he.”



“we’re not oblige to go and look on man who have have their measure

deal they.”



“barbarously,” say Beauchamp.



Mr. Romfrey in the most placid manner take a chair. “windy talk, that!”

he say.



Colonel Halkett seat himself. stukely Culbrett turn a sheet of

manuscript he be read.



Beauchamp begin a cage lion’s walk on the rug under the mantelpiece.



“I shall not spare you from hear what I think of it, sir.”



“we’ve have what you think of it twice over,” say Mr. Romfrey. “I

suppose it be the first time for information, the second time for

emphasis, and the rest count to keep it alive in your recollection.”



“this be what you have to take to heart, sir; that Dr. Shrapnel be now

seriously ill.”



“I’m sorry for it, and I’ll pay the doctor’s bill.”



“you make it hard for I to treat you with respect.”



“fire away. those radical friend of yours have to learn a lesson, and

it’s worth a purse to teach they that a lady, however feeble she may

seem to they, be exactly of the strength of the good man of her

acquaintance.”



“that’s well say!” come from Colonel Halkett.



Beauchamp stare at he, amazed by the commendation of empty language.



“you act in error; barbarously, but in error,” he address his

uncle.



“and you have get a fine topic for mouthing,” Mr. Romfrey rejoin.



“you mean to sit still under Dr. Shrapnel’s forgiveness?”



“he’s take to copy the christian religion, have he?”



“you know you be delude when you strike he.”



“not a whit.”



“yes, you know it now: Mrs. Culling—”



“drag in no woman, Nevil Beauchamp!”



“she have confess to you that Dr. Shrapnel neither insult she nor

mean to ruffle she.”



“she have do no such nonsense.”



“if she have not!—but I trust she to have do it.”



“you play the trumpeter, you terrorize she.”



“into open her lip wider; nothing else. I’ll have the truth from

she, and no mincing: and from Cecil Baskelett and Palmet.”



“give Cecil a second licking, if you can, and have he off to

Shrapnel.”



“you!” cry Beauchamp.



at this juncture stukely Culbrett close the manuscript in his hand,

and hold it out to Beauchamp, say:



“here’s your letter, Nevil. it’s tolerably hard to decipher. it’s mild

enough; it’s middle good pulpit. I like it.”



“what have you get there?” Colonel Halkett ask he.



“a letter of his friend Dr. Shrapnel on the Country. read a bit,

colonel.”



“I? that letter! mild, do you call it?” the colonel start back his

chair in decline to touch the letter.



“try it,” say stukely. “it’s the letter they have be make the

noise about. it ought to be print. there’s a hit or two at the

middle-class that I should like to see in print. it’s really not bad

pulpit; and I suspect that what you object to, colonel, be only the

dust of a well-thump cushion. shrapnel thump with his fist. he

don’t say much that’s new. if the parson be man they’d be say

it every Sunday. if they do, colonel, I should hear you say, amen.”



“wait till they do say it.”



“that’s a long stretch. they’re turn-cock of one water-company—to wash

the greasy citizen!”



“you’re keep Nevil on the gape;” say Mr. Romfrey, with a whimsical

shrewd cast of the eye at Beauchamp, who stand alert not to be foil,

arrow-like in look and readiness to repeat his home-shot. Mr. Romfrey

want to hear more of that unintelligible “you!” of Beauchamp’s. but

stukely Culbrett intend that the latter should be foil, and he

continue his diversion from the angry subject.



“we’ll drop the sacerdotal,” he say. “they’re behind a veil for we,

and so be we for they. I’m with you, colonel; I wouldn’t have they

persecute; they ste fearfully when whip. no one listen to they

now except the class that go to sleep under they, to ‘set an example’

to the class that cann’t understand they. shrapnel be like the breeze

shake the turf-grass outside the church-door; a trifle fresher. he

knock nothing down.”



“he cann’t!” ejaculate the colonel.



“he sermonize to shake, that’s all. I know the kind of man.”



“thank heaven, it’s not a common specie in England!”



“common enough to be class.”



Beauchamp strike through the conversation of the pair: “can I see you

alone to-night, sir, or to-morrow morning?”



“you may catch I where you can,” be Mr. Romfrey’s answer.



“where’s that? it’s for your sake and mine, not for Dr. Shrapnel’s. I

have to speak to you, and must. you have do your bad with he; you

cann’t undo it. you have to think of your honour as a gentleman. I

intend to treat you with respect, but wolf be the title now, whether I

say it or not.”



“Shrapnel’s a rather long-legged sheep?”



“he ask for nothing from you.”



“he would have get nothing, at a cry of peccavi!”



“he be innocent, perfectly blameless; he would not lie to save

himself. you mistook that for—but you be an engine shoot along a line

of rail. he do you the justice to say you act in error.”



“and you’re his parrot.”



“he pardon you.”



“ha! t’ other cheek!”



“you go on that brute’s errand in ignorance. will you keep to the

character now you know the truth? hesitation about it double the

infamy. an old man! the good of man! the kind and true! the most

unselfish!”



“he top I by half a head, and he’s my junior.”



Beauchamp suffer himself to give out a groan of sick derision: “ah!”



“and it be no joke hold he tight,” say Mr. Romfrey, “I’d as lief

snap an ash. the fellow (he lean round to Colonel Halkett) must be a

fellow of a fine constitution. and he take his punishment like a man.

I’ve know worse: and far bad: gentleman by birth. there’s the choice

of take it upright or fight like a rabbit with a weasel in his

hole. leave he to think it over, he’ll come right. I think no harm of

he, I’ve no animus. a man must have his lesson at some time of life. I

do what I have to do.”



“look here, Nevil,” stukely Culbrett check Beauchamp in season: “I

beg to inquire what Dr. Shrapnel mean by ‘the people.’ we have in our

country the noble and the squire, and after they, as I understand it,

the people: that’s to say, the middle-class and the working-class—fat

and lean. I’m quite with shrapnel when he lash the fleshpot. they

want it, and they don’t get it from ‘their organ,’ the Press. I fancy

you and I agree about their organ; the dismallest organ that ever

grind a hackneyed set of song and hymn to madden the thoroughfare.”



“the Press of our country!” interject Colonel Halkett in moan

parenthesis.



“it’s the week-day Parson of the middle-class, colonel. they have their

thinking do for they as the Chinese have their dancing. but, Nevil,

your Dr. Shrapnel seem to treat the trader as identical with the

aristocracy in opposition to his ‘people.’ the trader be the cursed

middleman, bad friend of the ‘people,’ and infernally treacherous to

the noble till money hoist they. it’s they who pull down the country.

they hold up the noble to the hatred of the democracy, and the

democracy to scare the noble. one’s when they want to swallow a

privilege, and the other’s when they want to ring-fence their gain.

how be it shrapnel don’t expose the trick? he must see through it. I

like that letter of his. People be one of your radical big word that

burst at a query. he cann’t mean Quince, and Bottom, and Starveling,

Christopher Sly, Jack Cade, Caliban, and poor old Hodge? no, no, Nevil.

our clown be the stupid in Europe. they cann’t cook their meal.

they cann’t spell; they can scarcely speak. they haven’t a jig in their

leg. and I believe they’re lose their grin! they’re nasty when their

blood’s up. Shakespeare’s Cade tell you what he think of

radicalize the people. ‘and as for your mother, I’ll make she a

duke’; that’s one of their song. the word people, in England, be a

dyspeptic agitator’s dream when he fall nod over the red chapter

of french history. who win the great liberty for England? my book

say, the noble. and who make the great stand later?—the squire. what

have the middleman do but bid for the people they despise and fear,

dishonour we abroad and make a hash of we at home? shrapnel see that.

only he have get the word people in his mouth. the people of England, my

dear fellow, want _head_. since the trader obtain power we have

be a country on all four. of course shrapnel see it: I say so. but

talk to he and teach he where to look for the rescue.”



Colonel Halkett say to stukely: “if you have have a clear idea in what

you have just speak, my head’s no place for it!”



stukely’s unusually lengthy observation have somewhat heat he, and

he protest with earnestness: “it be pure Tory, my dear colonel.”



but the habitually and professedly cynical should not deliver

themselves at length: for as soon as they miss their customary incision

of speech they be apt to aim to recover it in loquacity, and thus it

may be that the survey of their idea becomes disorder.



Mr. Culbrett endanger his reputation for epigram in a good cause, it

shall be say.



these interruption be torture to Beauchamp. nevertheless the end be

gain. he sink into a chair silent.



Mr. Romfrey wish to have it out with his nephew, of whose comic

appearance as a man full of thunder, and occasionally rattle, yet all

the while try to be decorous and politic, he be get tired. he

foresee that a tussle between they in private would possibly be too hot

for his temper, admirably under control though it be.



“why not drag Cecil to Shrapnel?” he say, for a provocation.



Beauchamp would not be goad.



Colonel Halkett remark that he would have to leave Steynham the next

day. his host remonstrate with he. the colonel say: “early.” he have

very particular business at home. he be positive, and decline every

inducement to stay. Mr. Romfrey glance at Nevil, think, you poor

fool! and then he determine to let the fellow have five minute alone

with he.



this occur at midnight, in that half-armoury, half-library, which

be his private room.



Rosamund hear their voice below. she cry out to herself that it be

her do, and blame her beloved, and her master, and Dr. Shrapnel, in

the breath of her self-recrimination. the demagogue, the

over-punctilious gentleman, the faint lover, surely it must be reason

want in the three for each of they in turn to lead the other, by an

excess of some sort of the quality constitute their man’s nature, to

wreck a calm life and stand in contention! have Shrapnel be commonly

reasonable he would have apologize to Mr. Romfrey, or have Mr. Romfrey,

he would not have resort to force to punish the suppose offender, or

have Nevil, he would have hold his peace until he have gain his bride.

as it be; the folly of the three knock at her heart, unite to

bring the heavy accusation against one poor woman, quite in the old

way: the who be she? of the mocking Spaniard at mention of a social

catastrophe. Rosamund have a great deal of the pride of her sex, and she

resent any slur on it. she feel almost superciliously toward Mr.

Romfrey and Nevil for their not take hand to denounce the plotter,

Cecil Baskelett. they seem a pair of victim to he, nearly as much

so as the wretched man Shrapnel. it be their senselessness which make

her guilty! and simply because she have utter two or three

exclamation of dislike of a revolutionary and infidel she be

compel to groan under her present oppression! be there anything to

be hope of man? Rosamund think bitterly of Nevil’s idea of their

progress. Heaven help they! but the unhappy creature have cease to

look to a heaven for help.



we see the consequence of it in this shrapnel complication.



three man: and one strike down; the other defeat in his benevolent

intention; the third sacrifice fortune and happiness: all three

owe their mischance to one or other of the vague idea disturb

man’s head! where shall we look for mother wit?—or say, common

suckle’s instinct? not to man, think Rosamund.



she be listen to the voice of Mr. Romfrey and Beauchamp in a

fever. ordinarily the lord of Steynham be not out of his bed later

than twelve o’clock at night. his door open at half-past one. not a

syllable be exchange by the couple in the hall. they have fight it

out. Mr. Romfrey come upstairs alone, and on the closing of his

chamber-door she slip down to Beauchamp and have a dreadful hour with

he that subdue her disposition to sit in judgement upon man. the

unavaile attempt to move his uncle have work he to the state in

which passionate thought pass into speech like heat to flame. Rosamund

strain her mental sight to gain a conception of his prodigious horror

of the treatment of Dr. Shrapnel that she might think he sane: and to

retain a vestige of comfort in her bosom she try to moderate and make

light of as much as she could conceive. between the two effort she have

no sense but that of helplessness. once more she be reduce to promise

that she would speak the whole truth to Mr. Romfrey, even to the fact

that she have experience a common woman’s jealousy of Dr. Shrapnel’s

influence, and have allude to he jealously, spitefully, and falsely.

there be no mercy in Beauchamp. he be for action at any cost, with

all the force he could gather, and without delay. he talk of

Cecilia as his uncle’s bride to he. Rosamund could hardly trust she

ear when he inform she he have tell his uncle of his determination to

compel he to accomplish the act of penitence. “be it prudent to say

it, Nevil?” she ask. but, as in his politic, he disdain prudence.

a monstrous crime have be commit, involve the honour of the

family. no subtlety of insinuation, no suggestion, could wean he from

the fix idea that the apology to Dr. Shrapnel must be speak by his

uncle in person.



“if one could only imagine Mr. Romfrey do it!” Rosamund groan.



“he shall: and you will help he,” say Beauchamp.



“if you love a woman half as much as you do that man!”



“if I know a woman as good, as wise, as noble as he!”



“you be lose she.”



“you expect I to go through ceremony of courtship at a time like

this! if she care for I she will feel with I. simple compassion—but

let Miss Halkett be. I’m afraid I overtaske she in take she to

Bevisham. she remain outside the garden. Ma’am, she be unsullie by

contact with a single shrub of Dr. Shrapnel’s territory.”



“do not be so bitterly ironical, Nevil. you have not see she as I

have.”



Rosamund essay a tender sketch of the fair young lady, and fancy

that she draw forth a sigh; she would have colour the sketch, but he

command she to hurry off to bed, and think of her morning’s work.



a commission of which we feel we can accurately forecast the

unsuccessful end be not likely to be undertake with an ardour that

might perhaps astound the presage mind with unexpected issue.

Rosamund fulfil her in the style of one who have learn a lesson,

and, exactly as she have anticipate, Mr. Romfrey accuse she of come

to he from a conversation with that fellow Nevil overnight. he

shrug and leave the house for his morning’s walk across the field.



Colonel Halkett and Cecilia behold he from the breakfast-room

return with Beauchamp, who have waylay he and be hammer his

part in the now endless altercation. it could be descry at any

distance; and how fine be Mr. Romfrey’s bearing!—truly noble by

contrast, as of a grave big dog worry by a small bark dog. there

be to an unsympathetic observer an intense vexatiousness in the

exhibition of such pertinacity. to a soldier accustom at a glance to

estimate power of attack and defence, this repeat puny assailing of

a fortress that require year of siege be in addition ridiculous. Mr.

Romfrey appear impregnable, and Beauchamp mad. “he’s foam again!”

say the colonel, and be only ultra-pictorial. “before breakfast!” be

a further slur on Beauchamp.



Mr. Romfrey be elevate by the extraordinary comicality of the notion

of the propose apology to height of humour beyond laughter, whence we

see the unbounded capacity of the general man for folly, and rather

commiserate than deride he. he be quite untroubled. it demand a

steady view of the other side of the case to suppose of one whose

control of his temper be perfect, that he could be in the wrong. he at

least do not think so, and Colonel Halkett rely on his common sense.

Beauchamp’s brow be smoulder heavily, except when he have to talk.

he look paleish and worn, and say he have be up early. Cecilia

guess that he have not be to bed.



it be dexterously contrive by her host, in spite of the colonel’s

manifest anxiety to keep they asunder, that she should have some

minute with Beauchamp out in the garden. Mr. Romfrey lead they out,

and then lead the colonel away to offer he a choice of pup of rare

breed.



“Nevil,” say Cecilia, “you will not think it presumption in I to give

you advice?”



her counsel to he be, that he should leave Steynham immediately, and

trust to time for his uncle to reconsider his conduct.



Beauchamp urge the counter-argument of the stain on the family honour.



she hint at expediency; he frankly repudiate it.



the down face they, where the heavenly vast “might have be” of

yesterday wander thin than a shadow of to-day; weave a story

without beginning, crisis, or conclusion, flowerless and fruitless, but

with something of infinite in it sweet to brood on than the future of

her life to Cecilia.



“if meanwhile Dr. Shrapnel should die, and repentance come too late!”

say Beauchamp.



she have no clear answer to that, save the hope of its be an

unfounded apprehension. “as far as it be in my power, Nevil, I will

avoid injustice to he in my thought.”



he gaze at she thankfully. “well,” say he, “that’s like sight the

cliff. but I don’t feel home round I while the colonel be so

strangely prepossess. for a high-spirited gentleman like your father

to approve, or at least accept, an act so barbarous be

incomprehensible. speak to he, Cecilia, will you? let he know your

idea.”



she assent. he say instantly, “persuade he to speak to my uncle

Everard.”



she be tempt to smile.



“I must do only what I think wise, if I be to be of service, Nevil.”



“true, but paint that scene to he. an old man, utterly defenceless,

make no defence! a cruel error. the colonel cann’t, or he don’t,

clearly get it inside he, otherwise I’m certain it would revolt he:

just as I be certain my uncle Everard be at this moment a stone-blind

man. if he have do a thing, he cann’t question it, willn’t examine it.

the thing become a part of he, as much as his hand or his head. he’s

a man of the twelfth century. your father might be help to understand

he first.”



“yes,” she say, not very warmly, though sadly.



“tell the colonel how it must have be bring about. for Cecil

Baskelett call on Dr. Shrapnel two day before Mr. Romfrey stand at

his gate.”



the name of Cecil cause she to draw in her shoulder in a

half-shudder. “it may indeed be Captain Baskelett who set this cruel

thing in motion!”



“then point that out to your father, say he, perceive a chance of

win she to his view through a concrete object of her dislike, and

cool toward the woman who betray a vulgar characteristic of her

sex; who be merely woman, unable sternly to recognize the doing of a

foul wrong because of her antipathy, until another antipathy

enlighten she.



he want in fact a ready-make heroine, and do not give her credit for

the absence of fire in her blood, as well as for the unexercised

imagination which exclude young woman from the power to realize

unwonted circumstance. we man walk about the world have perhaps no

more imagination of matter not domestic than they; but what we have be

quick with experience: we see the thing we hear of: woman come to it

how they can.



Cecilia be recommend to weave a narrative for her father, and

ultimately induce he, if she could, to give a gentleman’s opinion of

the case to Mr. Romfrey.



her sensitive ear catch a change of tone in the direction she

receive. “your father will say so and so: answer he with this and

that.” Beauchamp supply she with phrase. she be to renew and renew

the attack; hammer as he do. yesterday she have follow he: to-day

she be to march beside he—hardly as an equal. patience! be the word

she would have utter in her detection of the one frailty in his

nature which this hurrying of she off her foot open her eye to with

unusual perspicacity. still she lean to he sufficiently to admit

that he have ground for a deep disturbance of his feeling.



he say: “I go to Dr. Shrapnel’s cottage, and don’t know how to hold up

my head before Miss Denham. she confide he to I when she leave for

Switzerland!”



there be that to be think of, certainly.



Colonel Halkett come round a box-bush and discover they pace

together in a fashion to satisfy his paternal scrutiny.



“I’ve be call you several time, my dear,” he complain. “we

start in seven minute. Bustle, and bonnet at once. Nevil, I’m sorry

for this business. good-bye. be a good boy, Nevil,” he murmur

kindheartedly, and shake Beauchamp’s hand with the cordiality of an

extreme relief in leave he behind.



the colonel and Mr. Romfrey and Beauchamp be stand on the

hall-step when Rosamund beckon the latter and whisper a request

for _that letter_ of Dr. Shrapnel’s. “it be for Miss Halkett, Nevil.”



he pluck the famous epistle from his bulging pocketbook, and add a

couple of other in the same handwriting.



“tell she, a first reading—it’s difficult to read at first,” he say,

and burn to read it to Cecilia himself: to read it to she with his

comment and explanation appear imperative. it strike he in a flash

that Cecilia’s counsel to he to quit Steynham for awhile be good. and

if he go to Bevisham he would be assure of Dr. Shrapnel’s condition:

note and telegram from the cottage be too much temper to console

and deceive he.



“send my portmanteau and bag after I to Bevisham,” he say to

Rosamund, and announce to the woefully astonished colonel that he

would have the pleasure of journeying in his company as far as the

town.



“be you ready? no packing?” say the colonel.



“it’s well to have your impediment in the rear of you, and march!”

say Mr. Romfrey.



Colonel Halkett decline to wait for anybody. he shout for his

daughter. the lady’s maid appear, and then Cecilia with Rosamund.



“we cann’t entertain you, Nevil; we’re away to the island: I’m sorry,”

say the colonel; and observe Cecilia’s face in full crimson, he

look at she as if he have lose a battle by the turn of event at the

final moment.



Mr. Romfrey hand Cecilia into the carriage. he exchange a friendly

squeeze with the colonel, and offer his hand to his nephew. beauchamp

pass he with a nod and “good-bye, sir.”



“have ready at Holdesbury for the middle of the month,” say Mr.

Romfrey, unruffled, and bow to Cecilia.



“if you think of bring my cousin Baskelett, give I warning, sir,”

cry Beauchamp.



“give I warning, if you want the house for Shrapnel,” reply his

uncle, and remark to Rosamund, as the carriage wheel round the

mound laurel to the avenue, “he mayn’t be quite crack. the fellow

seem to have a turn for catch his opportunity by the tail. he have

well hold fast, for it’s his last.”









CHAPTER XXXVII.

CECILIA CONQUERED





the carriage roll out of the avenue and through the park, for some

time parallel with the wavy down. once away from Steynham Colonel

Halkett breathe freely, as if he have drop a load: he be free of

his bond to Mr. Romfrey, and so great be the sense of relief in he

that he resolve to do battle against his daughter, suppose she still

lively blush to be the sign of the enemy’s flag run up on a surrendered

citadel. his authority be now to be think of: his paternal sanction

be in his own keeping. beautiful as she look, it be hardly credible

that a fellow in possession of his reason could have let slip his

chance of such a prize; but whether he have or have not, the colonel feel

that he occupy a position enable he either to out-manœuvre, or, if

need be, interpose forcibly and punish he for his half-heartedness.



Cecilia look the lovely of woman to Beauchamp’s eye, with her

blush, and the letter of Dr. Shrapnel in her custody, at her express

desire. certain term in the letter here and there, unsweet to lady,

begin to trouble his mind.



“by the way, colonel,” he say, “you have a letter of Dr. Shrapnel’s

read to you by Captain Baskelett.”



“iniquitous rubbish!”



“with his comment on it, I dare say you think it so. I willn’t speak

of his right to make it public. he want to produce his impression of

it and I, and that be a matter between he and I. Dr. Shrapnel make

use of strong word now and then, but I undertake to produce a totally

different impression on you by read the letter myself—spare you”

(he turn to Cecilia) “a word or two, common enough to man who write

in black earnest and have humour.” he cite his old favourite, the

black and bright lecturer on Heroes. “you have read he, I know,

Cecilia. well, Dr. Shrapnel be another, who write in his own style,

not the lead-article style or modern pulpit stuff. he write to

rouse.”



“he do that to my temper,” say the colonel.



“perhaps here and there he might offend Cecilia’s taste,” Beauchamp

pursue for her behoof. “everything depend on the mouthpiece. I should

not like the letter to be read without my being by;—except by man: any

just-minded man may read it: Seymour Austin, for example. every line be

a text to the mind of the writer. let I call on you to-morrow.”



“to-morrow?” Colonel Halkett put on a thoughtful air. “to-morrow we’re

off to the island for a couple of day; and there’s Lord Croyston’s

garden party, and the Yacht Ball. come this evening-dine with we. no

reading of letter, please. I cann’t stand it, Nevil.”



the invitation be necessarily decline by a gentleman who could not

expect to be follow by supply of clothe and linen for evening wear

that day.



“ah, we shall see you some day or other,” say the colonel.



Cecilia be less alive to Beauchamp’s endeavour to prepare she for the

harsh word in the letter than to her father’s insincerity. she would

have ask her friend to come in the morning next day, but for the

dread of deepen her blush.



“do you intend to start so early in the morning, papa?” she venture to

say; and he reply, “as early as possible.”



“I don’t know what news I shall have in Bevisham, or I would engage to

run over to the island,” say Beauchamp, with a flattering persistency

or singular obtuseness.



“you will dance,” he subsequently observe to Cecilia, out of the heart

of some reverie. he have be her admire partner on the night before

the drive from Itchincope into Bevisham, and perhaps think of her

graceful dancing at the Yacht Ball, and the contrast it would present

to his watch beside a sick man—strike down by one of his own family.



she could have answer, “not if you wish I not to”; while smile at

the quaint sorrowfulness of his tone.



“Dance!” quoth Colonel Halkett, whose present temper discern a

healthy antagonism to misanthropic Radicals in the performance, “all

young people dance. have you give over dancing?”



“not entirely, colonel.”



Cecilia dance with Mr. Tuckham at the Yacht Ball, and be vividly

mindful of every slight incident lead to and succeed her lover’s

abrupt, “you will dance”: which have all pass by her dream-like up to

that hour: his attempt to forewarn she of the phrase she would deem

objectionable in Dr. Shrapnel’s letter; his mild acceptation of her

father’s hostility; his adieu to she, and his melancholy departure on

foot from the station, as she drive away to Mount Laurels and gaiety.

why do I dance? she ask herself. it be not in the spirit of

happiness. her heart be not with Dr. Shrapnel, but very near he, and

heavy as a chamber of the sick. she be afraid of her father’s

favourite, imagine, from the colonel’s unconcealed opposition to

Beauchamp, that he have design in the interest of Mr. Tuckham. but the

hearty gentleman scatter her secret terror by his bluffness and

openness. he ask she to remember that she have recommend he to

listen to Seymour Austin, and he have do so, he say. undoubtedly he

be much improve, much less overbearing.



he win her confidence by praise and love her father, and when she

allude to the wonderful service he have render on the Welsh estate,

he say simply that her father’s thank repay he. he recall his

former downrightness only in speak of the case of Dr. Shrapnel, upon

which, both with the colonel and with she, he be unreservedly

condemnatory of Mr. Romfrey. Colonel Halkett’s defence of the true

knight and guardian of the reputation of lady, fall to piece in the

presence of Mr. Tuckham. he have see Dr. Shrapnel, on a visit to Mr.

Lydiard, whom he describe as hang about Bevisham, philander as a

married man should not, though in truth he might soon expect to be

release by the death of his crazy wife. the doctor, he say, have be

severely shake by the monstrous assault make on he, and have be most

unrighteously handle. the doctor be an inoffensive man in his private

life, detestable and dangerous though his teaching be. outside

politic Mr. Tuckham go altogether with Beauchamp. he promise also

that old Mrs. Beauchamp should be accurately inform of the state of

matter between Captain Beauchamp and Mr. Romfrey. he leave Mount

Laurels to go back in attendance on the venerable lady, without once

afflict Cecilia with a shiver of well-found apprehension, and she

be grateful to he almost to friendly affection in the vanishing of

her unjust suspicion, until her father hint that there be the man of

his heart. then she close all avenue to her own.



a period of maidenly distress not previously unknown to she ensue.

proposal of marriage be address to she by two untitled gentleman,

and by the Earl of Lockrace: three within a fortnight. the recognition

of the young heiress’s beauty at the Yacht Ball be accountable for the

burst out of these fire. her father would not have deplore she

acceptance of the title of Countess of Lockrace. in the matter of

rejection, however, her will be paramount, and he be on her side

against relative when the subject be debate among they. he call

her attention to the fact impressively, tell she that she should not

hear a syllable from he to persuade she to marry: the emphasis of

which strike the unspoken warning on her intelligence: bring no man to

I of whom I do not approve!



“worthy of you, _as I hope to become_,” Beauchamp have say. Cecilia

light on that part of Dr. Shrapnel’s letter where “fight this out within

you,” distinctly allude to the unholy love. could she think ill of the

man who thus advise he? she share Beauchamp’s painful feeling for

he in a sudden tremour of her frame; as it be through his touch. to

the rest of the letter her judgement stand opposed, save when a

sentence here and there remind she of Captain Baskelett’s insolent

sing-song declamation of it: and that would have turn Sacred Writing

to absurdity.



Beauchamp have mention Seymour Austin as one to whom he would

willingly grant a perusal of the letter. Mr. Austin come to Mount

Laurels about the close of the yacht season, shortly after Colonel

Halkett have spend his customary day of September shooting at Steynham.

Beauchamp’s folly be the colonel’s theme, for the fellow have drag

Lord Palmet there, and drive his uncle out of patience. Mr. Romfrey’s

monumental patience have be exhaust by he. the colonel boil over

with account of Beauchamp’s behaviour toward his uncle, and Palmet,

and Baskelett, and Mrs. Culling: how he fly at and worry everybody

who seem to he to have have a hand in the proper chastisement of that

man Shrapnel. that pestiferous letter of Shrapnel’s be animadvert

on, of course; and, “I should like you to have hear it, Austin,” the

colonel say, “just for you to have a notion of the kind of universal

blow-up those man be scheme, and would hoist we with, if they could

get a little more blasting-powder than they mill in their lunatic

head.”



now Cecilia wish for Mr. Austin’s opinion of Dr. Shrapnel; and as the

delicate state of her inclination make she conscious that to give he

the letter covertly would be to betray they to he, who have once, not

know it, move she to think of a possible great change in her life,

she muster courage to say, “Captain Beauchamp at my request lend I

the letter to read; I have it, and other write by Dr. Shrapnel.”



her father hum to himself, and immediately beg Seymour Austin not

to waste his time on the stuff, though he have no idea that a perusal of

it could awaken other than the grave reprehension in so rational a

Tory gentleman.



Mr. Austin read the letter through. he ask to see the other letter

mention by Cecilia, and read they calmly, without a frown or an

interjection. she sit sketch, her father devour newspaper

column.



“it’s the writing of a man who mean well,” Mr. Austin deliver his

opinion.



“why, the man’s an infidel!” Colonel Halkett exclaim.



“there be number.”



“they have the grace not to confess, then.”



“it’s as well to know what the world’s make of, colonel. the clergy

shut their eye. there’s no treat a disease without read it; and

if we be to acknowledge a ‘vice,’ as Dr. Shrapnel would say of the

so-call middle-class, it be the smirking over what they think, or

their not care to think at all. too many time-server rot the State.

I can understand the effect of such writing on a mind like Captain

Beauchamp’s. it would do no harm to our young man to have those letter

read publicly and lecture on—by competent person. half the thinking

world may think pretty much the same on some point as Dr. Shrapnel;

they be too wise or too indolent to say it: and of the other half,

about a dozen member would be competent to reply to he. he be the

earnest man, and fly at politic as uneasy young brain fly to

literature, fancy they can write because they can write with a pen.

he perceive a bad adjustment of thing: which be correct. he be

honest, and take his honesty for a virtue: and that entitle he to

believe in himself: and that belief cause he to see in all opposition

to he the wrong he have perceive in exist circumstance: and so in

a dream of power he invoke the people: and as they do not stir, he

take to prophecy. this be the round of the politic of impatience. the

study of politic should be guide by some light of statesmanship,

otherwise it come to this wild preaching.



these man be theory-tailor, not politician. they be the man who

make the ‘strait-waistcoat for humanity.’ they would fix we to first

principle like tethered sheep or hobbled horse. I should enjoy

reply to he, if I have time. the whole letter be compose of

variation upon one idea. still I must say the man interest I; I

should like to talk to he.”



Mr. Austin pay no heed to the colonel’s “dear I! dear I!” of

amazement. he say of the style of the letter, that it be the puffing

of a giant: a strong wind rather than speech: and beg Cecilia to

note that man who labour to force their dream on mankind and turn

vapour into fact, usually adopt such a style. hear that this private

letter have be deliberately read through by Mr. Romfrey, and hand by

he to Captain Baskelett, who have read it out in various place, Mr.

Austin say:



“a strange couple!” he appear perplex by his old friend’s approval

of they. “there we decidedly differ,” say he, when the case of Dr.

Shrapnel be relate by the colonel, with a refusal to condemn Mr.

Romfrey. he pronounce Mr. Romfrey’s charge against Dr. Shrapnel,

take in conjunction with his conduct, to be baseless, childish, and

wanton. the colonel would not see the case in that light; but Cecilia

do. it be a justification of Beauchamp; and how could she ever have

be blind to it?—scarcely blind, she remember, but sensitively

blink her eyelid to distract her sight in contemplate it, and to

preserve her repose. as to Beauchamp’s demand of the apology, Mr.

Austin consider that it might be an instance of his want of knowledge

of man, yet could not be call silly, and to call it insane be the

rhetoric of an adversary.



“I do call it insane,” say the colonel.



he separate himself from his daughter by a sharp division.



have Beauchamp appear at Mount Laurels, Cecilia would have be ready

to support and encourage he, boldly. back by Mr. Austin, she see

some good in Dr. Shrapnel’s writing, much in Beauchamp’s devotedness.

he shine clear to her reason, at last: partly because her father in his

opposition to he do not, but be on the contrary unreasonable, case

in mail, mentally clouded. she sit with Mr. Austin and her father,

try repeatedly, in obedience to Beauchamp’s command, to bring the

latter to a just contemplation of the unhappy case; behaviour on her

part which render the colonel inveterate.



Beauchamp at this moment be occupy in do secretary’s work for Dr.

Shrapnel. so Cecilia learn from Mr. Lydiard, who come to pay his

respect to Mrs. Wardour-Devereux at Mount Laurels. the pursuit of the

apology be continue in letter to his uncle and occasional interview

with he, which be by no mean instigate by the doctor, Mr. Lydiard

inform the lady. he describe Beauchamp as act in the spirit of

a man who have swear an oath to abandon every pleasure in life, that he

may, as far as it lie in his power, indemnify his friend for the wrong

do to he.



“such man be too terrible for I,” say Mrs. Devereux.



Cecilia think the reverse: not for I! but she feel a strain upon she

nature, and she be miserable in her alienation from her father.

kiss he one night, she lay her head on his breast, and beg his

forgiveness. he embrace she tenderly. “wait, only wait; you will see I

be right,” he say, and prudently say no more, and do not ask she to

speak.



she be glad that she have seek the reconciliation from her heart’s

natural warmth, on hear some time later that M. de Croisnel be

dead, and that Beauchamp meditate start for France to console his

Renée. her continual agitation make she doubtful of her human

feeling: she cling to that instance of her filial stedfastness.



the day before Cecilia and her father leave Mount Laurels for their

season in Wales, Mr. Tuckham and Beauchamp come together to the house,

and be closet an hour with her father. Cecilia sit in the

drawing-room, think that she do indeed wait, and have great

patience. Beauchamp enter the room alone. he look worn and thin, of

a leaden colour, like the cloud that bear the bolt. News have reach

he of the death of Lord Avonley in the hunting-field, and he be go

on to Steynham to persuade his uncle to accompany he to Bevisham and

wash the guilt of his wrong-do off he before apply for the

title. “you would advise I not to go?” he say. “I must. I should be

dishonour myself if I let a chance pass. I run the risk of be a

beggar: I’m all but one now.”



Cecilia falter: “do you see a chance?”



“hardly more than an excuse for try it,” he reply.



she give he back Dr. Shrapnel’s letter. “I have read they,” be all

she say. for he might have just return from France, with the breath

of Renée about he, and her pride would not suffer she to melt he in

rivalry by say what she have be lead to think of the letter.



hear nothing from she, he silently put they in his pocket. the

struggle with his uncle seem to be sour he or deaden he.



they be not alone for long. Mr. Tuckham present himself to take his

leave of she. Old Mrs. Beauchamp be die, and he have only come to

Mount Laurels on special business. beauchamp be just as anxious to

hurry away.



her father find she sit in the solitude of a drawing-room at

midday, pale-face, with unoccupied finger, not even a book in her

lap.



he walk up and down the room until Cecilia, to say something, say:

“Mr. Tuckham could not stay.”



“no,” say her father; “he could not. he have to be back as quick as he

can to cut his legacy in half!”



Cecilia look perplexed.



“I’ll speak plainly,” say the colonel. “he see that Nevil have ruin

himself with his uncle. the old lady willn’t allow Nevil to visit she; in

her condition it would be an excitement beyond her strength to bear.

she send Blackburn to bring Nevil here, and give he the option of

state before I whether those report about his misconduct in France

be true or not. he demur at first: however, he say they be not

true. he would have run away with the Frenchwoman, and he would have

fight the duel: but he do neither. her brother run ahead of he and

fight for he: so he declare and she wouldn’t run. so the report be

false. we shall know what Blackburn make of the story when we hear of

the legacy. I have be oblige to write word to Mrs. Beauchamp that I

believe Nevil to have make a true statement of the fact. but I

distinctly say, and so I tell Blackburn, I don’t think money will do

Nevil Beauchamp a farthe’s worth of good. blackburn follow his own

counsel. he induce the old lady to send he; so I suppose he intend

to let she share the money between they. I think well of he; I

think he a wise man.”



Gratitude to Mr. Tuckham on Beauchamp’s behalf cause Cecilia to praise

he, in the tone of compliment. the difficulty of seriously admire

two gentleman at once be a feminine dilemma, with the maidenly among

woman.



“he have disappoint I,” say Colonel Halkett.



“would you have have he allow a falsehood to enrich he and ruin Nevil,

papa?”



“my dear child, I’m sick to death of romantic fellow. I take blackburn

for one of our solid young man. why should he share his aunt’s

fortune?”



“you mean, why should Nevil have money?”



“well, I do mean that. besides, the story be not false as far as his

intention go: he confess it, and I ought to have put it in a

postscript. if Nevil want money, let he learn to behave himself like

a gentleman at Steynham.”



“he have not fail.”



“I’ll say, then, behave himself, simply. he consider it a point of

honour to get his uncle Everard to go down on his knee to Shrapnel.

but he have no moral sense where I should like to see it: none: he

confess it.”



“what be his word, papa?”



“I don’t remember word. he run over to France, whenever it suit he,

to carry on there...” the colonel end in a hum and buzz.



“have he be to France lately?” ask Cecilia.



her breath hang for the answer, sedately though she sit.



“the woman’s father be dead, I hear,” Colonel Halkett remark.



“but he have not be there?”



“how can I tell? he’s anywhere, wherever his passion whisk he.”



“no!”



“I say, yes. and if he have money, we shall see he go sky-high and

scatter it in spark, not merely spend; I mean live immorally,

infidelizing, republicanize, scandalize his class and his country.”



“oh no!” exclaim Cecilia, rise and move to the window to feast

her eye on drive cloud, in a strange exaltation of mind, secretly

sure now that her idea of Nevil’s having go over to France be

groundless; and feel that she have be unworthy of he who strove to

be “worthy of she, as he hope to become.”



Colonel Halkett scoff at she “oh no,” and call it woman’s logic.



she could not restrain herself. “have you forget Mr. Austin, papa?

it be Nevil’s perfect truthfulness that make he appear bad to you

than man who be timeserver. too many time-server rot the State, Mr.

Austin say. Nevil be not one of they. I be not able to judge or

speculate whether he have a great brain or be likely to distinguish

himself out of his profession: I would rather he do not abandon it:

but Mr. Austin say to I in talk of he...”



“that notion of Austin’s of screw woman’s mind up to the pitch of

man’s!” interject the colonel with a despairing flap of his arm.



“he say, papa, that honestly active man in a country, who decline to

practise hypocrisy, show that the blood run, and be a sign of

health.”



“you misunderstood he, my dear.”



“I think I thoroughly understand he. he do not call they wise. he

say they might be dangerous if they be not meet in debate. but he

say, and I presume to think truly, that the reason why they be

decried be, that it be too great a trouble for a lazy world to meet

they. and, he say, the reason why the honest faction agitate be

because they encounter sneer until they appear in force. if they be

meet early, and fairly—I be only quote he—they would not, I think

he say, or would hardly, or would not generally, fall into

professional agitation.”



“Austin’s a speculative Tory, I know; and that’s his weakness,”

observe the colonel. “but I’m certain you misunderstood he. he never

would have call we a lazy people.”



“not in matter of business: in matter of thought.”



“my dear Cecilia! you’ve get hold of a language!... a way of speak!

.... who set you think on these thing?”



“that I owe to Nevil Beauchamp!”



Colonel Halkett indulge in a turn or two up and down the room. he

throw open a window, sniff the moist air, and go to his daughter to

speak to she resolutely.



“between a Radical and a Tory, I don’t know where your head have be

whirl to, my dear. your heart seem to be go: more sorrow for we!

and for Nevil Beauchamp to be pretend to love you while carry on

with this Frenchwoman!”



“he have never say that he love I.”



the splendour of her beauty in humility flash on her father, and he

cry out: “you be too good for any man on earth! we willn’t talk in the

dark, my darling. you tell I he have never, as they say, make love to

you?”



“never, papa.”



“well, that prove the french story. at any rate, he’s a man of honour.

but you love he?”



“the french story be untrue, papa.”



Cecilia stand in a blush like the burn cloud of the sunset.



“tell I frankly: I’m your father, your old dada, your friend, my dear

girl! do you think the man care for you, love you?”



she reply: “I know, papa, the french story be untrue.”



“but when I tell you, silly woman, he confess it to I out of his own

mouth!”



“it be not true now.”



“it’s not go on, you mean? how do you know?”



“I know.”



“have he be swear it?”



“he have not speak of it to I.”



“here I be in a woman’s web!” cry the colonel. “be it your instinct

tell you it’s not true? or what? what? you have not deny that you

love the man.”



“I know he be not immoral.”



“there you shoot again! haven’t you a yes or a no for your father?”



Cecilia cast her arm round his neck, and sob.



she could not bring it to her lip to say (she would have shun the

hearing) that her defence of Beauchamp, which be a shadowed avowal of

the state of her heart, be base on his desire to read to her the

conclusion of Dr. Shrapnel’s letter touch a passion to be overcome;

necessarily therefore a passion that be vanquish, and the full

and brave explanation of his shift treatment of she: nor would she

condescend to urge that her lover would have say he love she when

they be at Steynham, but for the misery and despair of a soul too

noble to be divert from his grief and sense of duty, and, as she

believe, unwilling to speak to win she while his material fortune be

in jeopardy.



the colonel cherish she on his breast, with one hand regularly

pat her shoulder: a form of consolation that cure the disposition

to sob as quickly as would the drip of water.



Cecilia look up into his eye, and say, “we will not be part,

papa, ever.”



the colonel say absently: “no”; and, surprised at himself, add: “no,

certainly not. how can we be part? you willn’t run away from I? no,

you know too well I cann’t resist you. I appeal to your judgement, and I

must accept what you decide. but he be immoral. I repeat that. he have

no root. we shall discover it before it’s too late, I hope.”



Cecilia gaze away, breathe through tremulous dilating nostril.



“one night after dinner at Steynham,” pursue the colonel, “Nevil be

rattle against the Press, with Stukely Culbrett to prime he: and he

say editor of paper be grow to be like priest, and as timid as

priest, and arrogant: and for one thing, it be because they suppose

themselves to be guardian of the national morality. I forget exactly

what the matter be: but he sneer at priest and morality.”



a smile weave round Cecilia’s lip, and in her tower superiority to

one who talk nonsense, she slip out of maiden shame and say:

“Attack Nevil for his political heresy and his wrath with the Press

for not print he. the rest concern his honour, where he be quite

safe, and all be who trust he.”



“if you find out you’re wrong?”



she shake her head.



“but if you find out you’re wrong about he,” her father reiterate

piteously, “you willn’t tear I to strip to have he in spite of it?”



“no, papa, not I. I will not.”



“well, that’s something for I to hold fast to,” say Colonel Halkett,

sigh.









CHAPTER XXXVIII.

LORD AVONLEY





Mr. Everard Romfrey be now, by consent, Lord Avonley, mount on his

direct heirship and ride hard at the earldom. his elevation occur

at a period of life that would have be a season of decay with most

man; but the prolonged and lusty Autumn of the veteran take new fire

from a tangible object to live for. his brother Craven’s death have

slightly stupefy, and it have grieve he: it seem to he peculiarly

pathetic; for as he never calculate on the happening of mortal

accident to man of sound constitution, the circumstance impart a

curious shake to his own solidity. it be like the quaking of earth,

which try the balance of the strong. if he have not be raise to

so splendid a survey of the actual world, he might have be lead to

think of the imaginary, where perchance a man may meet his old dog and

a few other favourite, in a dim perpetual twilight. Thither at all

event Craven have go, and goodnight to he! the earl be a rapidly

lapsing invalid. there could be no doubt that Everard be to be the

head of his House.



outwardly he be the same tolerant gentleman who put aside the poor

fool of the world to walk undisturbed by they in the path he have

choose: in this aspect he know himself: nor be the change so great

within he as to make he cognizant of a change. it be only a secret

turn in the bent of the mind, imperceptible as the touch of the cunning

artist’s brush on a finished portrait, which will alter the expression

without discompose a feature, so that you cannot say it be another

face, yet it be not the former one. his habit be invariable, as be

his meditation. he think less of Romfrey Castle than of his dog and

his device for trap vermin; his interest in bird and beast and

herb, “what ninny call Nature in book,” to quote he, be

undiminishe; imagination he have none to clap wing to his head and be

off with it. he betray as little as he feel that the come Earl of

Romfrey be different from the cadet of the family.



a novel sharpness in the “stop that,” with which he crush Beauchamp’s

affectedly gentle and unusually roundabout opening of the vex

shrapnel question, rang like a shot in the room at Steynham, and

breathe a different spirit from his customary easy pugnacity that

welcome and lure on an adversary to wild outhitting. some sorrowful

preoccupation be, however, to be expect in the man who have lose a

brother, and some degree of irritability at the intrusion of past

dispute. he choose to repeat a similar brief forbidding of the subject

before they start together for the scene of the accident and Romfrey

Castle. no notice be take of Beauchamp’s remark, that he consent to

go though his duty lie elsewhere. beauchamp have not the faculty of

read inside man, or he would have apprehend that his uncle be

engage in silently heap aggravation to shoot forth one fine day a

thunder and astonishing counterstroke.



he should have know his uncle Everard well.



in this respect he seem to have no memory. but who have much that have

give up his brain for a lodging to a single idea? it be at once a

devouring dragon, and an intractable steamforce; it be a tyrant that

have eat up a senate, and a prophet with a message. inspire of

solitariness and gigantic size, it claim divine origin. the world can

have no peace for it.



Cecilia have not please he; none have. he do not bear in mind that the

sight of Dr. Shrapnel sick and weak, which constantly reanimate his

feeling of pity and of wrath, be not give to the other of whom he

demand a corresponding energy of just indignation and sympathy. the

sense that he be leave unaided to the task of bend his tough uncle,

combine with his appreciation of the righteousness of the task to

embitter he and set he on a pedestal, from which he descend at

every sign of an opportunity for strike, and to which he retire

continually baffle and wrathful, in isolation.



then ensue the dreadful division in his conception of his power: for

he who alone see the just and right thing to do, be incapable of

compelling it to be do. Lay on to his uncle as he would, that

wrestler shake he off. and here be one man whom he could not move!

how move a nation?



there come on he a thirst for the harangue of crowd. they agree

with you or they disagree; excite you to activity in either case.

they do not interpose cold Tory exclusiveness and inaccessibility. you

have they in the rough; you have nature in they, and all that be

hopeful in nature. you drive at, over, and through they, for their

good; you plough they. you sow they too. some of they perceive that it

_be_ for their good, and what if they be a minority? ghastly as a

minority be in an Election, in a lifelong struggle it be refreshing and

encouraging. the young world and its triumph be with the minority. oh

to be speak! condemn to silence beside his uncle, Beauchamp chafe

for a loosed tongue and an audience toss like the well-whip

ocean, or open as the smooth sea-surface to the mark of the breeze.

let they be hostile or amicable, he want an audience as hotly as the

hump Richard a horse.



at Romfrey Castle he fall upon an audience that became transform into

a swarm of chatterer, adviser, and reprover the instant his lip

be part. the lady of the family declare his pursuit of the

Apology to be bad and vainer than his politic. the gentleman say

the same, but they be not so outspoken to he personally, and

indulge in aside, with quotation of some of his uncle Everard’s

recent observation concern he: as for example, “politically he’s a

mad harlequin jump his tight and spangle when nobody ask he to

jump; and in private life he’s a mad dentist poke his tong at my

sound tooth:” a highly ludicrous image of the persistent fellow, and a

reminder of situation in Molière, as it be act by Cecil Baskelett

and Lord Welshpool. Beauchamp have to a certain extent restore himself

to favour with his uncle Everard by offer a fair suggestion on the

fatal field to account for the accident, after the latter have take

measurement and examine the place in perplexity. his elucidation of

the puzzle be refer to by Lord Avonley at Romfrey, and finally

accept as possible and this from a wiseacre who go quack about

the county, expect to upset the order of thing in England! such a

mixing of sense and nonsense in a fellow’s noddle be never before meet

with, Lord Avonley say. cecil take the hint. he have be unworrie by

Beauchamp: Dr. Shrapnel have not be mention: and it delight Cecil

to let it be know that he think old Nevil have some good notion,

particularly as to the duty of the aristocracy—that first war-cry of

his when a midshipman. news of another fatal accident in the

hunting-field confirm Cecil’s high opinion of his cousin. on the

day of Craven’s funeral they hear at Romfrey that Mr. Wardour-Devereux

have be kill by a fall from his horse. two english gentleman

despatch by the same agency within a fortnight! “he smoke,” Lord

Avonley say of the second departure, to allay some perturbation in the

bosom of the lady who have cease to ride, by account for this

particular mishap in the most reassuring fashion. cecil’s immediate

reflection be that the unfortunate smoker have leave a rich widow. far

behind in the race for Miss Halkett, and uncertain of a settle

advantage in his other rivalry with Beauchamp, he fix his mind on the

widow, and as Beauchamp do not stand in his way, but on the contrary

might help he—for she, like the generality of woman, admire Nevil

Beauchamp in spite of her feminine good sense and conservatism—Cecil

begin to regard the man he feel less opposed to with some recognition

of his merit. the two nephew accompany Lord Avonley to London, and

sleep at his town-house.



they breakfast together the next morning on friendly term. half an

hour afterward there be an explosion; uncle and nephew be scatter

fragment: and if Cecil be the first to return to cohesion with his

lord and chief, it be, he protest energetically, common policy in a

man in his position to do so: all that he look for be a decent

pension and a share in the use of the town-house. Old Nevil, he

relate, begin cross-examine he and entangle he with the cunning

of the deuce, in my lord’s presence, and having get he to make an

admission, old Nevil fling it at the baron, and even cross he and

stand before he when he be walk out of the room. a furious wrangle

take place. Nevil and the baron give it to one another unmercifully.

the end of it be that all three fly apart, for Cecil confess to
