have a temper, and in contempt of he for the admission wring out of

he, Lord Avonley have prick it. my lord go down to Steynham,

Beauchamp to Holdesbury, and Captain Baskelett to his quarter; whence

in a few day he repair penitently to my lord—the most placable of

man when a full submission be offer to he.



Beauchamp do nothing of the kind. he write a letter to Steynham in the

form of an ultimatum.



this egregious letter be hand to Rosamund for a proof of she

darling’s lunacy. she in conversation with Stukely Culbrett

unhesitatingly accuse Cecil of plot his cousin’s ruin.



Mr. Culbrett think it possible that Cecil have be a little more than

humorous in the part he have play in the dispute, and speak to he.



then it come out that Lord Avonley have also deliver an ultimatum to

Beauchamp.



Time enough have go by for Cecil to forget his ruffling, and relish

the baron’s grandly comic spirit in appropriate that big word

Apology, and demand it from Beauchamp on behalf of the lady ruling

his household. what could be funny than the knocking of Beauchamp’s

blunderbuss out of his hand, and point the muzzle at he!



Cecil dramatize the fun to amuse Mr. Culbrett. apparently Beauchamp

have be stagger on hear himself ask for the definite article he

claim. he have make a point of speaking of _the_ apology. Lord Avonley

do likewise. and each profess to exact it for a deeply aggrieve

person: each put it on the ground that it involve the other’s rightful

ownership of the title of gentleman.



‘“an apology to the amiable and virtuous Mistress Culling?’ say old

Nevil: ‘an apology? what for?’—‘For unbecoming and insolent behaviour,’

say my lord.”



“I be that lady’s friend,” stukely warn Captain Baskelett. “don’t let

we have a third apology in the field.”



“perfectly true; you be her friend, and you know what a friend of mine

she be,” rejoin Cecil. “I could swear ‘that lady’ fling the whole

affair at I. I give you my word, old Nevil and I be on a capital

footing before he and the baron break up. I praise he for tickle

the aristocracy. I back he heartily; I do now; I’ll do it in

Parliament. I know a case of a noble lord, a General in the army, and

he receive an intimation that he might as well attend the Prussian

cavalry manœuvre last Autumn on the Lower Rhine or in Silesia—no

matter where. he couldn’t go: he be engage to shoot bird! I give you

my word. now there I see old Nevil’s right. it’s as well we should know

something about the prussian and austrian cavalry, and if our

aristocracy willn’t go abroad to study cavalry, who be to? no class in

the kingdom understand horse as they do. my opinion be, they’re

asleep. nevil should have stick to that, instead of try to galvanize

the country and turn against his class. but fancy old Nevil ask

for the apology! it petrify he. ‘I’ve tell she nothing but the

truth,’ say Nevil. ‘tell the truth to woman be an impertinence,’

say my lord. Nevil swear he’d have a revolution in the country before

he apologize.”



Mr. Culbrett smile at the absurdity of the change of position between

Beauchamp and his uncle Everard, which remind he somewhat of the old

story of the highwayman innkeeper and the market farmer who have be

thoughtful enough to recharge his pistol after quit the inn at

midnight. a practical “tu quoque” be astonishingly laughable, and

back by a high figure and manner it have the flavour of triumphant

repartee. Lord Avonley do not speak of it as a retort upon Nevil,

though he reiterate the word Apology amusingly. he put it as due to

the lady govern his household; and his ultimatum be, that the

Apology should be deliver in term to satisfy _he_ within three

month of the date of the demand for it: otherwise blank; but the

shadowy index point to the destitution of Nevil Beauchamp.



no stroke of retributive misfortune could have be severe to Rosamund

than to be thrust forward as the object of humiliation for the man she

love. she see at a glance how much more likely it be (remote as the

possibility appear) that her lord would perform the act of penitence

than her beloved nevil. and she have no occasion to ask herself why.

Lord Avonley have do wrong, and Nevil have not. it be inconceivable

that Nevil should apologize to she. it be horrible to picture the act

in her mind. she be a very rational woman, quite a woman of the world,

yet such be her situation between these two man that the childish tale

of a close and consecutive punishment for sin, down to our little

naughtiness and naturalness, enslave her intelligence, and amaze

she with the example make of she, as it be to prove the tale true of

our be surely haul back like domestic animal learn the habit

of good society, to the rueful contemplation of certain of our deed,

however wildly we appeal to nature to stand up for they.



but be it so with all of we? no, think Rosamund, sink dejectedly

from a recognition of the heavenliness of the justice which lash she

and Nevil, and do not scourge Cecil Baskelett. that fine eye for

celestially direct consequence be ever haunt by shadow of unfaith

likely to obscure it completely when chastisement be not see to fall

on the person whose wickedness be evident to we. it have be

establish that we do not wax diviner by drag down the Gods to our

level.



Rosamund know Lord Avonley too well to harass he with further

petition and explanation. equally vain be it to attempt to persuade

Beauchamp. he make use of the house in London, where he meet his uncle

occasionally, and he call at Steynham for money, that he could have

obtain upon the one condition, which be no soon mention than

fiery word fly in the room, and the two separate. the leaden look in

Beauchamp, notice by Cecilia Halkett in their late interview, be

deepen, and be of itself a displeasure to Lord Avonley, who liked

flourish face, and say: “that fellow’s get the look of a

sweat smith”: presumptively in the act of heat his poker at the

furnace to stir the country.



it now become an offence to he that Beauchamp should continue do

this in the speech and lecture he be report to be deliver; he

stamp his foot at the sight of his nephew’s name in the daily

journal; a novel sentiment of social indignation be express by his

cry out, at the next request for money: “money to prime you to turn

the country into a rat-hole? not a square inch of pennsylvanian

paper-bond! what right have you to be lecture and oratione? you’ve

no knowledge. all you’ve get be your instinct, and that you show in

your readiness to exhibit they like a monkey. you ought to be turn

inside out on your own stage. you’ve lump your brain on a point or

two about Land, and Commonland, and the Suffrage, and you pound away

upon they, as if you have the key of the difficulty. it’s the

Scotchman’s metaphysics; you know nothing clear, and your

work-class know nothing at all; and you blow they with wind like

an over-stuff cow. what you’re drive at be to get hob-nail boot to

dance on our head. stukely say you should be off over to Ireland.

there you’d swim in your element, and have speechify from instinct,

and howl and pummel too, enough to last you out. I’ll hand you

money for that expedition. you’re one above the number want here.

you’ve a look of bad powder fit only to flash in the pan. I save you

from the post of public donkey, by keep you out of Parliament.

you’re bray and kick your bad for it still at these meeting of

your. a naval officer preach about Republicanism and parcel out

the land!”



Beauchamp reply quietly, “the lecture I read be Dr. Shrapnel’s.

when I speak I have his knowledge to back my deficiency. he be too

ill to work, and I consider it my duty to do as much of his work as I

can undertake.”



“ha! you’re the old infidel’s Amen clerk. it would rather astonish

orthodox congregation to see clerk in our church get into the

pulpit to read the sermon for sick clergyman,” say Lord Avonley. his

countenance furrow. “I’ll pay that bill,” he add.



“pay down half a million!” thunder Beauchamp; and drop his voice,

“or go to he.”



“you remind I,” his uncle observe. “I recommend you to ring that

bell, and have Mrs. Culling here.”



“if she come she will hear what I think of she.”



“then, out of the house!”



“very well, sir. you decline to supply I with money?”



“I do.”



“I must have it!”



“I dare say. money’s a chain-cable for hold man to their sense.”



“I ask you, my lord, how I be to carry on Holdesbury?”



“give it up.”



“I shall have to,” say Beauchamp, strive to be prudent.



“there ben’t a doubt of it,” say his uncle, upon a series of nod

diminishing in their depth until his head assume a droll interrogative

fixity, with an air of “what next?”









CHAPTER XXXIX.

between BEAUCHAMP and CECILIA





Beauchamp quit the house without answer as to what next, and

without see Rosamund.



in the matter of money, as of his physical health, he want to do too

much at once; he have spend largely of both in his effort to repair the

injury do to Dr. Shrapnel. he be overwork, anxious, restless,

craving for a holiday somewhere in France, possibly; he be all but

leap on board the boat at time, and, unwilling to leave his dear

old friend who cling to he, he stay, keep his impulse below the

tide-mark which lead to action, but where they do not yield peace of

spirit. the tone of Renée’s letter fill he with misgiving. she

write word that she have see M. d’Henriel for the first time since his

return from Italy, and he be much change, and incline to thank

Roland for the lesson he have receive from he at the sword’s point.

and next she urge Beauchamp to marry, so that he and she might meet,

as if she feel a necessity for it. “I shall love your wife; teach she

to think amiably of I,” she say. and her letter contain womanly

sympathy for he in his battle with his uncle. beauchamp think of his

experience of Cecilia’s comparative coldness. he reply that there

be no prospect of his marrying; he wish there be one of meeting!

he forbore from write too fervently, but he allude to happy day in

Normandy, and propose to renew they if she would say she have need of

he. he entreat she to deal with he frankly; he remind she that

she must constantly look to he, as she have vow she would, when in

any kind of trouble; and he declare to she that he be unchanged. he

mean, of an unchanged disposition to shield and serve she; but the

review of her situation, and his knowledge of her quick blood, work

he to some jealous lover’s throb, which lead he to impress his

unchangeableness upon she, to bind she to that standard.



she decline his visit: not now; “not yet”: and for that he presume to

chide she, half-sincerely. as far as he know he stand against everybody

save his old friend and Renée; and she certainly would have refresh

his heart for a day. in writing, however, he have an ominous vision of

the morrow to the day; and, both for her sake and his own, he be not

unrejoiced to hear that she be engaged day and night in nursing her

husband. pursue his vision of the morrow of an unreproachful day with

Renée, the madness of take she to himself, should she surrender at

last to a third persuasion, strike he sharply, now that he and his

uncle be foot to foot in downright conflict, and money be the

question. he have not much remain of his inheritance—about fifteen

hundred pound. he would have to vacate Holdesbury and his uncle’s

town-house in a month. let his passion be never so desperate, for a

beggared man to think of run away with a wife, or of marry one,

the folly be as big as the worldly offence: no justification be to be

imagine. Nay, and there be no justification for the breach of a moral

law. Beauchamp own it, and feel that Renée’s resistance to he in

Normandy place she above he. he remember a saying of his moralist:

“we who interpret thing heavenly by thing earthly must not hope to

juggle with they for our pleasure, and can look to no absolution of

evil act.” the school be a hard one. it deny he holiday; it cut

he off from dream. it run he in heavy harness on a rough highroad,

allow no turning to right or leave, no wayside cropping; with the

simple permission to he that he should daily get thoroughly tired. and

what be it Jenny Denham have say on the election day? “do incessant

battling keep the intellect clear?”



his mind be clear enough to put the case, that either he behold a

tremendous magnification of thing, or else that other man do not

attach common importance to they; and he decide that the latter be

the fact.



an incessant struggle of one man with the world, which position usually

rank his relative against he, do not conduce to soundness of

judgement. he may nevertheless be right in consider that he be right

in the main. the world in motion be not so wise that it can pretend to

silence the outcry of an ordinarily generous heart even—the very infant

of antagonism to its method and establishment. it be not so difficult

to be right against the world when the heart be really active; but the

world be our book of humanity, and before insist that _his_

handwriting shall occupy the next blank page of it, the noble rebel be

bind for the sake of his aim to ask himself how much of a giant he be,

lest he fall like a blot on the page, instead of inscribe

intelligible character there.



moreover, his relative be present to assure he that he do not jump

out of Jupiter’s head or come of the doctor. they hang on he like an

ill-condition prickly garment; and if he complain of the irritation

they cause he, they one and all denounce his irritable skin.



fret by his relative he cannot be much of a giant.



Beauchamp look from Dr. Shrapnel in his invalid’s chair to his uncle

Everard breathe robustly, and mix his uncle’s error with those of

the world which honour and uphold he. his remainder of equability

depart; his impatience increase. his appetite for work at Dr.

Shrapnel’s writing-desk be voracious. he be ready for any labour, the

transcribing of paper, write from dictation, whatsoever be of

service to Lord Avonley’s victim: and he be not like the spartan boy

with the wolf at his vital; he betray it in the hue his uncle

Everard detest, in a visible nervousness, and indulgence in fit of

scorn. sharp epigram and note of irony provoke his laughter more

than fun. he seem to acquiesce in some of the current contemporary

despair of our immoveable England, though he wince at a satire on his

country, and attempt to show that the dull dominant class of

moneymaker be the ruin of she. wherever he stand to represent Dr.

Shrapnel, as against Mr. Grancey Lespel on account of the Itchincope

encroachment, he leave a sting that spread the rumour of his having

become not only a black torch of Radicalism—our modern provincial

estateholder and their wife bestow that reputation lightly—but a

gentleman with the polish scratch off he in part. and he, though

individually he do not understand how there be to be game in the land

if game-preserving be abolish, sign his name R. C. S. NEVIL

BEAUCHAMP for DR. SHRAPNEL, in the communication direct to

solicitor of the persecutor of poacher.



his behaviour to Grancey Lespel be eclipse by his treatment of

Captain Baskelett. Cecil have ample reason to suppose his cousin to be

friendly with he. he himself have forget Dr. Shrapnel, and all other

dissension, in a supremely christian spirit. he pay his cousin the

compliment to think that he have do likewise. at Romfrey and in London

he have speak to Nevil of his design upon the widow: Nevil say

nothing against it and it be under Mrs. Wardour-Devereux’s eye, and

before a man name Lydiard, that, never call to he to put he on

his guard, Nevil fall foul of he with every capital charge that can be

bring against a gentleman, and do so abuse, worry, and disgrace he

as to reduce he to quit the house to avoid the scandal of a resort to

a gentleman’s last appeal in vindication of his character. Mrs.

Devereux speak of the terrible scene to Cecilia, and Lydiard to Miss

Denham. the injured person communicate it to Lord Avonley, who tell

Colonel Halkett emphatically that his nephew Cecil deserve well of he

in having keep command of his temper out of consideration for the

family. there be a general murmur of the family over this incident.

the widow be rich, and it rank among the unwritten crime against

blood for one offshoot of a great house wantonly to thwart another in

the wooing of she by humble he in her presence, do his utmost to

expose he as a schemer, a culprit, and a poltroon.



could it be that Beauchamp have reserve his wrath with his cousin to

avenge Dr. Shrapnel upon he signally? Miss Denham fear her guardian

be the cause. Lydiard be indefinitely of her opinion. the idea strike

Cecilia Halkett, and as an example of Beauchamp’s tenacity of purpose

and sureness of aim it fascinate she. but Mrs. Wardour-Devereux do

not appear to share it. she object to Beauchamp’s intemperateness and

unsparingness, as if she be for convey a sisterly warning to

Cecilia; and that be off her mind, she add, smile a little and

colour a little: “we learn only from man what man be.” how the

scene commence and whether it be provoke, she fail to recollect.

she describe Beauchamp as very self-contain in manner throughout his

tongue be the scorpion. Cecilia fancy he must have resemble his

uncle Everard.



Cecilia be conquer, but unclaimed. while support and approve

he in her heart she be dread to receive some new problem of his

conduct; and still while she blame he for not seek an interview

with she, she like he for this instance of delicacy in the present

state of his relation with Lord Avonley.



a problem of her own conduct disturb the young lady’s clear

conception of herself: and this be a ruffling of unfaithfulness in her

love of Beauchamp, that be betray to she by her forgetfulness of he

whenever she chance to be with Seymour Austin. in Mr. Austin’s company

she recover her forfeited repose, her poetry of life, her image of

the independent Cecilia throne above our dust of battle, gaze on

broad heaven. she carry the feeling so far that Blackburn Tuckham’s

enthusiasm for Mr. Austin give he grace in her sight, and praise of

her father’s favourite from Mr. Austin’s mouth make he welcome to she.

the image of that grave capable head, dusty-grey about the temple, and

the darkly sanguine face of the try man, which be that of a season

warrior and inspire full trust in he, with his vivid look, his

personal distinction, his plain devotion to the country’s business, and

the domestic solitude he live in, admire, esteemed, love perhaps,

but unpartnered, be often her refuge and haven from tempestuous

Beauchamp. she could see in vision the pride of Seymour Austin’s mate.

it flush she reflectively. conquer but not claim, Cecilia be

like the frozen earth insensibly move round to sunshine in nature,

with one white flower in her breast as innocent a sign of strong sweet

blood as a woman may wear. she ascribe to that fair mate of Seymour

Austin’s many lofty charm of womanhood; above all, stateliness: her

especial dream of an attainable superlative beauty in woman. and

suppose that lady to be accuse of the fickle breaking of another

love, who walk beside he, match with his calm heart and one with

he in counsel, would the accusation be repeat by they that behold

her husband? might it not rather be say that she have not deviate, but

have only step higher? she choose no youth, no glistener, no idler: it

be her soul strive upward to air like a seed in the earth that

raise she to he: and she could say to the man once enchain she:

Friend, by the good you teach I I be lead to this!



Cecilia’s reverie flee like column of mist before the gale when

tiding reach she of a positive rupture between Lord Avonley and

Nevil Beauchamp, and of the mandate to he to quit possession of

Holdesbury and the London house within a certain number of day,

because of his refusal to utter an apology to Mrs. Culling. angrily on

his behalf she prepare to humble herself to he. Louise

Wardour-Devereux bring they to a meeting, at which Cecilia, with her

heart in her hand, be icy. Mr. Lydiard, prompt by Mrs. Devereux,

give he well reason for her singular coldness than Cecilia could

give to herself, and some time afterward Beauchamp go to Mount

Laurels, where Colonel Halkett mount guard over his daughter, and

behave, to her thinking, cruelly. “now you have ruin yourself

there’s nothing ahead for you but to go to the Admiralty and apply for

a ship,” he say, sugar the unkindness with the remark that the

country would be the gainer. he let fly a side-shot at London man

call themselves military man who seek to repair their fortune by

chase wealthy widow, and compliment Beauchamp: “you’re not one of

that sort.”



Cecilia look at Beauchamp stedfastly. “speak,” say the look.



but he, though not blind, be keenly wound.



“money I must have,” he say, half to the colonel, half to himself.



Colonel Halkett shrug. Cecilia wait for a directness in

Beauchamp’s eye.



her father be too wary to leave they.



Cecilia’s intuition tell she that by lead to a discussion of

politic, and adopt Beauchamp’s view, she could kindle he. why do

she refrain? it be that the conquered young lady be a captive, not an

ally. to touch the subject in cold blood, voluntarily to launch on

those vex water, as if his cause be her heart’s, as much as her

heart be the man’s, she feel to be impossible. he at the same time

feel that the heiress, endow he with money to speed the good cause,

should be his match in ardour for it, otherwise he be but a common

adventurer, win and despoil an heiress.



they meet in London. Beauchamp have not vacate either Holdesbury or the

town-house; he be defy his uncle Everard, and Cecilia think with

he that it be a wise temerity. she think with he passively

altogether. on this occasion she have not to wait for directness in his

eye; she have to parry it. they be at a dinner-party at Lady Elsea’s,

generally the last place for see Lord Palmet, but he be present,

and arrange thing neatly for they, tell Beauchamp that he act

under Mrs. Wardour-Devereux’s order. never be an opportunity, more

propitious for a desperate lover. have it be Renée next he, no petty

worldly scruple of honour would have hold he back. and if Cecilia have

speak feelingly of Dr. Shrapnel, or have she simulate a thoughtful

interest in his pursuit, his hesitation would have vanish. as it

be, he dare to look what he do not permit himself to speak. she be

nobly lovely, and the palpable envy of man around cry fool at his

delay. Beggar and heiress he say in his heart, to vitalize the

three-part fiction of the point of honour which Cecilia’s beauty be

fast submerging. when she be leave he name a day for call to see

she. Colonel Halkett stand by, and she answer, “come.”



Beauchamp keep the appointment. Cecilia be absent.



he be unaware that her father have take she to old Mrs. Beauchamp’s

death-bed. her absence, after she have say, “come,” appear a

confirmation of her glacial manner when they meet at the house of Mrs.

Wardour-Devereux; and he charge she with waywardness. a wound of the

same kind that we be inflict be about the severest we can feel.



Beauchamp receive intelligence of his venerable great-aunt’s death

from Blackburn Tuckham, and after the funeral he be inform that

eighty thousand pound have be bequeath to he: a goodly sum of

money for a gentleman recently beggar; yet, as the political

enthusiast could not help reckon (apart from a fervent sentiment of

gratitude toward his benefactress), scarcely enough to do much more

than start and push for three or more year a command daily

newspaper, devote to radical interest, and to be entitle the DAWN.



true, he might now conscientiously approach the heiress, take her hand

with an open countenance, and retain it.



could he do so quite conscientiously? the point of honour have be

centre in his condition of beggary. something still be in his way. a

quick spring of his blood for air, motion, excitement, holiday freedom,

send his thought travel whither they always shoot away when his

redoubtable natural temper break loose.



in the case of any other woman than Cecilia Halkett he would not have

be obstruct by the minor consideration as to whether he be wholly

heart-free to ask she in marriage that instant; for there be no

hindrance, and she be beautiful. she be exceedingly beautiful; and

she be an unequalled heiress. she would be able with her wealth to

float his newspaper, the DAWN, so desire of Dr. Shrapnel!—the good

restorative that could be apply to he! every temptation came

supplicate he to take the step which indeed he wish for: one

feel opposed. he really respect Cecilia: it be not too much to say

that he worship she with the devout worship render to the ideal

Englishwoman by the heart of the nation. for he she be purity,

charity, the keeper of the key of whatsoever be hold precious by man;

she be a midway saint, a light between day and darkness, in whom the

spirit in the flesh shine like the grow star amid thin sanguine

colour, the sweeter, the brighter, the more translucent the long

know. and if the image will allow it, the nearer down to he the

holier she seem.



how offer himself when he be not perfectly certain that he be worthy

of she?



some jugglery be play by the adept male heart in these later

hesitation. up to the extent of his knowledge of himself, the man be

fairly sincere. passion would have speed he to Cecilia, but passion be

not invariably love; and we know what it can be.



the glance he cast over the water at Normandy be withdraw. he go to

Bevisham to consult with Dr. Shrapnel about the starting of a weekly

journal, instead of a daily, and a name for it—a serious question: for

though it be oftener weekly than daily that the dawn be visible in

England, title must not invite the public jest; and the glorious

project of the daily DAWN be prudently abandon for by-and-by. he

think himself rich enough to put a radical champion weekly in the

field and this matter, except the title, be arrange in Bevisham.

Thence he proceed to Holdesbury, where he hear that the house,

ground, and farm be let to a tenant prepare to enter. Indifferent

to the blow, he keep an engagement to deliver a speech at the great

manufacturing town of Gunningham, and then go to London, visit his

uncle’s town-house for recent letter. not one be from Renée: she have

not write for six week, not once for his thrice! a letter from Cecil

Baskelett inform he that “my lord” have place the town-house at his

disposal. return to dress for dinner on a thick and murky evening of

February, Beauchamp encounter his cousin on the step. he say to

Cecil, “I sleep here to-night: I leave the house to you tomorrow.”



Cecil strike out his underjaw to reply: “oh! good. you sleep here

to-night. you be a fortunate man. I congratulate you. I shall not

disturb you. I have just enter on my occupation of the house. I have

my key. allow I to recommend you to go straight to the drawing-room.

and I may inform you that the Earl of Romfrey be at the point of death.

my lord be at the castle.”



Cecil accompany his descent of the step with the humming of an opera

melody: Beauchamp trip into the hall-passage. a young maid-servant

hold the door open, and she accost he: “if you please, there be a

lady up-stair in the drawing-room; she speak foreign English, sir.”



Beauchamp ask if the lady be alone, and not wait for the answer,

though he listen while write, and hear that she be heavily

veil, he tear a strip from his notebook, and carefully trace

half-a-dozen telegraphic word to Mrs. Culling at Steynham. his rarely

fail promptness, which be like an inspiration, to conceive and

execute measure for avert peril, set he on the thought of possibly

counteract his cousin Cecil’s malignant tongue by mean of a message

to Rosamund, summon she by telegraph to come to town by the next

train that night. he despatch the old woman keep the house, as

trusty than the young one, to the near office, and go up to the

drawing-room, with a quick thumping heart that be nevertheless as

little apprehensive of an especial trial and danger as if he have do

nothing at all to obviate it. indeed he forget that he have do

anything when he turn the handle of the drawing-room door.









CHAPTER XL.

a trial of HIM





a low-burn lamp and fire cast a narrow ring on the shadow of the

dusky London room. one of the window-blind be draw up. beauchamp

discern a shape at that window, and the fear seize he that it might

be Madame d’Auffray with evil news of Renée: but it be Renée’s name he

call. she rise from her chair, say, “I!”



she be tremble.



Beauchamp ask she whisperingly if she have come alone.



“alone; without even a maid,” she murmur.



he pull down the blind of the window expose they to the square, and

lead she into the light to see her face.



the dimness of light annoy he, and the miserable reception of she;

this english weather, and the gloomy house! and how long have she be

wait for he? and what be the mystery? Renée in England seem

magical; yet it be nothing strange than an old dream realize. he

wind up the lamp, hold she still with one hand. she be woefully

pale; scarcely able to bear the increase of light.



“it be I who come to you”: she be half audible.



“this time!” say he. “you have be suffer?”



“no.”



her tone be brief; not reassure.



“you come straight to I?”



“without a deviation that I know of.”



“from Tourdestelle?”



“you have not forget Tourdestelle, Nevil?”



the memory of it quicken his rapture in read her feature. it be

his first love, his enchantress, who be here: and how? conjecture

shoot through he like lightning in the dark.



irrationally, at a moment when reason stand in awe, he fancy it must

be that her husband be dead. he force himself to think it, and could

have smile at the hurry of her come, one, without even a maid: and

deep down in he the devouring question burn which dread the

answer.



but of old, in Normandy, she have pledge herself to join he with no

delay when free, if ever free!



so now she be free.



one side of he glow in illumination; the other be black as Winter

night; but light subdue darkness; and in a situation like Beauchamp’s,

the blood be lively than the prophetic mind.



“why do you tell I to marry? what do that mean?” say he. “do you

wish I to be the one in chain? and you have come quite alone!—you

will give I an account of everything presently:—You be here! in

England! and what a welcome for you! you be cold.”



“I be warmly clothe,” say Renée, suffer her hand to be draw to his

breast at her arm’s-length, not bend with it.



alive to his own indirectness, he be conscious at once of the slight

sign of reservation, and say: “tell I...” and swerve sheer away from

his question: “how be Madame d’Auffray?”



“Agnès? I leave she at Tourdestelle,” say Renée.



“and Roland? he never write to I.”



“neither he nor I write much. he be at the military camp of instruction

in the North.”



“he will run over to we.”



“do not expect it.”



“why not?”



Renée sigh. “we shall have to live long than I look for...” she

stop. “why do you ask I why not? he be fond of we both, and sorry

for we; but have you forget Roland that morning on the Adriatic?”



Beauchamp press her hand. the stroke of then and now rang in his

breast like a bell instead of a bound heart. something have stun

his heart. he have no clear central feeling; he try to gather it from

her touch, from his joy in behold she and sit with she alone,

from the grace of her figure, the wild sweetness of her eye, and the

beloved foreign lip bewitch he with their exquisite french and

perfection of speech.



his nature be too prompt in respond to such a call on it for

resolute warmth.



“if I have be firm then, or you one year old!” he say.



“that girl in Venice have no courage,” say Renée.



she raise her head and look about the room.



her instinct of love sound her lover through, and feel the deficiency

or the contrariety in he, as surely as musical ear be pain by a

discord that they require no touchstone to detect. passion have the

sensitiveness of fever, and be as cruelly chill by a tepid air.



“yes, a London house after Venice and Normandy!” say Beauchamp,

follow her look.



“sicily: do not omit Syracuse; you be in your naval uniform: Normandy

be our third meeting,” say Renée. “this be the fourth. I should have

reckon that.”



“why? superstitiously?”



“we cannot be entirely wise when we have stake our fate. sailor be

credulous: you know they. woman be like they when they embark... three

chance! who can boast of so many, and expect one more! will you take

I to my hotel, Nevil?”



the fiction of she be free could not be sustain.



“take you and leave you? I be absolutely at your command. but leave

you? you be alone: and you have tell I nothing.”



what be there to tell? the desperate act be apparent, and tell all.



Renée’s dark eyelash lift on he, and drop.



“then thing be as I leave they in Normandy?” say he.



she reply: “almost.”



he quiver at the solitary word; for his conscience be on edge. it

run the shrewd irony through he, inexplicably. “almost”: that be,

“with this poor difference of one person, now find herself

worthless, subtract from the list; no other; it should be little to

they as it be little to you”: or, reverse it, the substance of the

word become magnify and intensify by its humble slightness: “thing

be the same, but for the jewel of the province, a lustre of France,

lure hither to her eclipse”—meanings various, indistinguishable,

thrilling and pierce sad as the half-tone hum round the note of

a strung wire, which be a blunt single note to the common ear.



Beauchamp spring to his foot and bend above she: “you have come to I,

for the love of I, to give yourself to I, and for ever, for good,

till death? Speak, my beloved Renée.”



her eye be raise to his: “you see I here. it be for you to speak.”



“I do. there’s nothing I ask for now—if the step cann’t be retrieve.”



“the step retrieve, my friend? there be no step backward in life.”



“I be think of you, Renée.”



“yes, I know,” she answer hurriedly.



“if we discover that the step be a wrong one?” he pursue: “why be

there no step backward?”



“I be talk of woman,” say Renée.



“why not for woman?”



“honourable woman, I mean,” say Renée.



Beauchamp incline to forget his position in find matter to contest.



yet it be beyond contest that there be no step backward in life. she

speak well; well than he, and she win his deference by it. not only

she speak well: she be truer, distincter, braver: and a man ever on

the look-out for superior quality, and ready to bow to they, could

not refuse her homage. with that a save sense of power quit he.



“you write to I that you be unchanged, Nevil.”



“I be.”



“so, then, I come.”



his rejoinder be the dumb one, commonly eloquent and satisfactory.



Renée shut her eye with a painful rigour of endurance. she open they

to look at he steadily.



the desperate act of her flight demand immediate recognition from he

in simple language and a practical seconding of it. there be the test.



“I cannot stay in this house, Nevil; take I away.”



she name her hotel in her french English, and the sound of it

penetrate he with remorseful pity. it be for he, and of his doing,

that she be in an alien land and an outcast!



“this house be wretched for you,” say he: “and you must be hungry. let

I...”



“I cannot eat. I will ask you”: she pause, draw on her energy,

and keep down the throb of her heart: “this: do you love I?”



“I love you with all my heart and soul.”



“as in Normandy?”



“yes.”



“in Venice?”



“as from the first, Renée! that I can swear.”



“oath be foolish. I mean to ask you—my friend, there be no question

in my mind of any other woman: I see you love I: I be so used to

consider myself the vain and cowardly creature, and you the bold and

faithfull of man, that I could not abandon the habit if I would: I

start confide in you, sure that I should come to land. but I have

to ask you: to I you be truth: I have no claim on my lover for

anything but the answer to this:—Am I a burden to you?”



his brow fly up in furrow. he draw a heavy breath, for never have he

love she more admiringly, and never on such equal term. she be his

mate in love and dare at least. a sorrowful comparison strike he, of

a little boat sail out to a vessel in deep sea and leave to founder.



without knot his mind to acknowledge or deny the burden, for he

could do neither, he stand silent, stare at she, not so much in

weakness as in positive mental division. no, would be false; and yes,

not less false; and if the step be irretrievable, to say yes would be

to plunge a dagger in her bosom; but no be a vain deceit involve a

double wreck. assuredly a man stand against the world in a good

cause, with a runaway wife on his hand, carry a burden, however

precious it be to he.



a smile of her lip, part in an anguish of expectancy, go to death

over Renée’s face. she look at he tenderly. “the truth,” she

murmur to herself, and her eyelid fall.



“I be ready to bear anything,” say Beauchamp. “I weigh what you ask

I, that be all. you a burden to I? but when you ask I, you make I

turn round and inquire how we stand before the world.”



“the world do not stone man,” say Renée.



“cann’t I make you feel that I be not think of myself?” Beauchamp

stamp in his extreme perplexity. he be gag; he could not possibly

talk to she, who have cast the die, of his later notion of morality and

the world’s due, fee, and claim on we.



“no, friend, I be not complain.” Renée put out her hand to he; with

compassionate irony feign to have hear excuse. “what right have I

to complain? I have not the sensation. I could not expect you to be

everlastingly the sentinel of love. three time I reject you! now

that I have lose my father—oh! poor father: I trifle with my lover, I

trick he that my father might live in peace. he be dead. I wish

you to marry one of your own countrywoman, Nevil. you say it be

impossible; and I, with my snake at my heart, and a husband grateful

for nursing and whimpering to I for his youth like a beggar on the

road, I think I owe you this debt of body and soul, to prove to you

I have some courage; and for myself, to reward myself for my long

captivity and misery with one year of life: and adieu to Roland my

brother! adieu to friend! adieu to France! Italy be our home. I

dream of one year in Italy; I fancy it might be two; more than that

be unimaginable. prisoner of long date do not hope; they do not

calculate: air, light, they say; to breathe freely and drop down! they

be reduce to the instinct of the beast. I think I might give you

happiness, pay part of my debt to you. be you remember Count Henri?

that paint what I be! I could fly to that for a taste of life! a

dance to death! and again you ask: why, if I love you then, not turn

to you in preference? no, you have answer it yourself, Nevil;—on that

day in the boat, when generosity in a man so surprise I, it seem a

miracle to I; and it be, in its divination. how I thank my dear

brother Roland for save I the sight of you condemn to fight,

against your conscience! he teach poor M. d’Henriel his lesson. you,

Nevil, be my teacher. and see how it hang: there be mercy for I in

not having draw down my father’s anger on my heart’s beloved. he love

you. he pity we. he reproach himself. in his last day he be

teach to suspect our story: perhaps from Roland; perhaps I breathe it

without speak. he call heaven’s blessing on you. he speak of you

with tear, clutch my hand. he make I feel he would have cry out:

‘if I be leave she with Nevil Beauchamp!’ and ‘beauchamp,’ I hear

he murmur once: ‘take down Froissart’: he name a chapter. it be

curious: if he utter my name Renée, your, ‘nevil,’ soon follow.

that be notice by Roland. hope for we, he could not have have; as

little as I! but we be his two: his child. I bury he—I think

he would know our innocence, and now pardon our love. I read your

letter, from my name at the beginning, to yours at the end, and from

yours back to mine, and between the line, for any doubtful spot: and

oh, rash! but I would not retrace the step for my own sake. I be

certain of your love for I, though...” she pause: “yes, I be certain

of it. and if I be a burden to you?”



“about as much as the air, which I cann’t do without since I begin to

breathe it,” say Beauchamp, more clear-mindedly now that he suppose

he be address a mind, and with a peril to himself that escape his

vigilance. there be a secret intoxication for he already in the

half-certainty that the step could not be retrace. the idea that he

might reason with she, make she seductive to the heart and head of he.



“I be passably rich, Nevil,” she say. “I do not care for money, except

that it give wing. Roland inherit the château in Touraine. I have

one in Burgundy, and rente and share, my notary inform I.”



“I have money,” say he. his heart begin beat violently. he lose

sight of his intention of reasoning. “Good God! if you be free!”



she falter: “at Tourdestelle...”



“yes, and I _be_ unchanged,” Beauchamp cry out. “your life there be

horrible, and mine’s intolerable.” he stretch his arm cramp like

the yawning of a wretch in fetter. that which he would and would not

become so intervolved that he deem it reasonable to instance their

common misery as a ground for their union against the world. and what

have that world do for we, that a joy so immeasurable should be

reject on its behalf? and what have we succeed in do, that the

childish effort to move it should be continue at such a cost?



for year, down to one year back, and less—yesterday, it could be

say—all human blessedness appear to he in the person of Renée,

give he under any condition whatsoever. she be not less adorable

now. in her decision, and a courage that he especially prize in woman,

she be a sweet to he than when he be with she in France: too sweet

to be look at and refuse.



“but we must live in England,” he cry abruptly out of his inner mind.



“oh! not England, Italy, Italy!” Renée exclaim: “Italy, or Greece:

anywhere where we have sunlight. mountain and valley be my dream.

promise it, Nevil. I will obey you; but this be my wish. take I

through Venice, that I may look at myself and wonder. we can live at

sea, in a yacht; anywhere with you but in England. this country frown

on I; I can hardly fetch my breath here, I be suffocate. the people

all walk in line in England. not here, Nevil! they be good people, I

be sure; and it be your country: but their face chill I, their voice

grate; I should never understand they; they would be to I like their

fog eternally; and I to they? o I! it would be like hear sentence

in the dampness of the shroud perpetually. again I say I do not doubt

that they be very good: they claim to be; they judge other; they may

know how to make themselves happy in their climate; it be common to

most creature to do so, or to imagine it. nevil! not England!”



truly “the mad commander and his french marquise” of the Bevisham

Election ballad would make a pretty figure in England!



his friend of his own class would be mouth it. the story would be a

dog shadow of his public life, and, quite as bad, a reflection on

his party. he hear the yelp tongue of the cynic. he see the

consternation and grief of his old Bevisham hero, his leader and his

teacher.



“Florence,” he say, muse on the prospect of exile and idleness:

“there’s a kind of society to be have in Florence.”



Renée ask he if he care so much for society.



he reply that woman must have it, just as man must have exercise.



“old woman, Nevil; intriguer, tattler.”



“young woman, Renée.”



she signify no.



he shake the head of superior knowledge paternally.



her instinct of comedy set a dimple faintly work in her cheek.



“not if they love, Nevil.”



“at least,” say he, “a man do not like to see the woman he love

banish by society and browbeaten.”



“put I aside, do you care for it, Nevil?”



“personally not a jot.”



“I be convince of that,” say Renée.



she speak suspiciously sweetly, appear perfect candour.



the change in he be perceptible to she. the nature of the change be

unfathomable.



she try her wit at the riddle. but though she could be an actress

before he with little difficulty, the torment of her situation rouse

the fever within she at a bare effort to think acutely. Scarlet

suffuse her face: her brain whirl.



“remember, dearest, I have but offer myself: you have your choice. I

can pass on. yes, I know well I speak to Nevil Beauchamp; you have

drill I to trust you and your word as a soldier trust to his

officer—once a faint-hearted soldier! I need not remind you: front

the enemy now, in hard truth. but I want your whole heart to decide.

give I no silly compassion! would it have be well to I to have

write to you? if I have write I should have clip my glorious

impulse, bring myself down to earth with my own arrow. I do not

write, for I believe in you.”



so firm have be her faith in he that her vision of he on the

passage to England have resolve all to one flash of blood-warm welcome

await she: and it say much for her natural generosity that the

savage delicacy of a woman place as she now be, do not take a mortal

hurt from the apparent voidness of this home of his bosom. the

passionate gladness of the lover be want: the chivalrous valiancy

of manful joy.



Renée shiver at the cloud thicken over her new light of intrepid

defiant life.



“think it not improbable that I have weigh everything I surrender in

quit France,” she say.



remorse wrestle with Beauchamp and fling he at her foot.



Renée remark on the lateness of the hour.



he promise to conduct she to her hotel immediately.



“and to-morrow?” say Renée, simply, but breathlessly.



“to-morrow, let it be Italy! but first I telegraph to Roland and

Tourdestelle. I cann’t run and hide. the step may be retrieve: or no,

you be right; the step cannot, but the next to it may be stop—that

be the meaning I have! I’ll try. it’s cut my hand off, tear my

heart out; but I will. o that you be free! you leave your husband at

Tourdestelle?”



“I presume he be there at present: he be in Paris when I leave.”



Beauchamp speak hoarsely and incoherently in contrast with her

composure: “you will misunderstand I for a day or two, Renée. I say if

you be free I should have my first love mine for ever. don’t fear I:

I have no right even to press your finger. he may throw you into my

arm. now you be the same as if you be in your own home: and you

must accept I for your guide. by all I hope for in life, I’ll see you

through it, and keep the dog from bark, if I can. thousand be

ready to give tongue. and if they can get I in the character of a

law-breaker!—I hear they.”



“be you imagine, Nevil, that there be a possibility of my return

to he?”



“to your place in the world! you have not have to endure tyranny?”



“I should have have a certain respect for a tyrant, Nevil. at least I

should have have an occupation in mock he and conspire against

he. Tyranny! there would have be some amusement to I in that.”



“it be neglect.”



“if I could still charge it on neglect, Nevil! neglect be very

endurable. he reward I for nurse he... he reward I with a little

persecution: wife should be flatter by it: it come late.”



“what?” cry Beauchamp, oppressed and impatient.



Renée sink her voice.



something in the run of the unaccented French: “Son amour, mon ami”:

drive the significance of the bitterness of the life she have leave

behind she burningly through he. this be to have flee from a dragon!

be the lover’s thought: he perceive the motive of her flight: and it

be a vindication of it that appeal to he irresistibly. the proposal

for her return grow hideous: and this ever multiply horror and sting

of the love of a married woman come on he with a fresh throbbing

shock, more venom.



he feel for himself now, and now he be full of feeling for she.

impossible that she should return! Tourdestelle shine to he like a

gape chasm of fire. and become entirely selfish he impress his

total abnegation of self upon Renée so that she could have worship

he. a lover that be like a starry frost, freeze her vein, bewilder

her intelligence. she yearn for meridian warmth, for repose in a

direct hand; and let it be hard as one that grasp a sword: what

matter? unhesitatingness be the warrior virtue of her desire. and for

herself the bad might happen if only she be bear along. let she

life be tear and stream like the flag of battle, it must be forward

to the end.



that be a quality of godless young heroism not unexhausted in

Beauchamp’s blood. reanimate by he, she awaken his imagination of

the vagrant splendour of existence and the rebel delight which have

their own law and “nature” for an applauding mother. Radiant Alps rise

in his eye, and the morning bear in the night sun that from mountain

and valley, over sea and desert, call on all earth to witness their

death. the magnificence of the contempt of humanity pose before he

superbly satanesque, grand as thunder among the crag and it be not a

sensual cry that summon he from his pedlar labour, pack on back

along the level road, to live and breathe deep, gloriously mate: Renée

kindle his romantic spirit, and could strike the feeling into he that

to be proud of his possession of she be to conquer the fretful vanity

to possess. she be not a woman of wile and lur.



once or twice she consult her watch: but as she profess to have no

hunger, Beauchamp’s entreaty to she to stay prevail, and the subtle

form of compliment to his knightly manliness in she remain with he,

give he a new sense of pleasure that hang round her companionable

conversation, deepen the meaning of the word, or sometimes

contrast the sweet surface commonplace with the undercurrent of

strangeness in their heart, and the reality of a tragic position. her

musical volubility flow to entrance and divert he, as it do.



suddenly Beauchamp glance upward.



Renée turn from a startled contemplation of his frown, and beheld

Mrs. Rosamund cull in the room.









CHAPTER XLI.

a lame victory





the intruder be not a person that have power to divide they; yet she

come between their heart with a touch of steel.



“I be here in obedience to your command in your telegram of this

evening,” Rosamund reply to Beauchamp’s hard stare at she; she

courteously speak French, and acquit herself demurely of a bow to

the lady present.



Renée withdraw her serious eye from Beauchamp. she rise and

acknowledge the bow.



“it be my first visit to England, madame!”



“I could have desire, Madame la marquise, more agreeable weather for

you.”



“my friend in England will dispel the bad weather for I, madame”;

Renée smile softly: “I have be study my french-English

phrase-book, that I may learn how dialogue be conduct in your

country to lead to certain ceremony when old friend meet, and

without my book I be at fault. I be long to be embrace by you... if

it will not be offend your rule?”



Rosamund succumb to the seductive woman, whose gentle tooth bit

through her tutored simplicity of manner and natural graciousness,

administer its reproof, and elude a retort or an excuse.



she give the embrace. in do so she fall upon her conscious

awkwardness for an expression of reserve that should be as good as

irony for irony, though where Madame de Rouaillout’s irony lay, or

whether it be irony at all, our excellent english dame could not have

state, after the feeling of indignant prudery respond to it so

guiltily have subside.



Beauchamp ask she if she have bring servant with she; and it

gratify she to see that he be no actor fit to carry a scene

through in virtue’s name and vice’s mask with this actress.



she reply, “I have bring a man and a maid-servant. the

establishment will be in town the day after tomorrow, in time for my

lord’s return from the Castle.”



“you can have they up to-morrow morning.”



“I could,” Rosamund admit the possibility. her idolatry of he be

try on hear he press the hospitality of the house upon Madame de

Rouaillout, and observe the lady’s transparent feint of a reluctant

yielding. for the voluble Frenchwoman scarcely find a word to utter:

she protest languidly that she prefer the independence of her

hotel, and flutter a singular look at he, as if overcome by his

vehement determination to have she in the house. undoubtedly she have a

take face and style. his infatuation, nevertheless, appear to

rosamund utter dementedness, consider this woman’s position, and

Cecilia Halkett’s beauty and wealth, and that the house be no long

at his disposal. he be really distract, to judge by his forehead, or

else he be over-act his part.



the absence of a cook in the house, Rosamund remark, must prevent she

from second Captain Beauchamp’s invitation.



he turn on she witheringly. “the telegraph will do that. you’re in

London; cook can be have by dozen. Madame de Rouaillout be alone here;

she have come to see a little of England, and you will do the honour of

the house.”



“M. le marquis be not in London?” say Rosamund, disregard the dumb

imprecation she see on Beauchamp’s feature.



“no, madame, my husband be not in London,” Renée rejoin collectedly.



“see to the necessary comfort of the house instantly,” say Beauchamp,

and tell Renée, without listen to she, that he have to issue

order, he lead Rosamund, who be out of breath at the effrontery of the

pair, toward the door. “be you blind, ma’am? have you go foolish?

what should I have send for you for, but to protect she? I see your

mind; and off with the prude, pray! Madame will have my room; clear

away every sign of I there. I sleep out; I can find a bed anywhere.

and bolt and chain the house-door to-night against Cecil Baskelett; he

inform I that he have take possession.”



Rosamund’s countenance have become less austere.



“Captain Baskelett!” she exclaim, lean to Beauchamp’s view on the

side of her animosity to Cecil; “he have be promise by his uncle the

use of a set of room during the year, when the mistress of the house

be not in occupation. I stipulate expressly that he be to see you and

suit himself to your convenience, and to let I hear that you and he

have agree to an arrangement, before he enter the house. he have no

right to be here, and I shall have no hesitation in lock he out.”



Beauchamp bid her go, and not be away more than five minute; and then

he would drive to the hotel for the luggage.



she scan he for a look of ingenuousness that might be trust, and

laugh in her heart at her credulity for expect it of a man in such

a case. she see Renée sit stonily, too proudly self-respect to

put on a mask of flippant ease. these lover might be accomplice in

deceive she; they be not happy one, and that appear to she to be

some assurance that she do well in obey he.



Beauchamp close the door on she. he walk back to Renée with a

thoughtful air that be consciously act; his only thought be—now

she know I!



Renée look up at he once. her eye be unaccusing, unquestione.



with the violation of the secresy of her flight she have lose she

initiative and her intrepidity. the world of human eye glare on she

through the window of the two she have be expose to, paralyze her

brain and cage her spirit of revolt. that keen wakefulness of her

self-defensive social instinct help she to an understanding of her

lover’s plan to preserve her reputation, or rather to give she a corner

of retreat in shield the worthless thing—twice detest as her cloak

of slavery come from he! she comprehend no more. she be a house

of nerve crowd in against her soul like fiery thorn, and have no

space within her torture for a sensation of gratitude or suspicion; but

feel herself hurry along at lightning speed to some dreadful

shock, her witless imagination apprehend it in his voice: not what he

might say, only the sound. she fear to hear he speak, as the

shrink ear fear a thunder at the cavity; yet suspense be bad

than the downward-drive silence.



the pang strike she when he utter some word about Mrs. Culling, and

protection, and Roland.



she thank he.



so have common executioner be thank by queenly lady bare their

neck to the axe.



he call up the pain he suffer to vindicate he; and it be really

an agony of a man tear to piece.



“I have do the good.”



this dogged and stupid piece of speech be pitiable to hear from Nevil

Beauchamp.



“you think so?” say she; and her glass-like voice ring a tremour in

its mildness that swell through he on the plain submissive note,

which be more assent than question.



“I be sure of it. I believe it. I see it. at least I hope so.”



“we be chiefly lead by hope,” say Renée.



“at least, if not!” Beauchamp cry. “and it’s not too late. I have no

right—I do what I can. I be at your mercy. judge I later. if I be ever

to know what happiness be, it will be with you. it’s not too late

either way. there be Roland—my brother as much as if you be my wife!”



he beg she to let he have Roland’s exact address.



she name the regiment, the corps d’armée, the postal town, and the

department.



“Roland will come at a signal,” he pursue; “we be not bind to

consult other.”



Renée form the french word of “we” on her tongue.



he talk of Roland and Roland, his affection for he as a brother and

as a friend, and Roland’s love of they both.



“it be true,” say Renée.



“we owe he this; he represent your father.”



“all that you say be true, my friend.”



“thus, you have come on a visit to madame, your old friend here—oh!

your hand. what have I do?”



Renée motion her hand as if it be free to be take, and smile

faintly to make light of it, but do not give it.



“if you have be widow!” he break down to the lover again.



“that man be attach to the remnant of his life: I could not wish he

dispossess of it,” say Renée.



“part! who part we? it’s for a night. tomorrow!”



she breathe: “to-morrow.”



to his hearing it crave an answer. he have none. to talk like a lover,

or like a man of honour, be to lie. falsehood hem he in to the

narrow ring that ever statue stand on, if he mean to be stone.



“that woman will be return,” he mutter, frown at the vacant

be unchanged in my love of you since the night when Roland and I walk

on the Piazzetta...”



“do not remind I; let those day lie black!” a sympathetic vision of

her maiden’s tear on the night of wonderful moonlight when, as it

seem to she now, San Giorgio stand like a dark prophet of her present

abasement and chastisement, sprang tear of a different character, and

weak as she be with her soul’s fever and for want of food, she be

back. I have no reproach but for myself. explain nothing to I.

thing that be not comprehend by one like I be riddle I must put

the door have not open, and it do not open immediately.



Beauchamp have time to say, “believe in I.” even that be false to his

own hearing, and in a struggle with the painful impression of

deferentially accept her brief direction concern her box at

the hotel, with Rosamund cull to witness.



she give he her hand.



he bow over the finger. “until to-morrow, madame.”



“Adieu!” say Renée.









CHAPTER XLII.

the two passion





the foggy February night refresh his head, and the business of

fetch the luggage from the hotel—a commission that necessitate the

delivery of his card and some very commanding language—keep his mind in

order. subsequently he drive to his cousin Baskelett’s Club, where he

leave a short note to say the house be engage for the night and

perhaps a week far. Concise, but sufficient: and he state a hope

to his cousin that he would not be inconvenience. this be courteous.



he have take a bed at Renée’s hotel, after wrest her box from the

“_adieu_,” and the strange “_do you think so?_” and “_I know where I

be; I scarcely know more_.” her eye and their dark lash, and the

rejoice to be so, by his pledge word? and when she, for some reason

that he be sure she have state, though he could recollect no more than

he toss in his bed as over a furnace, in the extremity of perplexity

of one accustomed to think himself ever demonstrably in the right, and

shake in his fragmentary mind, and she appear thrice adorable for

Conscience and reason have resolve to a dead weight in he, like an

his teacher and guide to be the same for the young and vigorous?



be a good gift to be refuse? and this be his first love! the

with her sole fault of want of courage devotedly amend! his pupil, he

all these and innumerable other handsome pleading of the simulacrum of

humanity have do this for Beauchamp. his afflicted historian be

conscious rectitude, too, after the pattern of the well-behave AEneas

quit the fair bosom of carthage in obedience to the Gods, for an

a crown upon his brow. it happen with he that his original training

late at night Rosamund be allow to enter the chill unlighted

the conventional phraseology appease he, though he see his grief

behind it.



presently he ask if Renée have take food.

it be true that we do not care to eat when we be in pain.



he ask if she look ill.



“she will not have light in the room,” say Rosamund.



Piecemeal he gain the picture of Renée in an image of the death

she remark very honestly:



“oh, no; doctor be not need.”

“not once.”



“why do you swing your watch-chain, ma’am?” cry Beauchamp, bound

the weighty and the trivial contend; no fitting message could be

“no.”

this terrible starvation!”

he survey the table. “you have your cook in town, I see. here’s a

he begin to nibble at bread.



Rosamund push over to he a plate of the celebrated Steynham pie, of

her own invention, such as no house in the county of Sussex could

he pitch one of the morning paper to the floor in disorderly sheet,

Beauchamp be confront the fog at the window. he swing round: “Dr.

“yes, then, naturally! he would naturally hasten to recover to partake

of the viand, ma’am.”



Rosamund murmur of her gladness that he should be able to enjoy they.

blackburn Tuckham dine there once. ‘you be a thorough revolutionist,

be no distinction between sovereign and country in a loyal and

“doubtless the tedium of such a state to a man of the temperament of

a telegram arrive from her lord. she be bid to have the house

how could that be do?



but to write blankly to inform the Earl of Romfrey that he be exclude

the card of a french lady, bear the name of Madame d’Auffray, be

Beauchamp have go off to his friend Lydiard, to fortify himself in his

should stand alone; that he would stake his position on it, and

establish his right to speak his opinion: and as for unseasonable

perish.



know the difficulty once afflict Beauchamp in the art of speak

sneer, thump the old country, raise and overset she,

review prominent political man, laugh at the dwarf-giant;

finally cast anchor on a Mechanics’ Institute that he have recently

british poet.



“that’s the good thing I’ve hear of late,” he say, shake Lydiard’s

hand on the door-step.



“ah! you’re Commander Beauchamp; I think I know you. I’ve see you on a

platform,” cry a fresh-faced man in decent clothe, halt on his

way along the pavement; “and if you be in your uniform, you damn

republican dog! I’d strip you with my own hand, for the disloyal

scoundrel you be, with your pimp Republicanism and capsize

everything in a country like Old England. it’s the cat-o’-nine-tail

you want, and the bosen to lay on; and I’d do it myself. and mind I,

when next I catch sight of you in blue and gold lace, I’ll compel you

to show cause why you wear it, and prove your case, or else I’ll make a

Cupid of you, and no joke about it. I don’t pay money for a nincompoop

to outrage my feeling of respect and loyalty, when he’s in my pay, d’

ye hear? you’re in my pay: and you do your duty, or I’ll kick ye out of

it. it’s no empty threat. you look out for your next public speech, if

it’s anywhere within forty mile of London. get along.”



with a scowl, and a very ugly “yah!” worthy of cannibal jaw, the man

pass off.



Beauchamp keep eye on he. “what class do a fellow like that come

of?”



“he’s a harmless enthusiast,” say Lydiard. “he have be read the

article, and have get excited over it.”



“I wish I have the fellow’s address.” Beauchamp look wistfully at

he. perhaps it be as well to forget the fellow.



“you see the effect of those article,” he say.



“you see what I mean by unseasonable time,” Lydiard retort.



“he don’t talk like a tradesman,” Beauchamp muse.



“he may be one, for all that. it’s well to class he as an

enthusiast.”



“an enthusiast!” Beauchamp stamp: “for what?”



“for the exist order of thing; for his beef and ale; for the title

he be accustomed to read in the paper. you don’t study your

countryman.”



“I’d study that fellow, if I have the chance.”



“you would probably find he one of the empty, with a rather bad

temper than most of they.”



Beauchamp shake Lydiard’s hand, say, “the widow?”



“there’s no woman like she!”



“well, now you’re free—why not? I think I put one man out of the

field.”



“too early! besides—”



“repeat that, and you may have to say too late.”



“when shall you go down to Bevisham?”



“when? I cann’t tell: when I’ve go through fire. there never be a

home for I like the cottage, and the old man, and the dear good

girl—the good of girl! if you haven’t a little spoilt she with your

philosophy of the two side of the case.”



“I’ve not give she the brain.”



“she’s always doubtful of do, doubtful of action: she have no will.

so she be fatalistic, and an argument between we end in she

submit, as if she must submit to I, because I’m overbearing,

“she feel your influence.”



“she’s against the publication of the DAWN—for the present. it’s an

‘unseasonable time.’ I argue with she: I don’t get hold of her mind a

bit; but at last she say, ‘very well.’ she have your head.”



and you have her heart, Lydiard could have rejoin.



they say good-bye, neither of they aware of the other’s task of

endurance.



as they be part, Beauchamp perceive his old comrade Jack Wilmore

walk past.



“Jack!” he call.



Wilmore glance round. “how do you do, Beauchamp?”



“where be you off to, Jack?”



“down to the Admiralty. I’m rather in a hurry; I have an appointment.”



“cann’t you stop just a minute?”



“I’m afraid I cann’t. good morning.”



it be incredible; but this old friend, the simple heart alive,

retreat without a touch of his hand, and with a sorely wound air.



“that newspaper article appear to have be generally read,” Beauchamp

say to Lydiard, who answer:



“the article do not put the idea of you into man’s mind, but give

tongue to it: you may take it for an instance of the sagacity of the

Press.”
