Old Jack Wilmore! don’t go, Lydiard.”



Lydiard declare that he be bind to go: he be engage to read

Italian for an hour with Mrs. Wardour-Devereux.



“then go, by all mean,” Beauchamp dismiss he.



he feel as if he have hold a review of his friend and enemy on the

door-step, and find they of one colour. if it be an accident

befall he in a London square during a space of a quarter of an

hour, what of the sentiment of universal England? Lady Barbara’s

elopement with Lord Alfred last year do not rouse much execration;

hardly bad than gossip and compassion. Beauchamp drink a great deal

of bitterness from his reflection.



they who provoke huge battle, and gain but lame victory over

themselves, insensibly harden to the habit of distil sour thought

from their mischance and from most occurrence. so do the world they

combat win on they.



“for,” say Dr. Shrapnel, “the world and nature, which be oppose in

relation to our vital interest, each agree to demand of we a perfect

victory, on pain otherwise of prove it a stage performance; and the

victory over the world, as over nature, be over self: and this victory

lie in yield perpetual service to the world, and none to nature:

for the world have to be work out, nature to be subdue.”



the interior of the house be like a change of element to Beauchamp.

he have never before say to himself, “I have do my good, and I be

beat!” outside of it, his native pugnacity have be stimulate; but

here, within the wall where Renée lie silently breathing, barely

breathing, it might be die, he be overcome, and leave it to

circumstance to carry he to a conclusion. he go up-stair to the

drawing-room, where he behold Madame d’Auffray in conversation with

Rosamund.



“I be assure by Madame la Comtesse that I should see you to-day,” the

french lady say as she swam to meet he; “it be a real pleasure”: and

press his hand she continue, “but I fear you will be disappoint

of see my sister. she would rashly try your climate at its bad

period. believe I, I do not join in decry it, except on her

account: I could have forewarn she of an English Winter and early

Spring. you know her impetuosity; suddenly she decide on accept the

invitation of Madame la Comtesse; and though I have no fear of her

health, she be at present a victim of the inclement weather.”



“you have see she, madame?” say Beauchamp. so well have the clever

lady play the dupe that he forget there be a part for he to play.

even the acquiescence of Rosamund in the title of countess bewilder

he.



“Madame d’Auffray have be sit for an hour with Madame de

Rouaillout,” say Rosamund.



he speak of Roland’s come.



“ah?” say Madame d’Auffray, and turn to Rosamund: “you have

determine to surprise we: then you will have a gathering of the whole

family in your hospitable house, Madame la Comtesse.”



“if M. la Marquis will do it that honour, madame.”



“my brother be in London,” Madame d’Auffray say to Beauchamp.



the shattering blow be merit by one who could not rejoice that he

have act rightly.









CHAPTER XLIII.

the EARL of ROMFREY and the COUNTESS





an extraordinary telegraphic message, follow by a still more

extraordinary letter the next morning, from Rosamund Culling, all but

interdict the immediate occupation of his house in town to Everard,

now Earl of Romfrey. she beg he briefly not to come until after the

funeral, and propose to give he good reason for her request at their

meeting. “I repeat, I pledge myself to satisfy you on this point,” she

write. her tone be that of one of your heroic woman of history

refuse to surrender a fortress.



Everard’s wrath be ever of a complexion that could suffer

postponement without his have to fear an abatement of it. he have no

business to transact in London, and he have much at the Castle, so he

yield himself up to his new sensation, which be not commonly the

portion of gentleman of his year. he anticipate that Nevil would at

least come down to the funeral, but there be no appearance of he, nor

a word to excuse his absence. cecil be his only supporter. they walk

together between the double rank of bare poll of the tenantry and

peasantry, resemble in a fashion old Froissart engraving the earl

use to dote on in his boyhood, represent body of manacle

citizen, whose humble head look like nut to be crack, outside

the gate of capture french town, await the disposition of their

conqueror, with his banner above he and prance knight around. that

be a glory of the past. he have no successor. the thought be chill;

the solitariness of childlessness to an aged man, chief of a most

ancient and martial House, and proud of his blood, give he the

statue’s outlook on a desert, and make he feel that he be no more

than a whirl of the dust, settle to the dust.



he listen to the parson curiously and consentingly. we be ashe. ten

century have come to an end in he to prove the formula correct. the

chronicle of the House would state that the last Earl of Romfrey leave

no heir.



Cecil be a fine figure walk beside he. measure by foot, he might

be a worthy holder of great land. but so heartily do the earl despise

this nephew that he never think of try strength with the fellow,

and hardly care to know what his value be, beyond his immediate use

as an instrument to strike with. Beauchamp of Romfrey have be his

dream, not Baskelett: and it increase his disgust of Beauchamp that

Baskelett should step forward as the man. no doubt Cecil would hunt the

county famously: he would preserve game with the sleepless eye of a

General of the Jesuits. these thing be to be consider.



two day after the funeral Lord Romfrey proceed to London. he be meet

at the station by Rosamund, and inform that his house be not yet

vacate by the french family.



“and where have you arrange for I to go, ma’am?” he ask she

complacently.



she name an hotel where she have take room for he.



he nod, and be drive to the hotel, say little on the road.



as she expect, he be heavily armed against she and Nevil.



“you’re the slave of the fellow, ma’am. you be so infatuated that you

second his amour, in my house. I must wait for a clearance, it seem.”



he cast a comical glance of disapprobation on the fitting of the hotel

apartment, abhor gilt.



“they leave we the day after to-morrow,” say Rosamund, out of breath

with nervousness at the commencement of the fray, and skip over the

opening ground of a bold statement of fact. “Madame de Rouaillout have

be unwell. she be not yet recover; she have just rise. her

sister-in-law have nurse she. her husband seem much break in health;

he be perfect on the point of courtesy.”



“that be lucky, ma’am.”



“her brother, Nevil’s comrade in the war, be there also.”



“who come first?”



“my lord, you have only hear Captain Baskelett’s version of the story.

she have be my guest since the first day of her landing in England.

there cannot possibly be an imputation on she.”



“Ma’am, if her husband manage to be satisfied, what on earth have I to

do with it?”



“I be think of Nevil, my lord.”



“you’re never think of any one else, ma’am.”



“he sleep here, at this hotel. he leave the house to Madame de

Rouaillout. I bear witness to that.”



“you two seem to have make your preparation to stand a criminal

trial.”



“it be pure truth, my lord.”



“do you take I to be anxious about the fellow’s virtue?”



“she be a lady who would please you.”



“a scandal in my house do not please I.”



“the only approach to a scandal be make by Captain Baskelett.”



“a poor devil lock out of his bed on a Winter’s night hullabaloos

with pretty good reason. I suppose he feel the contrast.”



“my lord, this lady do I the honour to come to I on a visit. I have

not previously presume to entertain a friend. she probably form no

estimate of my exact position.”



the earl with a gesture imply Rosamund’s privilege to act the hostess

to friend.



“you invite she?” he say.



“that be, I have tell she I hope she would come to England.”



“she expect you to be at the house in town on her arrival?”



“it be her impulse to come.”



“she come alone?”



“she may have desire to be away from her own people for a time: there

may have be domestic difference. these case be delicate.”



“this case appear to have be so delicate that you have to lock out a

fourth party.”



“it be indelicate and base of Captain Baskelett to complain and to

hint. Nevil have to submit to the same; and Captain Baskelett take his

revenge on the housedoor and the bell. the house be visit by the

police next morning.”



“do you suspect he to have know you be inside the house that

night?”



she could not say so: but hatred of Cecil urge she past the bound of

habitual reticence to put it to her lord whether he, imagine the

bad, would have behave like Cecil.



to this he do not reply, but remark, “I be sorry he annoy you,

ma’am.”



“it be not the annoyance to I; it be the shocking, the unmanly

insolence to a lady, and a foreign lady.”



“that’s a matter between he and Nevil. I uphold he.”



“then, my lord, I be silent.”



Silent she remain; but Lord Romfrey be also silent: and silence

be a weapon of offence only when it be practise by one out of two,

she have to reflect whether in speak no far she have finish she

business.



“Captain Baskelett stay at the Castle?” she ask.



“he like his quarter there.”



“Nevil could not go down to Romfrey, my lord. he be oblige to wait,

and see, and help I to entertain, her brother and her husband.”



“why, ma’am? but I have no objection to his make the marquis a happy

husband.”



“he have do what few man would have do, that she may be a

self-respect wife.”



“the parson’s in that fellow!” Lord Romfrey exclaim. “now I have the

story. she come to he, he decline the gift, and you be turn into

the curtain for they. if he have only be off with she, he would have

do the country good service. here he’s a failure and a nuisance; he’s

a common cock-shy for the journal. I’m tired of hearing of he; he’s a

stench in our nostril. he’s tired of the woman.”



“he love she.”



“Ma’am, you’re hoodwink. if he refuse to have she, there’s a

something he love well. I don’t believe we’ve breed a downright

lackadaisical donkey in our family: I know he. he’s not a fellow for

abstract morality: I know he. it’s bargain against bargain with he;

I’ll do he that justice. I hear he have order the removal of the

Jersey bull from Holdesbury, and the beast be mine,” Lord Romfrey

conclude in a low key.



“Nevil have take he.”



“ha! pull and pull, then!”



“he contend that he be bind by a promise to give an american

gentleman the refusal of the bull, and you must sign an engagement to

keep the animal no long than two year.”



“I sign no engagement. I stick to the bull.”



“consent to see Nevil to-night, my lord.”



“when he have apologize to you, I may, ma’am.”



“surely he do more, in request I to render he a service.”



“there’s not a creature live that fellow wouldn’t get to serve he,

if he know the trick. we should all of we be march on London at

Shrapnel’s heel. the political mania be just as incurable as

hydrophobia, and he’s bite. that’s clear.”



“bite perhaps: but not mad. as you have always contend, the true

case be incurable, but it be very rare: and be this one?”



“it’s uncommonly like a true case, though I haven’t see he foam at

the mouth, and shun water—as his mob do.”



Rosamund restrain some tear, betray the effort to hide the

moisture. “I be no match for you, my lord. I try to plead on his

behalf;—I do bad than if I be dumb. this I most earnestly say: he

be the Nevil Beauchamp who fight for his country, and do not abandon

her cause, though he stand there—we have it from Colonel Halkett—a

skeleton: and he be the Nevil who—I be poorly pay my debt to

him!—defended I from the aspersion of his cousin.”



“boy!” Lord Romfrey ejaculate.



“it be the same dispute between they as man.”



“have you forget my proposal to shield you from liar and

scandalmonger?”



“could I ever forget it?” Rosamund appear to come shine out of a

cloud. “princeliest and true gentleman, I think you then, and I

know you to be, my dear lord. I fancy I have live the scandal down. I

be under the delusion that I have grow to be past backbiting: and that

no man could stand before I to insult and vilify I. but, for a woman

in any so-call doubtful position, it seem that the coward will not

be want to strike she. in quit your service, I be able to affirm

that only once during the whole term of it have I consciously

overstep the line of my duty: it be for Nevil: and captain

Baskelett undertake to defend your reputation, in consequence.”



“have the rascal be question your conduct?” the earl frown.



“oh, no! not question: he do not question, he accuse: he never

doubt: and what he went shout as a boy, be plain matter of fact to

he now. he be devoted to you. it be for your sake that he desire I

to keep my name from be mix up in a scandal he foresee the

occurrence of in your house.”



“he permit himself to sneer at you?”



“he have the art of sneer. on this occasion he wish to be direct

and personal.”



“what sort of hint be they?”



Lord Romfrey stride away from her chair that the answer might be easy

to she, for she be red, and evidently suffer from shame as well as

indignation.



“the hint we call distinct.” say Rosamund.



“in word?”



“in hard word.”



“then you willn’t meet Cecil?”



such a question, and the tone of indifference in which it come,

surprised and revolt she so that the unreflecte reply leapt out:



“I would rather meet a devil.”



of how tremblingly, vehemently, and hastily she have say it, she be

unaware. to her lord it be an outcry of nature, astutely touch by

he to put she to proof.



he continue his long leisurely stride, nod over his foot.



Rosamund stand up. she look a very noble figure in her broad

black-furred robe. “I have one serious confession to make, sir.”



“what’s that?” say he.



“I would avoid it, for it cannot lead to particular harm; but I have an

enemy who may poison your ear in my absence. and first I resign my

position. I have forfeit it.”



“time go forward, ma’am, and you go round. speak to the point. do you

mean that you toss up the rein of my household?”



“I do. you trace it to Nevil immediately?”



“I do. the fellow want to upset the country, and he begin with I.”



“you be wrong, my lord. what I have do place I at Captain

Baskelett’s mercy. it be too loathsome to think of: bad than the

whip; bad than your displeasure. it might never be know; but the

think that it might give I courage. you have say that to protect a

woman everything be permissible. it be your creed, my lord, and because

the world, I have hear you say, be unjust and implacable to woman. in

some case, I think so too. in reality I follow your instruction; I

mean, your example. cheap chivalry on my part! but it pain I not a

little. I beg to urge that in my defence.”



“well, ma’am, you have tie the knot tight enough; perhaps now you’ll

cut it,” say the earl.



Rosamund gasp softly. “M. le Marquis be a gentleman who, after a life

of dissipation, have be remind by bad health that he have a young and

beautiful wife.”



“he dig his pit to fall into it:—he’s jealous?”



she shake her head to indicate the immeasurable.



“senile jealousy be anxious to be deceive. he could hardly be deceive

so far as to imagine that Madame la Marquise would visit I, such as I

be, as my guest. knowingly or not, his very clever sister, a good

woman, and a friend to husband and wife—a Frenchwoman of the purest

type—give I the title. she insist on it, and I presume to guess

that she deem it necessary for the sake of peace in that home.”



Lord Romfrey appear merely inquisitive; his eyebrow be lift in

permanence; his eye be mild.



she continue: “they leave England in a few hour. they be not likely

to return. I permit he to address I with the title of countess.”



“of Romfrey?” say the earl.



Rosamund bow.



his mouth contract. she do not expect thunder to issue from it, but

she do fear to hear a sarcasm, or that she would have to endure a

deadly silence: and she be gather her own lip in imitation of his,

to nerve herself for some stroke to come, when he laugh in his

peculiar close-mouthed manner.



“I’m afraid you’ve dish yourself.”



“you cannot forgive I, my lord?”



he indulge in more of his laughter, and abruptly summon gravity,

bid her talk to he of affair. he himself talk of the condition of

the Castle, and with a certain off-hand contempt of the lady of the

family, and Cecil’s father, Sir John. “what be they to I?” say he,

and he complain of having be call Last Earl of Romfrey.



“the line end undegenerate,” say Rosamund fervidly, though she know

not where she stand.



“end!” quoth the earl.



“I must see stukely,” he add briskly, and stoop to she: “I beg you

to drive I to my Club, countess.”



“oh! sir.”



“once a countess, always a countess!”



“but once an impostor, my lord?”



“not always, we’ll hope.”



he enjoy this little variation in the language of comedy; let it

drop, to say: “be here to-morrow early. don’t chase that family away

from the house. do as you will, but not a word of Nevil to I: he’s a

bad mess in any man’s porringer; it’s time for I to claim exemption of

he from mine.”



she dared not let her thought flow, for to think be to triumph, and

possibly to be delude. they come in copious volume when Lord Romfrey,

alight at his Club, call to the coachman: “drive the countess

home.”



they be not thought of triumph absolutely. in her cool mind she

feel that it be a bad finish of a gallant battle. few woman have rise

against a tattling and pelt world so stedfastly; and would it not

have be well to keep her own ground, which she have win with tear

and some natural strength, and therewith her liberty, which she prize?

the hateful Cecil, a reminder of whom set her cheek burn and turn

her heart to serpent, have force she to it. so she honestly conceive,

owe to the circumstance of she honestly dislike the pomp of life

and not desire to occupy any position of brilliancy. she think

assuredly of her hoard of animosity toward the scandalmonger, and of

the quiet glance she would cast behind on they, and below. that thought

come as a fruit, not as a reflection.



but if ever two offend young gentleman, nephew of a long-suffer

uncle, be circumvent, undermine, and strike to earth, with one

blow, here be the instance. this be accomplish by Lord Romfrey’s

resolution to make the lady he have learn to esteem his countess: and

more, it fix to he for life one whom he could not bear to think of

lose: and still more, it might be; but what more be unwritten on his

tablet.



Rosamund fail to recollect that Everard Romfrey never take a step

without see a combination of object to be gain by it.









CHAPTER XLIV.

the nephew of the earl, and another exhibition of the two passion in

BEAUCHAMP





it be now the season when London be as a lighted tower to her

province, and, among other gentleman hurry thither by attraction,

Captain Baskelett arrive. although not a personage in the House of

Commons, he be a vote; and if he never commit himself to the peril

of a speech, he make himself hear. his be the part of chorus, which

he perform with a fairly close imitation of the original cry of

period before parliament be institute, thus represent a stage

in the human development besides the borough of Bevisham. he arrive in

the good of mood for the emission of high-pitch vowel-sound;

otherwise in the bad of temper. his uncle have notify an addition

of his income to he at Romfrey, together with command that he should

quit the castle instantly: and there do that woman, Mistress Culling,

do the honour to Nevil Beauchamp’s french party. he assure Lord

Palmet of his positive knowledge of the fact, incredible as the

sanction of such immoral proceeding by the Earl of Romfrey must appear

to that young nobleman. addition to income be of course acceptable,

but in the form of a palpable stipulation for silence, they neither

awaken gratitude nor effect their purpose. quite the contrary; they

prick the moral mind to sit in judgement on the donor. it mean, she

fear I! Cecil confidently think and say of the intriguing woman

who manage his patron.



the town-house be open to he. Lord Romfrey be at Steynham. cecil

could not suppose that he be fall into a pit in enter it. he

happen to be the favourite of the old housekeeper, who like he for

his haughtiness, which be to she think the sign of real english

nobility, and perhaps it be the popular sign, and a tonic to the

people. she raise lamentation over the shame of the locking of the

door against he that awful night, declare she have almost muster

courage to go down to he herself, in spite of Mrs. Culling’s order.

the old woman lower her voice to tell he that her official superior

have permit the french gentleman and lady to call her countess.

this she know for a certainty, though she know nothing of French; but

the french lady who come second bring a maid who know English a

little, and she say the very word—the countess, and say also that

her party take Mrs. Culling for the Countess of Romfrey. what be more,

my lord’s coachman catch it up, and he call her countess, and he have

a quarrel about it with the footman Kendall; and the day after a

dreadful affair between they in the mew, home drive madam, and

Kendall be to go up to she, and down the poor man come, and not a word

to be get out of he, but as if he have see a ghost. “she have such

power,” Cecil’s admirer conclude.



“I wager I match she,” Cecil say to himself, pull at his wristband

and let his low tooth shine out. the mean of match she be

not so palpable as the resolution. first he take man into his

confidence. then he touch lightly on the story to lady, with the

question, “what ought I to do?” in consideration for the Earl of

Romfrey he ought not to pass it over, he suggest. the lady of the

family urge he to go to Steynham and boldly confront the woman. he

be not prepare for that. well, it seem to he, to blow the

rumour, and make it the topic of the season, until Lord Romfrey should

hear of it. Cecil have the ear of the town for a month. he be in the

act of slice the air with his right hand in his accustomed style, one

evening at Lady Elsea’s, to protest how vast be the dishonour do to

the family by Mistress Culling, when Stukely Culbrett stop he,

say, “the lady you speak of be the Countess of Romfrey. I be

present at the marriage.”



Cecil receive the shock in the attitude of those martial figure we

see wield two wooden sword in provincial garden to tell the

disposition of the wind: abruptly abandon by it, they stand

transfix, one sword aloft, the other at their heel. the resemblance

extend to his astonished countenance. his big chest heave. like many

another wound giant before he, he experience the insufficiency of

interjection to solace pain. for they, however, the rock be handy

to fle, the tree to uproot; heaven’s concave resound companionably

to their bellowing. relief of so concrete a kind be not to be obtain

in crowded London assembly.



“you be jesting?—you be a jester,” he contrive to say.



“it be a private marriage, and I be a witness,” reply stukely.



“Lord Romfrey have make an honest woman of she, have he?”



“a peeress, you mean.”



Cecil bow. “exactly. I be correct. I mean a peeress.”



he get out of the room with as high an air as he could command, feel

as if a bar of iron have flatten his head.



next day it be intimate to he by one of the Steynham servant that

apartment be ready for he at the residence of the late earl: Lord

Romfrey’s house be about to be occupy by the Countess of Romfrey.

Cecil have to quit, and he choose to be enamour of that dignity of

sulk so seductive to the wound spirit of man.



Rosamund, Countess of Romfrey, have bad to endure from Beauchamp. he

indeed come to the house, and he go through the formality of

congratulation, but his opinion of her step be unconceale, that she

have take it for the title. he distress she by revive the case of

Dr. Shrapnel, as though it be a matter of yesterday, tell she she

have marry a man with a stain on he; she should have exact the

Apology as a nuptial present; ay, and she would have do it if she have

care for the earl’s honour or her own. so little do he understand

man! so tenacious be he of his idea! she have almost forget the

case of Dr. Shrapnel, and to see it shoot up again in the new path

of her life be really irritate.



Rosamund do not defend herself.



“I be very glad you have come, Nevil,” she say; “your uncle hold to

the ceremony. I may be of real use to you now; I wish to be.”



“you have only to prove it,” say he. “if you can turn his mind to

marriage, you can send he to Bevisham.”



“my chief thought be to serve you.”



“I know it be, I know it be,” he rejoin with some fervour. “you have

serve I, and make I miserable for life, and rightly. never mind,

all’s well while the hand’s to the axe.” Beauchamp smooth his

forehead roughly, try hard to inspire himself with the tonic

draught of sentiment cast in the form of proverb. “Lord Romfrey see

she, you say?”



“he do, Nevil, and admire she.”



“well, if I suffer, let I think of _she!_ for courage and nobleness I

shall never find she equal. have you change your idea of Frenchwomen

now? not a word, you say, not a look, to show her disdain of I

whenever my name be mention!”



“she could scarcely feel disdain. she be guilty of a sad error.”



“through trusting in I. will nothing teach you where the fault lie?

you woman have no mercy for woman. she go through the parade to

Romfrey Castle and back, and she must have be perish at heart.

that, you english call act. in history you have a respect for such

act up to the scaffold. good-bye to she! there’s a story end. one

thing you must promise: you’re a peeress, ma’am: the story’s out,

everybody have hear of it; that babbler have do his bad: if you have

a become appreciation of your title, you will promise I honestly—no,

give I your word as a woman I can esteem—that you will not run about

excuse I. whatever you hear say or suggest, say nothing yourself.

I insist on your keeping silence. press my hand.”



“Nevil, how foolish!”



“it’s my will.”



“it be unreasonable. you give your enemy licence.”



“I know what’s in your head. take my hand, and let I have your word

for it.”



“but if person you like very much, Nevil, should hear?”



“promise. you be a woman not to break your word.”



“if I decline?”



“your hand! I’ll kiss it.”



“oh! my darling.” Rosamund fling her arm round he and strain he an

instant to her bosom. “what have I but you in the world? my comfort be

the hope that I might serve you.”



“yes! by slay one woman as an offering to another. it would be

impossible for you to speak the truth. don’t you see, it would be a lie

against she, and make a figure of I that a man would rather drop to

the ground than have show of he? I be to blame, and only I. Madame

de Rouaillout be as utterly deceive by I as ever a trust woman by

a brute. I look at myself and hardly believe it’s the same man. I write

to she that I be unchanged—and I be entirely change, another

creature, anything Lord Romfrey may please to call I.”



“but, Nevil, I repeat, if Miss Halkett should hear...?”



“she know by this time.”



“at present she be ignorant of it.”



“and what be Miss Halkett to I?”



“More than you imagine in that struggle you undergo, I think, Nevil.

oh! if only to save she from Captain Baskelett! he gain your uncle’s

consent when they be at the Castle, to support he in propose for

she. he be persistent. woman have be snare without love. she be a

great heiress. reflect on his use of her wealth. you respect she, if

you have no warm feeling. let I assure you that the husband of

Cecilia, if he be of Romfrey blood, have the fair chance of the

estate. that man will employ every weapon. he will soon be here bow

to I to turn I to his purpose.”



“Cecilia can see through Baskelett,” say Beauchamp.



“single-mindedly selfish man may be see through and through, and still

be dangerous, nevil. the supposition be, that we know the bad of

they. he carry a story to poison her mind. she could resist it, if

you and she be in full confidence together. if she do not love you,

she could resist it. she do, and for some strange reason beyond my

capacity to fathom, you have not come to an understanding. sanction my

speak to she, just to put she on her guard, privately: not to injure

that poor lady, but to explain. shall she not know the truth? I need

say but very little. indeed, all I can say be, that find the

marquise in London one evening, you telegraph for I to attend on

she, and I join you. you shake your head. but surely it be due to

Miss Halkett. she should be protect from what will certainly wound

she deeply. her father be afraid of you, on the score of your theory.

I foresee it: he will hear the scandal: he will imagine you as bad in

moral as in politic. and you have lose your friend in Lord

Romfrey—though he shall not be your enemy. Colonel Halkett and Cecilia

call on we at Steynham. she be look beautiful; a trifle

melancholy. the talk be of your—that—I do not like it, but you hold

those opinion—the Republicanism. she have read your publish letter.

she speak to I of your sincerity. Colonel Halkett of course be vex.



it be the same with all your friend. she, however, by her tone, lead I

to think that she see you as you be, more than in what you do. they

be now in Wales. they will be in town after Easter. then you must

expect that her feel for you will be try, unless but you will! you

will let I speak to she, Nevil. my position allow I certain

liberty I be previously debar from. you have not be so very

tender to your Cecilia that you can afford to give her fresh reason

for sorrowful perplexity. and why should you stand to be blacken by

scandalmonger when a few word of mine will prove that instead of weak

you have be strong, instead of libertine blameless? I be not use

fine phrase: I would not. I would be as thoughtful of you as if you

be present. and for her sake, I repeat, the truth should be tell to

she. I have a lock of her hair.”



“Cecilia’s? where?” say Beauchamp.



“it be at Steynham.” Rosamund prim her lip at the success of she

probe touch; but she be unaware of the chief reason for his doting

on those fair lock, and how they colour his imagination since the

day of the drive into Bevisham.



“now leave I, my dear Nevil,” she say. “Lord Romfrey will soon be

here, and it be as well for the moment that you should not meet he, if

it can be avoid.”



Beauchamp leave she, like a man out-argue and overcome. he have no wish

to meet his uncle, whose behaviour in contract a misalliance and

cast a shadow on the family, in a manner so perfectly objectless and

senseless, appear to he to call for the reverse of compliment.

Cecilia’s lock of hair lie at Steynham hang in his mind. he see the

smooth flat curl lie secret like a smile.



the graceful head it have fall from be dimmer in his mental eye. he

go so far in this charmed meditation as to feel envy of the possessor

of the sever lock: passingly he wonder, with the wonder of

reproach, that the possessor should deem it enough to possess the lock,

and resign it to a drawer or a desk. and as when life roll back on we

after the long ebb of illness, little whisper and diminutive image of

the old joy and prize of life arrest and fill our heart; or as, to

man who have be beat down by storm, the opening of a daisy be

dear than the blaze orient which bid it open; so the visionary

lock of Cecilia’s hair become Cecilia’s self to Beauchamp, yield he

as much of she as he could bear to think of, for his heart be

shatter.



why have she give it to his warm friend? for the asking, probably.



this question be the first ripple of the breeze from other emotion

begin to flow fast.



he walk out of London, to be alone, and to think and from the paling

of a road on a south-western run of high land, he gaze, at the great

city—a place conquerable yet, with the proper appliance for

subjugate it: the starting of his daily newspaper, the DAWN, say, as

a commencement. it begin to seem a possible enterprise. it soon seem

a proximate one. if Cecilia! he leave the exclamation a blank, but not

an empty dash in the brain; rather like the shroud of night on a vast

and gloriously imagine land.



Nay, the prospect be partly visible, as the unknown country become by

degree to the traveller’s optic on the dark hill-top. it be much, of

course, to be domestically well-mate: but to be fortify and arm by

one’s wife with a weapon to fight the world, be rare good fortune; a

rapturous and an infinite satisfaction. he could now support of his own

resource a weekly paper. a paper publish weekly, however, be a poor

thing, out of the tide, behind the date, mainly a literary periodical,

no foremost combatant in politic, no champion in the arena; hardly

well than a commentator on the event of the six past day; an echo,

not a voice. it sit on a Saturday bench and pretend to sum up. who

listen? the verdict knock dust out of a cushion. it have no steady

continuous pressure of influence. it be the organ of sleeper. of all

the big instrument of money, it be the feeble, Beauchamp think.

his constant faith in the good effect of utterance naturally incline

he to value six occasion per week above one; and in the fight he be

for waging, it be necessary that he should enter the ring and hit blow

for blow san intermission. a statement that he could call false must

be challenge hot the next morning. the covert Toryism, the fit of

flunkeyism, the cowardice, of the relapse middle-class, which be now

England before mankind, because it fill the sail of the Press, must

be expose. it support the Press in its own interest, affect to

speak for the people. it belie the people. and this Press, declare

itself independent, can hardly walk for fear of treading on an interest

here, an interest there. it cannot have a conscience. it be a bad

guide, a false guardian; its abject claim to be our national and

popular interpreter—even that be hollow and a mockery! it be powerful

only while subservient. an engine of money, appeal to the

sensitiveness of money, it have no connection with the mind of the

nation. and that it be not of, but apart from, the people, may be see

when great crisis come. can it stop a war? the people would, and with

thunder, have they the medium. but in strong gale the power of the

Press collapse; it wheeze like a prick pigskin of a piper. at its

good Beauchamp regard our lordly press as a curiously diapere

curtain and delusive mask, behind which the country struggle vainly to

show an honest feature; and as a trumpet that deafen and terrorize

the people; a mere engine of leaguer band to keep a smooth face upon

affair, quite soullessly: he meanwhile have to be dumb.



but a Journal that should be actually independent of circulation and

advertisement: a popular journal in the true sense, very lung to the

people, for they to breathe freely through at last, and be hear out of

it, with well-pay man of mark to head and aid them;—the establishment

of such a Journal seem to he brave work of a life, though one should

die early. the money launch it would be coin wash pure of its

iniquity of selfish reproduction, by service to mankind. this dawn of

his conception stand over he like a rosy Aurora for the country. he

behold it in imagination as a new light rise above hugeous London.

you turn the sheet of the DAWN, and it be the manhood of the land

address you, no long that alternately pule and insolent cry of

the coffer. the health, wealth, comfort, contentment of the great

number be there to be strive for, in contempt of compromise and

“unseasonable time.”



Beauchamp’s illuminated dream of the power of his DAWN to vitalize old

England, liberate he singularly from his wear regret and

heart-sickness.



Surely Cecilia, who judge he sincere, might be bent to join hand

with he for so good a work! she would bring rich to her husband:

sufficient. he require the able man of the country to write for he,

and it be just that they should be largely pay. they at least in

their present public apathy would demand it. to fight the brewer,

distiller, publican, the shopkeeper, the parson, the landlord, the

law limpet, and also the indifferent, the log, the craven and the

fool, high talent be need, and an ardour stimulate by rate of pay

outdo the offer of the lucre-journal. a large annual outlay would

therefore be need; possibly for as long as a quarter of a century.

Cecilia and her husband would have to live modestly. but her

inheritance would be immense. Colonel Halkett have never spend a tenth

of his income. in time he might be teach to perceive in the DAWN the

one greatly beneficent enterprise of his day. he might through his

daughter’s eye, and the grow success of the Journal. benevolent and

gallant old man, patriotic as he be, and kind at heart, he might learn

to see in the DAWN a broad channel of philanthropy and chivalry than

any we have yet have a notion of in England!—a school of popular

education into the bargain.



Beauchamp revert to the shine curl. it could not have be clear

to vision if it have lain under his eye.



ay, that first wild life of his be dead. he have slay it. now for the

second and sober life! who can say? the Countess of Romfrey suggest

it:—Cecilia may have prompt he in his unknown heart to the sacrifice

of a lawless love, though he take it for simply barren iron duty.

brood on she, he begin to fancy the victory over himself less and

less a lame one: for it wax less and less difficult in his

contemplation of it. he be look forward instead of back.



who cut off the lock? probably Cecilia herself; and think at the

moment that he would see it, perhaps beg for it. the lustrous little

ring of hair wound round his heart; smile both on its emotion and its

aim; bind they in one.



but proportionately as he grow tender to Cecilia, his consideration for

Renée increase; that become a law to he: pity nourish it, and

glimpse of self-contempt, and something like worship of her

high-heartedness.



he write to the countess, forbid she sharply and absolutely to

attempt a vindication of he by explanation to any person whomsoever;

and state that he would have no falsehood tell, he desire she to

keep to the original tale of the visit of the french family to she as

guest of the Countess of Romfrey. Contradictory indeed. Rosamund shake

her head over he. for a wilful character that be guilty of issue

contradictory command to friend who would be friend in spite of he,

appear to be expressly angle for the cynical spirit, so surely do

it rise and snap at such provocation. he be even more emphatic when

they next meet. he would not listen to a remonstrance; and though, of

course, her love of he grant he the liberty to speak to she in what

tone he please, there be sensation proper to her new rank which his

intemperateness wound and tempt to revolt when he vex she with

unreason. she have a glimpse of the face he might wear to his enemy.



he be quite as resolute, too, about that slight matter of the Jersey

bull. he have the bull in Bevisham, and would not give he up without

the sign manual of Lord Romfrey to an agreement to resign he over to

the American Quaker gentleman, after a certain term. moreover, not once

have he, by exclamation or innuendo, during the period of his recent

grief for the loss of his first love, complain of his uncle Everard’s

refusal in the old day to aid he in sue for Renée. Rosamund have

expect that he would. she think it unloverlike in he not to stir

the past, and to bow to intolerable fact. this idea of he, come in

conjunction with his present behaviour, convince she that there

exist a contradiction in his nature: whence it ensue that she lose

her warmth as an advocate design to intercede for he with Cecilia;

and warmth be go, the power of the scandal seem to she

unassailable. how she could ever have presume to combat it, be an

astonishment to she. Cecilia might be indulgent, she might have faith

in Nevil. little else could be hope for.



the occupation, duty, and ceremony of her new position contribute

to the lassitude into which Rosamund sink. and she soon have a

communication to make to her lord, the nature of which be more

startling to herself, even tragic. the bondwoman be a free woman

compare with the wife.



Lord Romfrey’s friend notice a glow of hearty health in the splendid

old man, and a prouder animation of eye and stature; and it be agree

that matrimony suit he well. luckily for Cecil he do not sulk very

long. a spectator of the earl’s first introduction to the House of

Peers, he call on his uncle the follow day, and Rosamund accept

his homage in her husband’s presence. he vow that my lord be the

noble figure in the whole assembly; that it have be to he the most

move sight he have ever witness; that Nevil should have be there
