Cecilia herself break down, and give way to sob in her father’s arm.









CHAPTER XLIX.

a fabric of BARONIAL despotism crumbles





the earl’s precaution do duty night and day in all the avenue

lead to the castle and his wife’s apartment; and he could believe

that he have undertake as good a defence as the mountain guard the

fertile vale from storm: but he the element pelt heavily. letter

from acquaintance of Nevil, from old shipmate and from queer

political admirer and opponent, hail on he; thing not to be

frigidly read be relate of the fellow.



Lord Romfrey’s faith in the power of constitution to beat disease

battle sturdily with the daily report of his physician and friend,

whom he have direct to visit the cottage on the common outside

Bevisham, and with Miss Denham’s intercept letter to the countess.

still he have to calculate on the various injury Nevil have do to his

constitution, which have make of he another sort of man for a struggle

of life and death than when he stand like a riddled flag through the

war. that late freak of the fellow’s, the abandonment of our natural

and wholesome sustenance in animal food, be to be take in the

reckoning. Dr. Gannet do not allude to it; the Bevisham doctor do;

and the earl meditate with a fury of wrath on the dismal chance that

such a folly as this of one old vegetable idiot influence a young

noodle, might strike his House to the dust.



his watch over his wife have grow mechanical: he fail to observe that

her voice be miss. she rarely speak. he lose the art of observe

himself: the wrinkling up and drop of his brow become his habitual

language. so long as he have not to meet inquiry or face tear, he

enjoy the sense of security. he never quit his wife save to walk

to the southern park lodge, where letter and telegram be pile

await he; and she be forbid to take the air on the castle

terrace without his be beside she, lest a whisper, some accident of

the kind that donkey who nod over their drowsy nose-length-ahead

precaution call fatality, should rouse she to suspect, and in a turn

of the hand undo his labour: for the race be get terrible: death

have not yet step out of that evil chamber in Dr. Shrapnel’s cottage

to aim his javelin at the bosom contain the prized young life to

come, but, like the smoke of wax fire, he shadow forth his

presence in wreath blacker and thick day by day: and Everard Romfrey

know that the hideous beast of darkness have only to spring up and pass

his guard to deal a blow to his House the direr from all he suppose

himself to have gain by mask it hitherto. the young life he look

to for renewal swallow he: he partly lose human feeling for his wife

in the tremendous watch and strain to hurry she as a vessel round the

dangerous headland. he be oblivious that his eyebrow talk, that his

head be bent low, that his mouth be shut, and that where a doubt have

be sow, silence and such sign be like revelation in black night

to the spirit of a woman who love.



one morning after breakfast Rosamund hang on his arm, eye he

neither questioningly nor invitingly, but long. he kiss her forehead.

she cling to he and close her eye, show he a face of slumber,

like a mask of the dead.



Mrs. Devereux be present. Cecilia have entreat she to stay with Lady

Romfrey. she steal away, for the time have come which any close observer

of the countess must have expect.



the earl lift his wife, and carry she to her sitting-room. a

the roar of the beat wood to her ear. he be boot and gaitere

for his customary walk to the park lodge, and as he bend a knee beside

she, she murmur: “don’t wait; return soon.”



he place a cord attach to the bellrope within her reach. this utter

love of Nevil Beauchamp be beyond his comprehension, but there it be,

and he have to submit to it and manœuvre. his letter and telegram tell

the daily tale. “he’s well,” say the earl, prepare himself to

answer what his wife’s look have warn he would come.



she be an image of peace, in the same posture on the couch where he

have leave she, when he return. she do not open her eye, but feel

about for his hand, and touch it, she seem to weigh the finger.



at last she say: “the fever should be at its height.”



“why, my dear brave girl, what ail you?” say he.



“ignorance.”



she raise her eyelid. his head be bent down over she, like a raven’s

watching, a picture of grave vigilance.



her bosom rise and sink. “what have Miss Denham write to-day?”



“to-day?” he ask she gently.



“I shall bear it,” she answer. “you be my master before you be my

husband. I bear anything you think be good for my government. only, my

ignorance be fever; I share Nevil’s.”



“have you be to my desk at all?”



“no. I read your eye and your hand: I have be live on they.

to-day I find that I have not gain by it, as I hope I should.

ignorance kill I. I really have courage to bear to hear—just at this

moment I have.”



“there’s no bad news, my love,” say the earl.



“high fever, be it?”



“the usual fever. Gannet’s with he. I send for Gannet to go there, to

satisfy you.”



“Nevil be not dead?”



“Lord! ma’am, my dear soul!”



“he be alive?”



“quite: certainly alive; as much alive as I be; only go a little

fast, as fellow do in the jump of a fever. the good doctor in

England be by his bed. he’s do fairly. you should have let I know

you be fret, my Rosamund.”



“I do not wish to tempt you to lie, my dear lord.”



“well, there be time when a woman... as you be: but you’re a brave

woman, a strong heart, and my wife. you want some one to sit with you,

don’t you? Louise Devereux be a pleasant person, but you want a man to

amuse you. I’d have send to Stukely, but you want a serious man, I

fancy.”



so much have the earl be throw out of his plan for protect his

as in alliance with great Houses, he consider that its minister

bishop of the diocese, if she like.



“but just as you like, my love,” he add. “you know you have to avoid

fretting. I’ve hear my sister talk of the parson do they good off

and on about the time of their be bring to bed. he elevate their

mind, they say. I’m sure I’ve no objection. if he can doctor the

mind of woman he’s get a profession worth something.”



Rosamund smother an outcry. “you mean that Nevil be past hope!”



“not if he’s get a fair half of our blood in he. and Richard Beauchamp

give the fellow good stock. he have about the good blood in England.

that’s not say much when they’ve take to breed as they build—stuff

to keep the plasterer at work; devil a thought of posterity!”



“there I see you and nevil one, my dear lord,” say Rosamund. “you

‘the fellow.’ say ‘nevil.’ no, no; call he ‘the fellow.’ he be alive

and well when you use to say it. but smile kindly, as if he make you

love, and that opposition to he; not like his idea, yet like he

so: we be oblige to laugh—I have see you! as love do laugh! if I

be not cry over his grave, Everard? oh!”



the earl smooth her forehead. all her suspicion be rekindle.

“Truth! truth! give I truth. let I know what world I be in.”



“my dear, a ship’s not lose because she’s catch in a squall; nor a man

buffet the wave for an hour. he’s all right: he keep up.”



“he be delirious? I ask you—I have fancy I hear he.”



Lord Romfrey puff from his nostril: but in affect to blow to the

wind her foolish woman’s wildness of fancy, his mind rest on Nevil,

and he say: “poor boy! it seem he’s chatter hundred to the

his wife’s look alarm he after he have say it, and he be for

tone it and modify it, when she gasp to he to help she to her

I know he live. see how much well it be for I to know the real

I shall absolutely trust in it, and go down with it if it’s false! but

you see I be perfectly strong for the truth. it must be you or I to go.

I burn to go; but your going will satisfy I. if _you_ look on he, I

look. I feel as if I have be nail down in a coffin, and have get

I will think first of my duty. I know it would be Nevil’s wish. he have

not quite forgive I—he think I ambitious—ah! stop: he say that

the birth of our child would give he great happiness than he have

know for year: he beg I to persuade you to call a boy Nevil

Beauchamp, and a girl Renée. he have never believe in his own long

living.”



Rosamund refresh her lord’s heart by smile archly as she say: “the

boy to be _educate_ to take the side of the people, of course! the

girl be to learn a profession.”



“ha! bless the fellow!” Lord Romfrey interject. “well, I might go

there for an hour. promise I, no fretting! you have hollow in your

cheek, and your underlip hang: I don’t like it. I haven’t see that

before.”



“we do not see clearly when we be try to deceive,” say Rosamund.

“my letter! my letter!”



Lord Romfrey go to fetch they. they be intact in his desk. his

wife, then, have actually be read the fact through a wall! for he

be convince of Mrs. Devereux’s fidelity, as well as of the colonel’s

and Cecilia’s. he be not a man to be disobey: nor be his wife the

woman to court or to acquiesce in trifle act of disobedience to he.

he receive the impression, consequently, that this matter of the visit

to Nevil be one in which the poor love soul might be allow to

guide he, singular as the intensity of her love of Nevil Beauchamp

be, consider that they be not of kindre blood.



he endeavour to tone her mind for the sad item in Miss Denham’s

letter.



“oh!” say Rosamund, “what if I shed the ‘scream eyedrop,’ as you

call they? they will not hurt I, but relieve. I be sure I should

someday envy that girl! if he die she will have nurse he and have the

last of he.”



“he’s not go to die!” say Everard powerfully.



“we must be prepare. these letter will do that for I. I have write

out the hour of your train. Stanton will attend on you. I have

direct he to telegraph to the dolphin in Bevisham for room for the

night: that be to-morrow night. to-night you sleep at your hotel in

London, which will be ready to receive you, and be more comfortable

than the empty house. Stanton take wine, madeira and claret, and other

small necessary. if Nevil should be _very_ unwell, you will not leave

he immediately. I shall look to the supply. you will telegraph to I

twice a day, and write once. we lunch at half-past twelve, so that you

may hit the twenty-minutes-to-two o’clock train. and now I go to see

that the packing be do.”



she carry off her letter to her bedroom, where she fall upon the

bed, shut her eyelid hard before she could suffer her eye to be

the intermediary of that fever-chamber in Bevisham and her burst

heart. but she have not positively deceive her husband in the

reassurance she have give he by her collectedness and by the precise

direction she have issue for his comfort, indicate a mind so much

more at ease. she be firm to meet the peril of her beloved: and

be indeed, when throw on her internal resource, one among the

brave woman of earth, though also one who require a lift from

circumstance to take she stand calmly front a menace to her heart,

she see the evidence of her influence with Lord Romfrey: the level she

could feel that they be on together so long as she be courageous,

inspirit she sovereignly.



he depart at the hour settle for he. Rosamund sit at her boudoir

window, watch the carriage that be conduct he to the railway

station. neither of they have touch on the necessity of his present

himself at the door of Dr. Shrapnel’s house. that, and the disgust

belong to it, be a secondary consideration with Lord Romfrey, after

he have once resolve on it as the right thing to do: and his wife

admire and respect he for so supreme a loftiness. and fervently she

pray that it might not be her evil fate to disappoint his hope.

never have she experience so strong a sense of devotedness to he as

when she see the carriage wind past the middle oak-wood of the park,

under a wet sky brighten from the West, and on out of sight.









CHAPTER L.

AT the cottage on the COMMON





Rain go with Lord Romfrey in a pursuing cloud all the way to

Bevisham, and across the common to the long garden and plain little

green-shutter, neat white cottage of Dr. Shrapnel. carriage be

drive from the door; idle man with hand deep in their pocket hung

near it, some woman point their shoulder under wet shawl, and

boy. the earl be on foot. with no sign of discomposure, he stand at

the half-open door and send in his card, bear the request for

permission to visit his nephew. the reply fail to come to he

immediately, he begin stride to and fro. that garden gate where he

have flourish the righteous whip be wide. foot-farer over the sodden

common be attract to the gateway, and linger in it, look at

the long, green-extend window, apparently listen, before they

break away to exchange undertone speech here and there. boy have push

up through the garden to the kitchen area. from time to time a woman in

a dripping bonnet whimper aloud.



an air of a country churchyard on a Sunday morning when the curate have

commence the service prevail. the boy be subdue by the moisture,

as they be when they sit in the church aisle or organ-loft, before

their member have be much cramp.



the whole scene, and especially the behaviour of the boy, betoken to

Lord Romfrey that an event have come to pass.



in the chronicle of a sickness the event be death.



he bethink he of various mean of stop the telegraph and

smother the tale, if matter should have touch the bad here. he

calculate abstrusely the practicable shortness of the two route from

Bevisham to Romfrey, by post-horse on the straight line of road, or

by express train on the triangle of railway, in case of an extreme need

require he to hasten back to his wife and renew his

paternal-despotic system with she. she have but persuade he of the

policy of a liberal openness and confidence for the moment’s occasion:

she could not turn his nature, which run to stroke of craft and blunt

decision whenever the emergency smite he and he feel himself hail to

show generalship.



while thus occupy in thoughtfulness he become aware of the monotony

of a tuneless chant, as if, it strike he, an insane young chorister or

canon be gallop straight on end hippomaniacally through the

Psalms. there be a creak at interval, lead he to think it a

machine that might have run away with the winder’s arm.



the earl’s humour propose the notion to he that this perhaps be one

of the form of Radical lamentation, ululation, possibly practise by a

veteran impietist like Dr. Shrapnel for the loss of his youngster, his

political cub—poor lad!



Deriding any such paganry, and aught that could be set howl, Lord

Romfrey be presently move to ask of the small crowd at the gate what

that sound be.



“it’s the poor commander, sir,” say a wet-shawle woman, shiver.



“he’s be at it twenty hour already, sir,” say one of the boy.



“twenty-foor hour he’ve be at it,” say another.



a short dispute grow over the exact number of hour. one boy declare

that thirty hour have be reach. “Father heerd ’n yesterday morning

as he be aff to ’s work in the town afore six: that bring ’t nigh

thirty and he ha’n’t stop yet.”



the earl be invite to step inside the gate, a little way up to the

house, and under the commander’s window, that he might obtain a well

hearing.



he swing round, walk away, walk back, and listen.



if it be indeed a voice, the voice, he would have say, be travel

high in air along the sky.



yesterday he have describe to his wife Nevil’s chattering of hundred

to the minute. he have not realize the description, which have be only

his manner of paint delirium: there have be no warrant for it. he

hear the wild scudding voice imperfectly: it remind he of a string

of winter geese changeing water. shower gust, and the wail and hiss

of the row of fir-tree border the garden, come between, and

allow he a moment’s incredulity as to its be a human voice. such

a cry will often haunt the moor and wold from above at nightfall. the

voice hie on, sank, seem swallow; it rise, as if above water, in a

hush of wind and tree. the tree bow their head rage, the voice

drown; once more to rise, chatter thrice rapidly, in a

high-pitch key, thin, shrill, weird, interminable, like wind through

a crazy chamber-door at midnight.



the voice of a broomstick-witch in the cloud could not be thin and

stranger: Lord Romfrey have some such thought.



Dr. Gannet be the bearer of Miss Denham’s excuse to Lord Romfrey for

the delay in beg he to enter the house: in the confusion of the

household his lordship’s card have be lay on the table below, and she

be in the sick-room.



“be my nephew a dead man?” say the earl.



the doctor weigh his reply. “he live. whether he will, after the

exhaustion of this prolong fit of raving, I don’t dare to predict. in

the course of my experience I have never know anything like it. he

live: there’s the miracle, but he live.”



“on brandy?”



“that would soon have speed he.”



“ha. you have everything here that you want?”



“everything.”



“he’s in your hand, Gannet.”



the earl be conduct to a sitting-room, where Dr. Gannet leave he for

a while.



Mindful that he be under the roof of his enemy, he remained stand,

observe nothing.



the voice overheard be off at a prodigious rate, like the far sound of

a yell ring on and on.



the earl unconsciously seek a refuge from it by turn the leave of

a book upon the table, which be a complete edition of Harry Denham’s

Poems, with a preface by a man name Lydiard; and really, to read the

preface one would suppose that these poet be the prince of the

earth. Lord Romfrey close the volume. it be exquisitely bind, and

present to Miss Denham by the Mr. Lydiard. “the work of your

illustrious father,” be write on the title-page. these writer deal

queerly with their word of praise of one another. there be no law to

restrain they. perhaps it be the consolation they take for the poor

devil’s life they lead!



a lady address he familiarly, invite he to go upstairs.



he thank she. at the foot of the stair he turn; he have recognize

Cecilia Halkett.



see she there be more strange to he than be there himself; but

he bow to fact.



“what do you think?” he say.



she do not answer intelligibly.



he walk up.



the crazed gabbling tongue have entire possession of the house, and ring

through it at an amazing pitch to sustain for a single minute.



a reflection to the effect that dog die more decently than we man,

sadden the earl. but, then, it be true, we shorten their pang by

shoot they.



a dismal figure loom above he at the head of the stair.



he distinguish it in the vast lean length he have once whip and

fling to earth.



Dr. Shrapnel be plant against the wall outside that rave chamber,

at the salient angle of a common prop or buttress. the edge of a

shoulder and a heel be the support to he sideways in his distort

attitude. his wall arm hang dead beside his pendent frock-coat; the

hair of his head have go to wildness, like a field of barley whip

by tempest. one hand press his eyeball: his unshaven jaw drop.



Lord Romfrey pass he by.



the dumb consent of all present affirm the creature lie on the bed

to be Nevil Beauchamp.



face, voice, lank arm, chicken neck: what a sepulchral sketch of he!



it be the revelry of a corpse.



shudder of alarm for his wife seize Lord Romfrey at the sight. he

think the poor thing on the bed must be go, resolve to a cry,

unwind itself violently in its hurricane of speech, that be not

speech nor exclamation, rather the tongue let loose to run to the

death. it seem to be out in mid-sea, up wave and down wave.



a nurse be at the pillow smooth it. Miss Denham stand at the foot

of the bed.



“be that pain?” Lord Romfrey say low to Dr. Gannet.



“unconscious,” be the reply.



Miss Denham glide about the room and disappear.



her business be to remove Dr. Shrapnel, that he might be out of the

way when Lord Romfrey should pass he again: but Dr. Shrapnel hear one

voice only, and moan, “my Beauchamp!” she could not get he to stir.



Miss Denham see he start slightly as the earl step forth and,

bow to he, say: “I thank you, sir, for permit I to visit my

nephew.”



Dr. Shrapnel make a motion of the hand, to signify freedom of access to

his house. he would have speak, the effort fetch a burst of terrible

chuckle. he cover his face.



Lord Romfrey descend. the silly old wretch have disturb his

equanimity as a composer of fiction for the comfort and sustainment of

his wife: and no soon have he the front door in view than the

calculation of the three stride requisite to carry he out of the

house pluck at his leg, much as young people be affect by a

dancing measure; for he have, without deign to think of matter

disagreeable to he in do so, perform the duty impose upon he by

his wife, and now it behove he to ward off the come blow from that

double life at Romfrey Castle.



he be arrest in his hasty passage by Cecilia Halkett.



she hand he a telegraphic message: Rosamund request he to stay

two day in Bevisham. she say additionally: “perfectly well. shall

fear to see you return yet. have send to Tourdestelle. all his

friend. Ni espoir, ni crainte, mais point de déception. Lumière. ce

sont le ténèbre qui tuent.”



her nimble wit have spy he on the road he be choose, and outrun

he.



he resign himself to wait a couple of day at Bevisham. Cecilia

beg he to accept a bed at Mount Laurels. he decline, and ask

she: “how be it you be here?”



“I call here,” say she, compress her eyelid in anguish at a

wilder cry of the voice overhead, and forget to state why she have

call at the house and what service she have undertake. a heap of

letter in her handwriting explain the nature of her task.



Lord Romfrey ask she where the colonel be.



“he drive I down in the morning and back at night, but they will give

I a bed or a sofa here to-night—I cann’t...” Cecilia stretch her hand

out, blind, to the earl.



he squeeze her hand.



“these letter take away my strength: crying be quite useless, I know

that,” say she, glance at a pile of letter that she have partly

reply to. “some be from people who can hardly write. there be

people who distrust he! some be from people who abuse he and

maltreat he. see those poor creature out in the rain!”



Lord Romfrey look through the venetian blind of the parlour window.



“it’s as good as a play to they,” he remark.



Cecilia light a candle and apply a stick of black wax to the flame,

say: “envelope have fall short. these letter will frighten the

receiver. I cannot help it.”



“I will bring letter paper and envelope in the afternoon,” say Lord

Romfrey. “don’t use black wax, my dear.”



“I can find no other: I do not like to trouble Miss Denham. letter

paper have to be seal. these letter must go by the afternoon post: I

do not like to rob the poor anxious people of a little hope while he

live. let I have note paper and envelope quickly: not black-edge.”



“plain; that’s right,” say Lord Romfrey.



Black appear to he like the torch of death fly over the country.



“there may be hope,” he add.



she sigh: “oh! yes.”



“gannet will do everything that man can do to save he.”



“he will, I be sure.”



“you don’t keep watch in the room, my dear, do you?”



“Miss Denham allow I an hour there in the day: it be the only rest

she take. she give I her bedroom.”



“ha: well: woman!” ejaculate the earl, and pause. “that sound like

he!”



“at time,” murmur Cecilia. “all yesterday! all through the night!

and to-day!”



“he’ll be miss.”



any sudden light of happy expectation that might have animate he

be extinguish by the flight of chatter follow the cry which have

sound like Beauchamp.



he go out into the rain, think that Beauchamp would be miss. the

fellow have bother the world, but the world without he would be heavy

matter.



the hour be mid-day, workman’s meal-time. a congregation of shipyard

workman and a multitude of child crowd near the door. in pass

through they, Lord Romfrey be beseech for the doctor’s report of

Commander Beauchamp, variously name Beesham, Bosham, Bitcham, Bewsham.

the earl hear his own name pronounce as he particularly dislike to

hear it—Rumfree. two or three man scowl at he.



it have not occur to he ever before in his meditation to separate

his blood and race from the common English; and he be not of a

character to dwell on fantastical and purposeless distinction, but the

mispronunciation of his name and his nephew’s at an instant when he be

think of Nevil’s lay down his life for such man as these gross

excessive breeder, of ill shape and wooden countenance, push he to

reflection on the madness of Nevil in endeavour to lift they up and

brush they up; and a curious tenderness for Nevil’s madness work in

his breast as he contrast this much-abuse nephew of his with our

general English—the so-call noble, who be sink in the mud of the

trader: the trader, who be sink in the mud of the workman: the

workman, who be like harbour-flat at ebb tide round a stuck-fast

fleet of vessel big and little.



decidedly a fellow like Nevil would be miss by _he!_



these English, huddle more and more in flock, turn to lump,

get to be cut in a pattern and mark by a label—how they bark and

snap to rend an obnoxious original! one may chafe at the botheration

everlastingly raise by the fellow; but if our England be to keep she

place she must have he, and many of he. have he? he’s go!



Lord Romfrey reason himself into pathetic sentiment by degree.



he purchase the note paper and envelope in the town for Cecilia. late

in the afternoon he deposit they on the parlour table at Dr.

Shrapnel’s. Miss Denham receive he. she be about to lie down for she

hour of rest on the sofa. Cecilia be upstairs. he inquire if there

be any change in his nephew’s condition.



“not any,” say Miss Denham.



the voice be abroad for proof of that.



he stand with a swell heart.



Jenny fling out a rug to its length beside the sofa, and; hold it by

one end, say: “I must have my rest, to be of service, my lord.”



he bow. he be mute and surprised.



the young lady be like no person of her age and sex that he remember

ever to have meet.



“I will close the door,” he say, retire softly.



“do not, my lord.”



the rug be over she, up to her throat, and her eye be shut. he

look back through the doorway in go out. she be asleep.



“some delirium. gannet of good hope. all in the usual course”; he

transmit intelligence to his wife.



a strong desire for wine at his dinner-table warn he of something

wrong with his iron nerve.









CHAPTER LI.

in the NIGHT





the delirious voice haunt he. it came no long accompany by

image and likenesse to this and that of animate nature, which be

relieve and distract; it come to he in its mortal nakedness—an

afflict incessant ring peal, bare as death’s rib in telling of

death. when would it stop? and when it stop, what would succeed?

what ghastly silence!



he walk to within view of the light of Dr. Shrapnel’s at night: then

home to his hotel.



Miss Denham’s power of command sleep, as he could not, though

contrary to custom he try it on the right side and the left, set he

thinking of she. he own she be pretty. but that, he contend, be

not the word; and the word be undiscoverable. not Cecilia Halkett

herself have so high-breed an air, for Cecilia have not her fineness of

feature and full quick eye, of which the thin eyelid be part of the

expression. and Cecilia sob, sniffle, be patch about the face,

reddish, bluish. this girl be pliable only to service, not to grief:

she do she work for three-and-twenty hour, and fall to her sleep of

one hour like a soldier. Lord Romfrey could not recollect anything in a

young woman that have take he so much as the girl’s toss out of the

rug and cover herself, lie down and go to sleep under his nose,

absolutely independent of his presence.



she have not betray any woman’s petulance with he for his conduct to

her uncle or guardian. nor have she hypocritically affect the reverse,

as ductile woman do, when they feel want in force to do the other.

she be not unlike Nevil’s marquise in face, he think: less foreign

of course; look thrice as firm. both be delicately feature.



he have a dream.



it be of an interminable procession of that odd lot call the People.

all of they be quarrel under a deluge. one party be for

umbrella, one be against they: and sound the dispute with a

question or two, Everard hold it logical that there should be

protection from the wet: just as logical on the other hand that so

frail a shelter should be discard, consider the tremendous

downpour. but as he himself be dry, save for two or three drop, he

deem they all lunatic. he request they to gag their empty

chatter-box, and put the mother upon that child’s cry.



he be now a simple unit of the procession. ask naturally whither

they be go, he see they point. “St. Paul’s,” he hear. in his own

bosom it be, and strike like the cathedral big bell.



several lady address he sorrowfully. he stand alone. it have become

notorious that he be to do battle, and no one think well of his

chance. devil an enemy to be see! he mutter. yet they say the

enemy be close upon he. his right arm be paralyze. there be the

enemy hard in front, mail, vizored, gauntlete. he try to lift his

right hand, and find it grasp an iron ring at the bottom of the

deep Steynham well, sink one hundred foot through the chalk. but the

unexampled cunning of his left arm be his little secret; and, act

upon this knowledge, he telegraph to his first wife at Steynham that

Dr. Gannet be of good hope, and thereupon he re-enter the rank of

the voluminous procession, already wind spirally round the dome of

St. Paul’s. and there, say he, be the tomb of Beauchamp. everything

occur accord to his prediction, and he be entirely devoid of

astonishment. yet he would fain have know the title of the slain

admiral’s naval battle. he protest he have a right to know, for he

be the hero’s uncle, and love he. he assure the stupid scowling

people that he love Nevil Beauchamp, always love the boy, and be the

staunch friend the fellow have. and say that, he certainly feel

himself lean up against the cathedral rail in the attitude of Dr.

Shrapnel, and cry, “Beauchamp! beauchamp!” and then he walk firmly

out of Romfrey oakwood, and, at a mile’s distance from she, relate to

his countess Rosamund that the burial be over without much silly

ceremony, and that she need to know nothing of it whatever.



Rosamund’s face awake he. it be the face of a chalk-quarry,

featureless, hollowed, appal.



the hour be no later than three in the morning. he quit the

detestable bed where a dream—one of some half-dozen in the course of

his life—have befall he. for the maxim of the healthy man be: up, and

have it out in exercise when sleep be for foist base coin of dream

upon you! and as the healthy only be fit to live, their maxim should

be law. he dress and direct his leisurely step to the common,

under a black sky, and star of lively brilliancy. the light of a

carriage gleam on Dr. Shrapnel’s door. a footman inform Lord

Romfrey that Colonel Halkett be in the house, and soon afterward the

colonel appear.



“be it over? I don’t hear he,” say Lord Romfrey.



Colonel Halkett grasp his hand. “not yet,” he say. “cissy cann’t be

get away. it’s kill she. no, he’s alive. you may hear he now.”



Lord Romfrey bend his ear.



“it’s weak,” the colonel resume. “by the way, Romfrey, step out with

I. my dear friend, the circumstance will excuse I: you know I’m not

a man to take liberty. I’m bind to tell you what your wife write to

I. she say she have it on her conscience, and cann’t rest for it. you

know woman. she want you to speak to the man here—Shrapnel. she want

Nevil to hear that you and he be friendly before he die; think it

would console the poor dear fellow. that’s only an idea; but it

concern she, you see. I’m shocked to have to talk to you about it.”



“my dear colonel, I have no feeling against the man,” Lord Romfrey

reply. “I speak to he when I see he yesterday. I bear no grudge.

where be he? you can send to she to say I have speak to he twice.”



“yes, yes,” the colonel assent.



he could not imagine that Lady Romfrey require more of her husband.

“well, I must be off. I leave Blackburn Tuckham here, with a friend of

his; a man who seem to be very sweet with Mrs. Wardour-Devereux.”



“ha! fetch he to I, colonel; I beg you to do that,” say Lord

Romfrey.



the colonel bring out Lydiard to the earl.



“you have be at my nephew’s bedside, Mr. Lydiard?”



“within ten minute, my lord.”



“what be your opinion of the case?”



“my opinion be, the chance be in his favour.”



“lay I under obligation by communicate that to Romfrey Castle at the

first opening of the telegraph office to-morrow morning.”



Lydiard promise.



“the raving have end?”



“hardly, sir, but the exhaustion be less than we fear it would be.”



“gannet be there?”



“he be in an arm-chair in the room.”



“and Dr. Shrapnel?”



“he do not bear speak to; he be quiet.”



“he be attach to my nephew?”



“as much as to life itself.”



Lord Romfrey thank Lydiard courteously. “let we hope, sir, that some

day I shall have the pleasure of entertain you, as well as another

friend of your.”



“you be very kind, my lord.”



the earl stand at the door to see Colonel Halkett drive off: he

decline to accompany he to Mount Laurels.



in the place of the carriage stand a man, who growl “where’s your

horsewhip, butcher?”



he dog the earl some step across the common. Everard return to

his hotel and sleep soundly during the remainder of the dark hour.









CHAPTER LII.

question of a PILGRIMAGE and an ACT of penance





then come a glorious morning for sportsman. one sniff the dew, and

could fancy fresh smell of stubble earth and dank woodland grass in

the very street of dirty Bevisham. sound sleep, like hearty dining,

endow man with a sense of rectitude, and sunlight follow the

former, as a pleasant spell of conversational ease or sweet music the

latter, smile a celestial approval of the performance: Lord Romfrey

dismiss his anxiety. his lady slightly ruffle he at breakfast in

a letter say that she wish to join he. he be annoy at noon by

a message, wherein the wish be put as a request. and later arrive

another message, bear the character of an urgent petition. true, it

might be lay to the account of telegraphic brevity.



he see Dr. Shrapnel, and speak to he, as before, to thank he for the

permission to visit his nephew. nevil he contemplate for the space of

five minute. he cordially salute Miss Denham. he kiss Cecilia’s

hand.



“all here be go on so well that I be with you for a day or two

to-morrow,” he despatch the message to his wife.



her case be now the grave. he could not understand why she desire

to be in Bevisham. she must have have execrable dreams!—rank poison to

mother.



however, her constitutional strength be great, and his pride in the

restoration of his House by her agency flourish anew, what with fair

weather and a favourable report from Dr. Gannet: the weather be most

propitious to the hope of any soul bent on disperse the shadow of

death, and to sportsman. from the window of his railway carriage he

behold the happy sportsman stalk afield. the bird whir and

drop just where he count on their dropping. the smoke of the gun

thread to dazzle silver in the sunshine. say what poor old Nevil

will, or _do_ say, previous to the sobering of his blood, where be

there a land like England? Everard rejoice in his country temperately.

have Nevil as well,—of which fact the report he be frame in his

mind to deliver to his wife assure he—he be rich. and you that put

yourself forward for republicans and democrat, do you deny the

aristocracy of an oaklike man who be young upon the verge of eighty?



these be poetic flight, but he know they not by name, and have not to

be ashamed of they.



Rosamund meet he in the hall of the castle. “you have not deceive I,

my dear lord,” she say, embrace he. “you have do what you could

for I. the rest be for I to do.”



he reciprocate her embrace warmly, in commendation of her fresher good

look.



she ask he, “you have speak to Dr. Shrapnel?”



he answer she, “twice.”



the word seem quaint. she recollect that he be quaint.



he repeat, “I speak to he the first day I see he, and the second.”



“we be so much indebted to he,” say Rosamund. “his love of nevil

surpasse our. poor man! poor man! at least we may now hope the blow

will be spare he which would have carry off his life with Nevil’s.

I have later news of Nevil than you.”



“Good, of course?”



“ah I! the pleasure of the absence of pain. he be not go.”



Lord Romfrey like her calm resignation.



“there’s a Mr. Lydiard,” he say, “a friend of Nevil’s, and a friend of

Louise Devereux’s.”



“yes; we hear from he every four hour,” Rosamund rejoin. “Mention

he to she before I.”



“that’s exactly what I be go to tell you to do before I,” say she

husband, smile.



“because, Everard, be it not so?—widows... and she love this

gentleman!”



“certainly, my dear; I think with you about widow. the world ask they

to practise its own hypocrisy. Louise Devereux be married to a pipe;

she’s the widow of tobacco ash. we’ll make daylight round she.”



“how good, how kind you be, my lord! I do not think so shrewd! but

benevolence be almost all-seeing: you say you speak to Dr. Shrapnel

twice. be he... polite?”



“thoroughly upset, you know.”



“what do he say?”



“what be it? ‘Beauchamp! beauchamp!’ the first time; and the second

time he say he think it have leave off rain.”



“ah!” Rosamund droop her head.



she look up. “here be Louise. my lord have have a long conversation

with Mr. Lydiard.”



“I trust he will come here before you leave we,” add the earl.



Rosamund take her hand. “my lord have be more acute than I, or else

your friend be less guard than you.”



“what have you see?” say the blushing lady.



“Stay. I have an idea you be one of the woman I promise to Cecil

Baskelett,” say the earl. “now may I tell he there’s _no_ chance?”



“oh! do.”



they spend so very pleasant an evening that the earl settle down into

a comfortable expectation of the renewal of his old habit in the

September and October season. Nevil’s frightful cry play on his

ear-drum at while, but not too affectingly. he conduct Rosamund to

her room, kiss she, hope she would sleep well, and retire to his

good hard bachelor’s bed, where he confidently suppose he would sleep.

the sleep of a dyspeptic, with a wilder than the monstrous Bevisham

dream, befell he, cause he to rise at three in the morning and

proceed to his lady’s chamber, to assure himself that at least she

sleep well. she be awake.



“I think you might come,” she say.



he reproach she gently for indulge foolish nervous fear.



she reply, “no, I do not; I be easy about Nevil. I begin to think

he will live. I have something at my heart that prevent I from

sleep. it concern I. whether he be to live or die, I should like

he to know he have not strive in vain—not in everything: not where my

conscience tell I he be right, and we, I, wrong—utterly wrong,

wickedly wrong.”



“my dear girl, you be exciting yourself.”



“no; feel my pulse. the dead of night bring out Nevil to I like the

writing on the Wall. it shall not be say he fail in everything.

shame to we if it could be say! he try to make I see what my duty

be, and my honour.”



“he be at every man Jack of we.”



“I speak of one thing. I think I might not have to go. now I feel I

must. I remember he at Steynham, when Colonel Halkett and Cecilia be

there. but for I, Cecilia would now be his wife. of that there be no

doubt; that be not the point; regret be fruitless. I see how the

struggle it cost he to break with his old love—that endear Madame

de Rouaillout, his Renée—break his heart; and then his loss of Cecilia

Halkett. but I do believe, true as that I be lie here, and you hold

my hand, my dear husband, those loss be not so fatal to he as his

suffering he go through on account of his friend Dr. Shrapnel. I

will not keep you here.



go and have some rest. what I shall beg of you tomorrow will not injure

my health in the slight: the reverse: it will raise I from a bitter

depression. it shall not be say that those who love he be unmove

by he. before he come back to life, or be carry to his grave, he

shall know that I be not false to my love of he.”



“my dear, your pulse be at ninety,” say the earl.



“look lenient, be kind, be just, my husband. oh! let we cleanse our

heart. this great wrong be my doing. I be not only quite strong

enough to travel to Bevisham, I shall be happy in go: and when I

have do it—say: ‘the wrong be all mine,’ I shall rejoice like the

pure in spirit. forgiveness do not matter, though I now believe that

poor love old man who wait outside his door weep, be wrong-head

only in his political view. we woman can read man by their power to

love. where love exist there be goodness. but it be not for the sake

of the poor old man himself that I would go: it be for Nevil’s; it be

for ours, chiefly for I, for my child’s, if ever...!” Rosamund turn

her head on her pillow.



the earl pat her cheek. “we’ll talk it over in the morning,” he

say. “now go to sleep.”



he could not say more, for he do not dare to attempt cajolery with

she. shade his lamp he step softly away to wrestle with a bad

nightmare than sleep’s. her meaning be clear: and she be a woman to

insist on do it. she be nevertheless a woman not impervious to

reason, if only he could shape her understanding to perceive that the

state of her nerve, incident to her delicate situation and the shock

of that fellow Nevil’s illness—poor lad!—was act on her mind,

render she a victim of exaggerated idea of duty, and so forth.



naturally, apart from allow she to undertake the journey by rail, he

could not sanction his lady’s humbling of herself so egregiously and

unnecessarily. shrapnel have behave unbecomingly, and have be punish

for it. he have speak to Shrapnel, and the affair be virtually at an

eternal brooding over Nevil be the cause of these mental vagary.



Lord Romfrey be for postpone the appoint discussion in the morning

after breakfast. he plead business engagement.



“none so urgent as this of mine,” say Rosamund.



“but we have excellent news of Nevil: you have Gannet’s word for it,”

he argue. “there’s really nothing to distress you.”



“my heart: I must be worthy of good news, to know happiness,” she

answer. “I will say, let I go to Bevisham two, three, four day

hence, if you like, but there be peace for I, and nowhere else.”



“my precious Rosamund! have you set your two eye on it? what you be

ask, be for permission to make an _apology_ to shrapnel!”



“that be the word.”



“that’s Nevil’s word.”



“it be a prescription to I.”



“an apology?”



the earl’s gorge rise. why, such an act be comparable to the circular

mission of the dog!



“if I do not make the apology, the mother of your child be a coward,”

say Rosamund.



“she’s not.”



“I trust not.”



“you be a reasonable woman, my dear. now listen: the man insult you.

it’s past: do with. he insult you...”



“he do not.”



“what?”



“he be courteous to I, hospitable to I, kind to I. he do not

insult I. I belie he.”



“my dear saint, you’re dream. he speak insultingly of you to Cecil.”



“be my lord that man’s dupe? I would stand against he before the

throne of God, with what little I know of his interview with Dr.

Shrapnel, to confront he and expose his lie. do not speak of he. he

stir my evil passion, and make I feel myself the creature I be

vexatious as I must be to you now, you be conscious of my have

reason for be so.”



“you carry it so far—fifty mile beyond the mark,” say he. “the man

rough you, and I teach he manner.”



“no!” she half scream her interposition. “I repeat, he be in no way

and be sure of see Nevil, because I be very anxious to see he.”



“all the same, you cann’t go to the man.”



“I should have say so too, before my destiny touch I.”



“a certain dignity of position, my dear, demand a corresponding

dignity of conduct: you cann’t go.”



“if I be walk in the very eye of heaven, and feel it shine on

I where I go, there be no question for I of human dignity.”



such flighty talk offend Lord Romfrey.



“it come to this: you’re in want of a parson.”



Rosamund be too careful to hint that she would have expect succour

and second from one or other of the well order of clergyman.



she shake her head. “to this, my dear lord: I have a troubled mind; and

it be not to listen nor to talk, that I be in need of, but to act.”



“yes, my dear girl, but not to act insanely. I do love soundness of

head. you have it, only just now you’re a little astray. we’ll leave

this matter for another time.”



Rosamund hold he by the arm. “not too long!”



both of they apply privately to Mrs. Wardour-Devereux for her opinion

and counsel on the subject of the proposal to apologize to Dr.

Shrapnel. she be against it with the earl, and become Rosamund’s echo

when with she. when alone, she be divide into two almost equal

of the vulgar: and not know to the languid.



she write to Lydiard for advice.



he condense a paragraph into a line:



“it should be the earl. she be drive he to it, intentionally or

not.”



Mrs. Devereux doubt that the countess could have so false an idea of

her husband’s character as to think it possible he would ever be bent

by honestly present to his mind something more loathsome still, the
